<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677062 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00867: Pre-commit validation

- page_id: `169677062`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677062/Pre-commit+validation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:24.620+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1048761071

1048761074

1048761070

**On this page**

3

1048761072

The pre-commit validation runs just before a committing server project to the Teamwork Cloud. This validation protects against violations that are not allowed according to the preset validation suites. You will receive a notification prior to commit completion if the validation fails, according to setup. The notifications can be either a warning (with an ability to continue the commit) or error messages (to disallow commit). Pre-commit validation does not run validations on auto-commits (e.g. when upgrading profiles, on element move to the used project, update from a local file and other).

##### Required options for pre-commit validation

To enable pre-commit validation, you must set up at least two project options:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1']

or

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1']

##### Customize validation options

Customize validation options in the**Project Options** dialog.

To open the **Validation Options**

1. Open the Project Options dialog in one of the following ways:
  - On the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validation Options .
  - On the main menu, select Options > Project . In the **Project Options** dialog, select General > Validation .
2. Specify the options you need in the Validation area. How to use the **Project Options** dialog >>
3. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Option | Values | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Validate Only Locally Changed Elements | True or False | When set to true, pre-commit validation will only run on the elements that are locally modified or added by the user. |
| SuitesExclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project | True or False | When set to true, it will exclude projects that are used in read-only mode and elements that exist in these projects. |
| Validation Suites | Validation suites | Specify a validation suites list that must be run before committing changes to a server. |
| CommitExcluded scope | Excluded scope | Specify the validation scope to be excluded from the rules that belong to pre-commit validation suites. Elements in the selected scope will not be validated before committing changes to a server. |
| WarningMinimal Severity That Prohibits Commit | None - debug. - info. - warning. - error. - fatal. | Specify the minimal severity violation severity threshold that prohibits commit. Pre-commit validation will be performed before executing commit to a server. It will validate the model and check if there are validation results with reached or exceeded selected severity levels. Commit will be prohibited until these violations are fixed. |
| Minimal Severity That Produces Warning | None - debug. - info. - warning. - error. - fatal. | Specify the minimal severity threshold that produces a warning. Pre-commit validation will be performed before executing commit to a server. It will validate the model and check if there are validation results with reached or exceeded selected severity level. If these violations are found, a warning dialog will appear, allowing you either to review warnings or to execute project commit. |

##### Warning and error dialogs

- warning If a warning appears, you are allowed to choose whether to review violations or to continue to commit. If you choose to review violations, the pre-commit validation results panel will be open; otherwise, your project will be committed without showing violations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- error If there is an error, you are not allowed to choose. You will get a list of the validation results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When the pre-commit validation is supposed to show a warning and an error at the same time, the tool will only show an error due to its behavior being more restricted.

##### Pre-commit Validation Results panel

The **Pre-commit****Validation Results** panel opens after you either get an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1'] or you select to review violations in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1'].

It is impossible to ignore Pre-Commit Validation results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Toolbar

The toolbar allows you to manage the validation results. All buttons are described in the following table

| Button icon | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand All | Click to recursively expand all the elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Collapse All | Click to recursively collapse all the elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Navigate to Validated Object (Alt+B) | Click the button to select the element in the .Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following navigation commands:Open Specification - opens the of the selected element in the validation results area.Go To - opens a submenu whose commands allow you to track and navigate to the elements. Select Rule in The Containment Tree - selects the failed validation rule in the of the selected element in the validation results area.Add to Search Results - selects an element in the . |
|  | Solve | Click to open the list of available commands to solve a selected element violation in the validation results area. |
|  | Display the full path of the elements | Click to show the full path of all elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Run validation with current settings | Click to run the validation with the current settings. |
|  | Validation Options | Click to open the Project Options dialog with the Validation node open. |
|  | Export | Click to export the list of validation results either to an .html or .csv file. |

###### Validation results area

The validation results area provides a list of violated elements. This area contains the following columns:

- Element - elements that violate the validation rule.
- Severity - severity level of the validation rule.
- Abbreviation - a short word, phrase, or acronym of the validation rule used for sorting/filtering.
- Message - description of the invalid situation when a validation rule fails.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="058250b4-0583-4b84-b971-4bea234a8b57"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048761071</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26eecb1e-ad36-4786-9f7f-6cc102d06a06"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048761074</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09ede1ea-0001-435f-b702-3e0cde8f092b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048761070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd80506a-d206-4829-830d-429d759a1d38"><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3e50f08-9f66-4173-8210-b76c020643d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048761072</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The pre-commit validation runs just before a committing server project to the Teamwork Cloud. This validation protects against violations that are not allowed according to the preset validation suites. You will receive a n</span>otification prior to commit completion if the validation fails, according to setup. The notifications can be either a warning (with an ability to continue the commit) or error messages (to disallow commit). Pre-commit validation does not run validations on auto-commits <span>(e.g. when upgrading profiles, on element move to the used project, update from a local file and other)</span>.</p><h3>Required options for pre-commit validation</h3><p>To enable pre-commit validation, you must set up at least two project options:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Suites"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation Suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Commit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Minimal Severity That Prohibits Commit]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>or</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Suites"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation Suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Warning"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Minimal Severity That Produces Warning]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Customize validation options</h3><p>Customize validation options in the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Validation Options</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:<br /><ul><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation Options</strong>.</li><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Setting+project+options" rel="nofollow"><strong>Project Options</strong></a> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the options you need in the <strong>Validation</strong> area. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Setting+project+options" rel="nofollow">How to use the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog &gt;&gt;</a></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Precommit_validation_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 91.3939%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.7233%;" /><col style="width: 16.7814%;" /><col style="width: 59.3988%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option</th><th>Values</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Validate Only Locally Changed Elements</td><td>True or False</td><td>When set to Pre-commit validation will be performed before executing commit to a server. It will validate the model and check if there are validation results with reached or exceeded selected severity level. If these violations are found, a warning dialog will appear, allowing you either to review warnings or to execute project commit.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><br />Warning and error dialogs</h3><ul><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6c9c2f0-0397-4f3b-8423-bc24c2ad290f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">warning</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>If a warning appears, you are allowed to choose whether to review violations or to continue to commit. All buttons are described in the following table</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button icon</th><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Expand All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Expand All" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment the validation results area.</li><li><strong><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="﻿go_to.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Go To</strong> - opens a submenu whose commands allow you to track and navigate to the elements.</li><li><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_rule_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Select Rule in The Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the selected element in the validation results area.</li><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_to_search_results.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Add to Search Results</strong> - selects an element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Search Results tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Search Results tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<strong> </strong><br /></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Solve" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Solve" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="solve.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Solve</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the list of available commands to solve a selected element violation in the validation results area.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Display the full path of the elements" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Display the full path of the elements</p></td><td>Click to show the full path of all elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Run validation with current settings" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Run validation with current settings" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_validation_with_current_settings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Run validation with current settings</p></td><td>Click to run the validation with the current settings.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Run validation with new settings" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Run validation with new settings" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_validation_with_new_settings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f71570e6-b6f8-4c84-b080-eed1d3e3c07c"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Validation Options</span><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td><td><p>Click to open the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Project Options</strong></span> dialog<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> with the Validation node open. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Export" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Export" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="export.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Export</td><td><p>Click to export the list of validation results either to an .html or .csv file.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4><br />Validation results area</h4><p>The validation results area provides a list of violated elements. This area contains the following columns:</p><ul><li><strong>Element</strong> - elements that violate the validation rule.</li><li><strong>Severity</strong> - severity level of the validation rule.</li><li><strong>Abbreviation</strong> - a short word, phrase, or acronym of the validation rule used for sorting/filtering.</li><li><strong>Message</strong> - description of the invalid situation when a validation rule fails.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677573 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00868: Pre-merge check

- page_id: `169677573`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677573/Pre-merge+check
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-22T12:00:18.747+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Model merge in Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the optional pre-merge check functionality to check permissions and prepare for branch merging.

How to perform the pre-merge check

1. Open the project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. Select Tools > Project Merge.
3. Select the project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2022xR1'] .
4. Click the Pre-merge check refresh icon: [ATTACHMENT filename='per_merge_check.png']

This checks if you can execute this merge, i.e., if you have sufficient permissions and a valid model structure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Use the optional pre-merge check functionality to check permissions and prepare for branch merging.</p><p style="text-align: left;">How to perform the pre-merge check</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project that will be the <ac:link ac:anchor="Target"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Merge" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[target]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.</span></li><li>Select <strong>Tools &gt; Project Merge.</strong></li><li>Select the project that will be the <ac:link ac:anchor="Source"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Model Merge" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[source]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link">.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Pre-merge check</strong> refresh icon:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="per_merge_check.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pre-merge check" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: left;">This checks if you can execute this merge, i.e., if you have sufficient permissions and a valid model structure.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676368 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00869: Predefined traceability rules

- page_id: `169676368`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676368/Predefined+traceability+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:21.192+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1965944762

1965944764

1965944763

Besides allowing you to create your own traceability rules, our modeling tool allows you to use a set of predefined element properties for traceability purposes. The traceability solution also includes a report template used for coverage analysis. You can customize this template or create your own templates for producing your own traceability analysis reports.

The main objective of the predefined traceability rules is to allow the use of the traceability engine instantly (out- of-the-box) for a typical project. The predefined rules allow for analyzing and verifying links between elements residing in Analysis, Design, and Implementation layers of your models. Elements in these layers are extended with a set of properties, pointing to elements from the higher abstraction layer (backward traceability) and the lower abstraction layer (forward traceability).

Overall, there are three groups of predefined traceability properties, which will be detailed in subsequent sections:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forward traceability - realization' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Backward traceability – specification' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Other traceability relations' space='MD2024xR1']

1965944761

**Related pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Custom traceability rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676915 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00870: Predefined validation suites

- page_id: `169676915`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676915/Predefined+validation+suites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:22.857+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

499947510

499947512

499947509

**On this page**

43

499947511

##### Introduction

You can validate your model against a set of constraints called validation suites. You must perform the validation manually to check your model against the predefined validation suites. The predefined validation suites have the «validationSuite» stereotype applied.

Each Profile contains its own validation suites. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='MD2024xR1'].

The following predefined validation suites appear in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation** dialog or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Suites** dialog:

- Composition Integrity contains validation rules that check the structure of the project, such as recovered elements, references, versions of project usages, etc.
- Project Integrity Correctness contains validation rules that check cyclical dependencies between current project and used projects.
- Spelling contains validation rules that check the spelling of properties and tagged values.
- Text References Validation contains validation rules that check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.
- UML completeness constraints contains validation rules that check if the model is complete, that there are no gaps, and the essential information fields in the elements have been filled in (e.g. checks that all the properties have type specified etc.).
- UML correctness constraints contains validation rules that check if the model is correct, that related information is consistent across the model (e.g. the multiplicity between elements coincides between their instances).

You can find predefined validation suites in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1'] when the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option is enabled, expand the *UML Standard Profile* Package> *Validation Profile* Package.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MD2024xR1']true

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='MD2024xR1']Learn how to start the validation >>

##### Limiting the scope of the constraints to be validated against

If you select the **All Validation Suites** as the **Validation Suite** in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation** dialog, your model is validated against all validation suites. To limit the scope of the constraints to be validated against, you can select a more specific validation suite, such as **Numbering Validation**. The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Results** panel shows the results, listing all elements that do not conform to constraints in the selected validation suite. These elements are called “invalid” elements and are highlighted.

INLINE

##### Customizing validation options

You can customize the Validation options in the**Project Options** dialog.

To open the Validation options

1. Open the Project Options dialog in one of the following ways:
  - On the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validation Options .
  - On the main menu, select Options > Project . In the **Project Options** dialog, select General > Validation .
2. Specify the options you need in the Validation area. How to use the **Project Options** dialog >>
3. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Validation options in the **Project Options** dialog.

227108230

**Related pages**

- Active validation suites
- SysML validation suites
- [SysML active validation suites](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR1/SysML+active+validation+suites)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirements validation suites' space='CRMP2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676010 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00871: Preparing for merge

- page_id: `169676010`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676010/Preparing+for+merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-23T12:26:24.422+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the model merge procedure by changing **Merge and Compare**options in the **Environment Options**dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing Merge and Compare options in the Environment Options dialog.

You can:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change types' space='MD2024xR1']. This means you can skip the order changes of specified properties while merging.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change types' space='MD2024xR1'] . Instead of analyzing the changes in all diagrams in the model, you can limit detection to diagrams that were loaded (open) when the changes were made.
- Choose whether to Detect Changes in Diagrams from Used Projects in Teamwork Cloud . Setting this option to False may improve performance if a project has a lot of differences in diagrams from used projects.

#### NOTE: For server projects

For server projects

- Partial locking of a project occurs when merging changes of a project in the Teamwork Cloud server. Only changed/updated and merging elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. Thus, actions with other server project elements are not interrupted for other users by locking and merging activities.
- While a user performs the merge process in a server project on the Teamwork Cloud server, other users are not able to commit project changes to the server.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding merge types' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting Model Merge' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finishing and canceling merge' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can customize the <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">model merge procedure by changing <strong>Merge and Compare </strong>options in the <strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog.</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_and_compare_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Preparing for merge" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Viewing Merge and Compare options in the Environment Options dialog.</span></h6><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can:</span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link ac:anchor="Order change"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Turn off order changes detection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">This means you can skip the order changes of specified properties while merging.</span></span></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Direct and All changes"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Choose to investigate for direct changes only]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Instead of analyzing the changes in all diagrams in the model, you can limit detection to diagrams that were loaded (open) when the changes were made.</li><li>Choose whether to <strong>Detect Changes in Diagrams from Used Projects in Teamwork Cloud</strong>. Setting this option to <em>False </em>may improve performance if a project has a lot of differences in diagrams from used projects.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d801feda-5d24-491b-b06d-560871a92b13"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">For server projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Partial locking of a project occurs when merging changes of a project in the Teamwork Cloud server. Only changed/updated and merging elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. Thus, actions with other server project elements are not interrupted for other users by locking and merging activities.</li><li>While a user performs the merge process in a server project on the Teamwork Cloud server, other users are not able to commit project changes to the server.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro conf-macro output-block"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding merge types" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding change concept" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Starting Model Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing and managing merge results" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Finishing and canceling merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674910 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00872: Presentation Mode

- page_id: `169674910`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674910/Presentation+Mode
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:56.413+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

57060115

INLINE

57060123

INLINE

57079926

INLINE

**On this page**

**33**

57060122

INLINE

Presentation Mode enables users to present a selected diagram or sequence of diagrams in Full Screen mode without showing the status line, diagram palette, Model Browser, toolbars, and main menu. Additionally, it helps prevent unintended changes that may occur during a presentation.

700500

##### Presentation Mode workflow

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content Diagram Wizard' space='MD2024xR1']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Mode' space='MD2024xR1']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Mode' space='MD2024xR1']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Presentation Mode' space='MD2024xR1']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabled Presentation Mode with a defined sequence of diagrams

Creating sequenceTo create a sequence of diagrams

1. Open the created Content diagram with the displayed diagram content.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'] , select Presentation Flow. [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram_palette_presentation_mode.png']
  - Select a diagram shape and then in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024xR1'] , click Presentation Flow. [ATTACHMENT filename='presentation_flow_smart_manipulator.png']
3. Connect the selected diagrams in the sequence you want to present them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can navigate through diagrams that are connected using Presentation Flow only.

Only diagram shapes can be connected in a sequence.

Enter presentation modeTo enter Presentation Mode

Do one of the following:

- In the main menu, click Window > Editor Tabs > Enter Presentation Mode .
- In the bottom right corner of the status line, click the Presentation Mode icon. [ATTACHMENT filename='presentation_mode_from_bar.png']
- In the smart manipulator toolbar of the diagram you want to present, click the Presentation Mode icon. [ATTACHMENT filename='entering_presentation_mode_from_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png']

You can enter Presentation Mode from any diagram in a defined sequence.

Diagrams cannot be opened in new tabs if Presentation Mode is active.

Exit presentation modeTo switch back to the normal mode

Do one of the following:

- Press the Esc button on your keyboard.
- Click X in the diagram tab next to the diagram name. [ATTACHMENT filename='closing_presentation_mode.png']

##### Simulation in Presentation Mode

You can show diagram simulations in Presentation Mode as well. Once the simulation is started, a group of buttons for managing a simulation flow appears at the bottom of the window next to the Presentation Mode control buttons (see the diagram below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To learn more about diagram simulation, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram' space='CST2024xR1'].

1408563896

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram' space='CST2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678118 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00873: Presentation Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `169678118`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678118/Presentation+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:26.825+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Designer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this section, you will learn how to create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree and select Report Designer Diagrams > Presentation Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Presentation Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as needed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements3.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Presentation Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** > **PPTX Template** to generate the template. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] NoteGenerated templates get only symbols on the diagram. Elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate templates.
5. Refine more layouts, style, and code logic in Microsoft PowerPoint.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generating Report from Added Template2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Generating Report from Added Template3.png']

#### Diagram elements

The Presentation Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive that can be created from the toolbar on the diagram. You can see diagram elements description in the [Document Report Designer diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Document+Report+Designer+diagram) page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this section, you will learn how to create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a Package in the Containment tree and select <strong>Report Designer Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Presentation Report Designer Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Presentation Report Designer Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.</li><li>Arrange diagram elements as needed.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Arranging Diagram Elements3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click <strong>Presentation Report Designer Diagram</strong> and select <strong>Generate Template</strong> &gt; <strong>PPTX Template</strong> to generate the template.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PPTX Template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="346b6768-2d5f-48fb-9981-cb53fa835ce4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Generated templates get only symbols on the diagram. Elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate templates.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Refine more layouts, style, and code logic in Microsoft PowerPoint.</li><li>Add the complete template to Report Wizard.</li><li>Generate the report from the added template.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating Report from Added Template2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating Report from Added Template3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h2>Diagram elements</h2><p>The Presentation Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive that can be created from the toolbar on the diagram. You can see diagram elements description in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Document+Report+Designer+diagram">Document Report Designer diagram</a> page.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675082 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00874: Primitive

- page_id: `169675082`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675082/Primitive
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T04:57:21.821+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Data Type
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Primitive defines a predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='MD2024xR1'] without possessing any relevant UML substructure; that is, it has no UML parts. A Primitive data type can have an algebra as well as operations defined outside of UML (for example, mathematically). The Primitive data types used in UML include Integer, Unlimited Integer, Real, and String.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Primitive defines a predefined <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Data Type" /></ac:link> without possessing any relevant UML substructure; that is, it has no UML parts. A Primitive data type can have an algebra as well as operations defined outside of UML (for example, mathematically). The Primitive data types used in UML include Integer, Unlimited Integer, Real, and String.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Data Type" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674899 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00875: Print dialog

- page_id: `169674899`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674899/Print+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-12-01T09:13:56.119+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Printing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the Print dialog, do one of the following

- From the File menu, select Print .
- Press CTRL+P.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To open the Print dialog, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Print</strong>.</li><li>Press CTRL+P.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Print dialog" ac:title="Print dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="print_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Print dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674896 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00876: Printing

- page_id: `169674896`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674896/Printing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:47:19.361+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

272773126

272773137

272773136

You can print:

- Active diagram
- Any diagram(s)
- All open diagrams
- Selected symbols of active diagram

You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:

- Print Dialog
- Print Options Dialog
- Print Preview Window

To print an active diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print.png'] Print Active Diagram button.
  - From the File menu, select Print . On the Print dialog, Print what area, select Active diagram , and click the Print button.

To print any diagram(s)

1. From the File main menu, select Print . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, choose Selected diagram(s) . The Select Diagram dialog opens.
3. Select diagram(s) you want to print.
4. Click OK .
5. Click Print .

To print all open diagrams

1. From the File main menu, select the Print command. The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, select All opened diagram(s) .
3. Click Print .

To print selected symbols of active diagram

1. On the diagram, select symbols you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected symbols' shortcut menu, select Print Selected .
  - From the File menu, select Print . In the Print dialog, Print what area, choose Selected symbols of active diagram , and click the Print button.

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), which affect diagram print/preview, in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024xR1'] page to learn more.

272773125

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675362 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00877: Profile

- page_id: `169675362`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675362/Profile
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:09.509+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

852159285

852159288

852159287

A Profile is a kind of a package that extends a reference metamodel. The primary extension construct is a stereotype. Stereotypes are defined as a part of profiles.

A profile introduces several constraints or restrictions to ordinary metamodeling. Constraints and restrictions are realized using metaclasses defined in the package. It is a restricted form of a metamodel that always must be related to a reference metamodel, such as UML, as it is described below. It cannot be used without its reference metamodel, and it defines a limited capability to extend metaclasses of the reference metamodel. The extensions are defined as stereotypes that apply to the existing metaclasses.

Each profile contains a set of stereotypes. Profiles are defined as separate modules. Profiles are loaded on demand, that is when you start or open your project only profiles used in that project are loaded.

Profiles are defined using the UML extensibility mechanisms that allow modelers to customize UML for specific domains, for example, for software development processes. The mechanism of the profile is similar to the functionality of modules.

The program comes with some predefined profiles: UML Standard Profile, DDL, EDOC, and other. All profiles are stored in *<modeling tool installation directory>\profiles*.

###### Profile Properties

The profile is defined as a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024xR1'], that is, it has package properties.

You can specify profile properties in the profile's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. You can also find descriptions of each [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1'] in this window. Descriptions are provided in the description area below the property list.

###### Creating Profiles

You can create a profile in one of the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MD2024xR1']

ShortcutTo create a profile using the package / model / profile shortcut menu

1. In the Containment tree, select a package, a model, or another profile, wherein you want to create the new profile.
2. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024xR1'] of the selected package, model, or profile, select Create Element > Profile . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_profiles.png']

Profile DiagramTo create a profile using the profile diagram palette

1. Either create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] or open an existing one.
2. On the diagram palette, click the Profile button.
3. Click a free space on the diagram pane to see the created Profile.

Package DiagramTo create a profile using the package diagram palette

1. Either create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] or open an existing one.
2. On the diagram palette, click the Package arrow to see more buttons.
3. Click the Profile button.
4. Click a free space of the diagram pane to see the created Profile.

Class DiagramTo create a profile using the class diagram palette

1. Either create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] or open an existing one.
2. On the Class diagram palette, do one of the following:
  - Expand the Profile Diagram button group and click the Profile button.
  - Expand the Package Diagram button group, click the Package arrow to see more buttons, and then click the Profile button.
3. Click a free space of the diagram pane to see the created Profile.

852159284

**Related pages**

- Model Elements
- Stereotype
- Package
- Working with Profiles

## PAGE 00878: Profile Application

- page_id: `169675365`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675365/Profile+Application
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-24T12:29:57.155+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A profile application is used to show which profiles have been applied to a package.

Specify the Profile Application element, in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A profile application is used to show which profiles have been applied to a package.</span></p><p>Specify the Profile Application element, in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673916 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00879: Profile diagram

- page_id: `169673916`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673916/Profile+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-23T12:07:34.056+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308723076

308723078

308723077

**Overview**

In general, a Profile diagram has been introduced in UML 2.0 to display the usage of profiles. It is an extensibility mechanism that allows extending and customizing UML. To be more specific, the Profiles package contains mechanisms that allow metaclasses from existing metamodels to be extended to adapt them for different purposes.

**Purpose**

A Profile Diagram extends UML by adding new building blocks, creating new properties, and specifying new semantics so that the language would be suitable to the specific domain, for example, medicine, financial services, or engineering.

**Usage**

A Profile diagram extends UML to allow the use of:

- user-defined stereotypes
- meta attributes
- constraints.

**Summary**

Profile diagrams are valuable because they allow for adaptation of the UML metamodel for:

- different platforms (e.g., Java Platform, Microsoft .NET Framework, Enterprise Edition (Java EE), etc.)
- domains (e.g., business process modeling, medical applications, service-oriented architecture)

If the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='MD2024xR1'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Primitive' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1'], Direct Association, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2024xR1'] are not available on the diagram palette, make sure you are working in the Expert mode.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Profile diagram

308723075

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']

diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d975972d-bef2-4dbf-9c1c-57489e111031"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308723075</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678496 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00880: Profile Management dialog

- page_id: `169678496`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678496/Profile+Management+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:27.255+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard > Uploading reports to remote locations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the following items

22

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Profile Management dialog.

#### Profile Management dialog buttons

The Profile Management dialog contains five buttons:

|  | Adds a new profile to the list of existing Profiles. You can neither create a profile with a blank name, nor with a name that already been used. You can add and edit multiple Profiles. However, these profiles will not be saved until you click the Save Profiles button. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Removes the currently selected profile. Note that you cannot remove the "No Upload" profile. |
|  | Saves all profiles. If new profiles have been added or existing Profiles have been modified, then these changes will be saved. All profiles will be checked for consistency or information correctness. You will be asked to fix issues, if any. It is not possible to save profiles as long as invalid information is detected. See Profile Management dialog fields below for the description of "valid profile". |
|  | Abandons all newly-added profiles and all changes made to existing profiles. If you do not make any changes to any profiles, clicking this button will bring you back to the Output Options pane of the Report Wizard dialog. Otherwise, the Confirm Cancel dialog will open. In this dialog, you will see the names of the profiles whose content you have changed. You can choose to either abandon all changes, or return to the Profile Management dialog and save the changes. |
|  | Works similar to the Cancel button. |

#### Profile Management dialog fields

Profiles describe information necessary for sending a report to a remote location. The report will be saved remotely with the same name as the locally-generated report, and will overwrite any file with the same name on the remote location.

The Address field is URL sensitive, and can hold protocol as well as username information. If the protocol or username information is found in the Address field, the corresponding fields, namely the Protocol and Username fields, will be updated with the values from the Address field. At the very least, a profile must have a hostname in the Address field.

You cannot save a profile unless this condition is met. The Username and Password fields contain sensitive data and, therefore, are optional. If these fields are empty in a profile, you will be prompted for your username and password when the server asks for your authentication.

- Profile name : This drop-down list holds all the existing Profiles. They are alphabetically sorted. The "No upload" profile is always available, and you can neither modify, nor remove it. Select the "No Upload" profile if you do not want to upload a report.
- Protocol : Five different protocols are supported: FTP, FTP over SSL (Secure Socket Layer), Webdav, Webdav over SSL, and SSH (Secure Shell) . You can either choose to select a protocol from the protocol drop-down list, or fill in the scheme in the address part of the profile. Certificates that are sent by servers using secure traffic (FTP over SSL, Webdav over SSL, and SSH) are silently accepted. The scheme identifiers and default port numbers of the five supported protocols are listed in the table below.

The table below lists Scheme Identifier and Default Port Numbers of Five Supported Protocols

| Protocol Name | Scheme | Default Port Number | Traffic Mode |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ftp | ftp:// | 21 | Plain text |
| webdav | http:// | 80 | Plain text |
| ftp + ssl | ftps:// | 990 | Encrypted & Secure |
| webdav + ssl | https:// | 443 | Encrypted & Secure |
| ssh | ssh:// or sftp:// | 22 | Encrypted & Secure |

- Address

The Address field is the central part of a profile. It can set other fields, except the Password field. An Address contains five different parts: scheme, username, hostname, port (number), and (remote) path. Except hostname, every part is optional. Depending on the circumstances, you might need to add a special port number and/or the remote path where you have write access. Even though an address is valid without the path part, most servers will require a path, and will not let you write to the root directory. In every case, scheme and username parts will take precedence over the selection in the Protocol Field or the content in the Username field. The following is the basic pattern for the address field:

[scheme://][username@]hostname[:port][path]

1. scheme : The supported schemes are ftp, ftps, http, https, ssh, or sftp. A scheme must be followed by “://”.
2. username : A username is needed for user authentication. It must be followed by “@”.
3. hostname : Must be either an IP address (for example, 10.1.1.195) or a human readable URL (for example, www.ftpserver.com ), and a valid address.
4. port : A port number where the server is listed. It is only necessary if the port number is different from the default port for well-known services. It must be preceded by ":".
5. path : The predefined path will start from the home directory of the specified username. Therefore, the report will be generated at the home directory of the username, e.g., if the address is [ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish](ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish) , and the username is developer , the report will be generated at the home directory of the developer username. The designated path will be /home/developer/mymodel/publish .

Examples of valid addresses:

1. 10.1.1.195 (hostname only)
2. 10.1.1.195/companyDav (hostname and path)
3. 10.1.1.195:80/companyDav (hostname, port number, and path)
4. ftp://www.ftpserver.com (scheme and hostname)
5. john@10.1.1.195 (username and hostname)
6. john@www.ftpserver.com /users/john/reports (username, hostname, and path)
7. ssh://john@10.1.2.35:22/reports (all parts)

- User Name

This field specifies a username that will be used for authentication. If the address contains a username part, the content of the Username field will be ignored. It will be either filled in or overwritten with the username from the Address. A Profile with an empty username field is valid, but you will be prompted for a username and password every time you upload to the server using this profile.

- Password

A profile with a password must always be accompanied by a username. A Profile with a password, but not a username, is invalid and, therefore, cannot be saved. The password field uses an '*' to represent a character in a password. Saving a profile with a password will save the password in an encrypted form. For example, if the password is 'test', it will be encrypted to '0013BCA5590DE'. A Profile with an empty password field is valid, but you will be prompted for a password every time you upload a report to the server in this profile.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676154 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00881: Profile Migration Transformation

- page_id: `169676154`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676154/Profile+Migration+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-02-15T06:44:26.032+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformation provides a way to migrate your model according specified mapping rules.

Profile migration transformation search-and-replaces the following elements:

- Applied stereotypes
- Tagged values
- Usages of one type with another type

Element symbols are updated according to model changes.

Before starting transformation you have to define transformation mapping.

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Transformation provides a way to migrate your model according specified mapping rules.</p><p>Profile migration transformation search-and-replaces the following elements:</p><ul><li>Applied stereotypes</li><li>Tagged values</li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Usages of one type with another type</span></li></ul><p>Element symbols are updated according to model changes.</p><p>Before starting transformation you have to define transformation mapping.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1559d803-b9a2-44d9-ba79-24b69de5050a"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676155 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00882: Profile Migration Transformation mapping

- page_id: `169676155`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676155/Profile+Migration+Transformation+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-11T10:45:05.491+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Profile Migration Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before starting transformation you have to create transformation mapping rules.

To create mapping rules you need to create Dependency relationship between elements you want to transform.

Mapping rules can be created (dependency relationship can be created) between the following elements:

1. Stereotypes
2. Tags
3. Types

###### Creating mapping rules for Stereotype transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace Stereotype.

To create Stereotype transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Stereotypes which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceStereotype stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

Tag values of old stereotype are preserved when tag name and type of tag value is the same. For tag values with different names create mapping rule for tag transformation.

*ReplaceStereotype* stereotype has the following tags:

- disableNewTypeCreation tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want to perform transformation when target and source metaclasses are not compatible. For example, if you do not want that Class would be changed to Use Case.
- disableReplaceWhereSaveAsElementValue tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want that stereotype would be changed where it is used as Tag value (tag value is stereotype itself, for which you perform transformation).

###### Creating mapping rules for Tag transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace Tag (when tag names differs). For example, source stereotype has author Tag and target stereotype has name Tag.

To create Tag transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Tags which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceTaggedValue stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

To create mapping rule correctly, you have to create Dependency relationship not only between Tags, but also between Stereotypes of these tags (see mapping between stereotypes, which is described above).

###### Creating mapping rules for types transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace type. For example, to replace type of Attribute.

To create types transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Types which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceType stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For tag values with different names create mapping rule for tag transformation.</p><p><em>ReplaceStereotype</em> stereotype has the following tags:</p><ul><li><strong>disableNewTypeCreation</strong> tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want to perform transformation when target and source metaclasses are not compatible. For example, if you do not want that Class would be changed to Use Case. </li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">disableReplaceWhereSaveAsElementValue</strong> tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want that stereotype would be changed where it is used as Tag value (tag value is stereotype itself, for which you perform transformation).</li></ul><h4>Creating mapping rules for Tag transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace Tag (when tag names differs). For example, source stereotype has author Tag and target stereotype has name Tag.</p><p> </p><p>To create Tag transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Tags which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceTaggedValue</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e2a42d0-cd17-4429-80d5-f568021f30ef"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create mapping rule correctly, you have to create Dependency relationship not only between Tags, but also between Stereotypes of these tags (see mapping between stereotypes, which is described above).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h4>Creating mapping rules for types transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace type. For example, to replace type of Attribute.</p><p> </p><p>To create types transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Types which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceType</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679656 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00883: Profiling changes

- page_id: `169679656`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679656/Profiling+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-25T09:20:51.688+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML 2.5.1 support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The previously implemented UML profiling metamodelused Instance Specifications, Slots and Value Specificationsto store the tagged values ofappliedstereotypes.Since that approach required two model elements (Slot and Value Specification) to store each tagged value, it has been optimized.

The InstanceSpecifications, Slots, and ValueSpecifications are no longer used to store profiling data.Instead,theElementdirectlyreferences the TaggedValue that is used to specify the Boolean, Integer, Real, String, or Elementtaggedvalues of the applied stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Profiling changes in the UML metamodel.

As a consequence, expressions based on the previously implemented profiling data storing metamodel no longer work; they have to be updated manually.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


## PAGE 00884: Profiling tool

- page_id: `169678211`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678211/Profiling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T13:57:07.996+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2049646004

2049646007

2049646000

**On this page**

43

2049646006

You can use $profiling to access meta-model information.

****

###### **$profiling.getGeneralizationName(modelName)**

Return a generalization model of modelName.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A list of generalization model names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getDeclaringElementName (modelName, propertyName)**

Retrieve the meta-model name which is the declared property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getPropertyTypeName (modelName, propertyName)**

Retrieve property types from meta-model names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A property type name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getPropertyTypeName (element, propertyName)**

Retrieve property types from an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A property type name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperties(modelName)**

Retrieve a collection of element property names from meta-model names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A collection of element property names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperties(element)**

Retrieve a collection of element property names from elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A collection of element property names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperty(element, propertyName)**

Retrieve property values of specified elements and property names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.Object | A property object. |

****

###### **$profiling. getHumanPropertyName(element, propertyName)**

Return text representing a property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
|  | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Text representing a property name. |

## PAGE 00885: Project characteristics

- page_id: `169678586`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678586/Project+characteristics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-24T11:56:47.009+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Use Case driven
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Technical and Environmental factors are associated with the weight of the project. You have to assign a value to each factor. The value assigned to a particular factor depends on the degree of influence a factor has. The relevance values range from 0 to 5, where 0 means no influence, 3 is the average level of influence, and 5 means a strong influence.

#### Technical Factors

The table below lists Technical Factors

| Factor | Technical Factors | Weight | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| T1 | Distributed system | 2.0 | The system distributed architecture or centralized architecture. |
| T2 | Response adjectives | 1.0 | The response time is one of the important criteria. |
| T3 | End-user efficiency | 1.0 | The end-user efficiency. |
| T4 | Complex processing | 1.0 | The business process is very complex. |
| T5 | Reusable code | 1.0 | The project maintains high reusability. The design is complex. |
| T6 | Easy to install | 0.5 | The project requires simple installation. |
| T7 | Easy to use | 0.5 | User-friendly is one of the important criteria. |
| T8 | Portable | 2.0 | The project requires cross-platform implementation. |
| T9 | Easy to change | 1.0 | The project is highly customizable in the future. The architectural design is complex. |
| T10 | Concurrent | 1.0 | The project has a large numbers of users working with locking support. The architecture increases the project complexity. |
| T11 | Security features | 1.0 | The project has heavy security. |
| T12 | Access for third parties | 1.0 | The project is dependent on the third party’s control. Studying and understanding the third party is required. |
| T13 | Special training required | 1.0 | The application is so complex for the user that training must be provided. |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven Model Data E3.

##### Technical Factor Value

Technical Factor Value (TFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Technical factor by its weight.

```text
TFactor = value x weight
```

##### Technical Complexity Factor

Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) is obtained with the addition of 0.6 to the sum of TFactor multiplied by 0.01.

```text
TCF = 0.6 + (0.01 * TFactor)
```

#### Environmental Factors

The Table below lists Environmental Factors

| Factor | Environmental Factors | Weight | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| E1 | Familiar with RUP | 1.5 | Staff in the project are familiar with domain and technical details of the project. |
| E2 | Application experience | 0.5 | The application experience level. |
| E3 | Object-oriented experience | 1.0 | Staff in the project have basic knowledge of the OOP concept. The project is implemented on the object oriented design. |
| E4 | Lead analyst capability | 0.5 | The analyst who is leading the project has enough domain knowledge. |
| E5 | Motivation | 1.0 | The project motivates staff to work including how the software industry is going on. |
| E6 | Stable requirements | 2.0 | The requirements are clear, stable, and unlikely to change in the future. |
| E7 | Part-time workers | -1.0 | Part-time staff are working on the project. |
| E8 | Difficult programming language | -1.0 | Complexity of a programming language. |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven Model Data E3.

##### Environmental Factor Value

Environmental Factor Value (EFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Environmental factor by its weight.

##### Environmental Factor

Environmental Factor (EF) is obtained with the addition of 1.4 to the sum of EFactor multiplied by -0.03.

```text
EF = 1.4 + (-0.03 * EFactor)
```

#### Project Estimation

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Variables of Use Case Project Estimation.

##### Adjusted Use Case Points

The Adjusted Use Case Point (UCP) is determined with the multiplication of Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP) by Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) and Environmental Factor (EF).

```text
UCP = UUCP * TCF * EF
```

##### Estimated Effort in Person Hours

The person hours multiplier or X hours is a ratio of the number of man hours (PHM) per Use Case Point based on past projects. If no historical data has been collected, industry experts suggest using a figure between 15 and 30. A typical value is 20.

```text
X hours = UCP * PHM
```

##### Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time

Divide X hours by the number of developers working on the project and working hours per day to determine Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time or Y days. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Y days to complete the project.

```text
Y days = X / nDev / hay
```

##### Estimated Effort in Working Days

Divide Y days by working days per month to get the estimated effort in working days or Z months. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Z month to complete the project.

```text
Z months = Y / d Month
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
