## PAGE 00886: Project Constraints

- page_id: `169679488`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679488/Project+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:48:59.962+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter describes how to setinclude pathsfor an includesystem file.

###### Include system file example

If you include any system file, you must set the system file path as well.

###### System file paths

To set the include path

1. From the Options menu choose Project .
2. Expand Code Engineering , and choose C++ Language Options . Select the Include path check-box. Click the Use include paths button (...) appearing on the right side of the Project options dialog box.
3. The Set Include Path dialog appears.

###### Set Include Path dialog box

| Button | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Add | The Open dialog box appears for the select directory path. |

| Remove | Remove the selected Include path. |
| --- | --- |
| Up | Move the selected Include path upward. |
| Down | Move the selected Include path downward. |

Select the code-engineering set and then select**Properties**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Finally, set the “Parse includes” property to “true”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Parse Includes option

Parse Includes option

If the user sets the “Parse Includes” property to “false”, during reverse engineering of the C++ code, the modeling tool will not parse inside the header file; all unknown types will be created in the “Default” package of the model. The user can then solve keyword-specific problems in the library, speed up the reverse process, and remove unnecessary models inside the header file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>This chapter describes how to set </span><span>include paths </span><span>for an include </span><span>system file</span><span>.</span></p><h6>Include system file example</h6><p>If you include any system file, you must set the system file path as well.</p><h6>System file paths</h6><p>To set the include path</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu choose <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>Expand <strong>Code Engineering</strong>, and choose <strong>C++ Language Options</strong>. Select the Include path check-box. Click the <strong>Use include paths</strong> button (...) appearing on the right side of the <strong>Project</strong> options dialog box.</li><li>The Set Include Path dialog appears.</li></ol><h6>Set Include Path dialog box</h6><table><colgroup><col style="width: 31.174475%;" /><col style="width: 68.82552%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Button </span></p></th><th><p><span>Action </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>Add </span></p></td><td><p><span>The </span><strong>Open </strong><span>dialog box appears for the select directory path. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table><colgroup><col style="width: 31.174475%;" /><col style="width: 68.82552%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Remove </strong></p></td><td><p><span>Remove the selected Include path. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Up </strong></p></td><td><p><span>Move the selected Include path upward. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Down </strong></p></td><td><p><span>Move the selected Include path downward. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>Select the code-engineering set and then select </span><strong>Properties</strong><span>.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-24_17-32-58.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Finally, set the “Parse includes” property to “true”.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-24_17-29-24.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ed922b7-b8db-419b-a8b8-5f71d700fc5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Parse Includes option</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>If the user sets the “Parse Includes” property to “false”, during reverse engineering of the C++ code, the modeling tool will not parse inside the header file; all unknown types will be created in the “Default” package of the model. The user can then solve keyword-specific problems in the library, speed up the reverse process, and remove unnecessary models inside the header file. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673566 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00887: Project disclaimer configuration

- page_id: `169673566`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673566/Project+disclaimer+configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-09T08:06:58.430+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a project disclaimer configuration to display custom disclaimers upon project opening. The disclaimers can contain any information you want the project users to be aware of before opening the project. The project can only be opened once all of the specified disclaimers have been read.

#### NOTE: Disclaimers in used projects

Disclaimers in used projects

- If the disclaimer configuration is created in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024xR1'] , it must be located in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing project data' space='MD2024xR1'] of the used project for the disclaimer to be shown when opening the main project.
- If you are using multiple projects that contain disclaimers, the order in which they are displayed is determined by the order in which the projects were used (however, disclaimers created in the main project are shown first). The same order applies if you create multiple classes with the «disclaimerConfiguration» stereotype in the same project; the disclaimers of a class that was created first are also displayed first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating project disclaimer configuration

To create a project disclaimer configuration

1. Create a Class element and name it (e.g. 'Project Disclaimer Configuration').
2. Right-click the Class element. In the shortcut menu, click Stereotype > select «disclaimerConfiguration» from the menu, and click Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_1.png']
3. Right-click the Class element and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
5. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text and click the Create Value button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_2.png']
6. In the Value box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_3.png']
7. (Optional) To add more disclaimer messages, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
8. Click **Close**.

##### Adding more messages to the project disclaimer configuration

To add more messages to the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text, and on the right side of the window, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_button.png'] button.
4. In the Value box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer.
5. Click Close .

##### Removing messages from the project disclaimer configuration

To remove messages from the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to remove, and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_button.png'] button.
5. Click Close .

##### Editing messages in the project disclaimer configuration

To edit messages in the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to edit, and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button.
5. Click Close .

##### Reordering messages in the project disclaimer configuration

The order of the messages displayed in the disclaimer depends on the order specified for the text values in the configuration's **text**tag.

To reorder messages in the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to move, and do one of the following:
  1. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_up_button.png'] button to move the text value up.
  2. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_down_button.png'] button to move the text value down.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The disclaimers can contain any information you want the project users to be aware of before opening the project. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673666 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00888: Project partitioning

- page_id: `169673666`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673666/Project+partitioning
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T15:42:44.682+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

In life, all large things are built from smaller parts. This also applies to large projects. For large models with several weakly dependent parts, it is advisable to split them into several separate project files (or project resources in a collaborative environment), also called used projects.

Project partitioning opens up additional possibilities, such as:

- Reusing the same model part (e.g. library) in several projects.
- Separate administration of different project parts. You can define different access rights in the collaborative environment for each used project. Separate developers or teams of developers can be assigned for each resource, clearly establishing responsibility boundaries and preventing inadvertent modifications when these projects are used in the read-only mode.
- Separate versioning. Each used project can have its own version history with its own tagging and branching, life cycle of development, feature-freezes, and stable releases.
- Increased performance on very large projects. Whenever used projects are marked as loaded manually, a user can selectively load only the necessary parts of the project. He/she can also open the isolated used project separately.

A model can be decomposed into separate projects. A used project is a part that can be separated from the main project and can be used for a specific purpose.

The modeling tool supports the following used project accessibility modes:

- Read-only
- Read-write

The decision to use a project as read-only or read-write depends on the maturity of a project and the organization ownership or responsibility rules for projects using other projects. If the library in the used project is complete (changes to it are not expected / likely / possible) it should be used in read-only mode. If a project is developed by a separate team and this team is responsible for this project and the project is reused in another project, it is recommended to use this project as read-only. This prevents inadvertent changes in the project by another team.

If a project is actively developed and evolves together with the projects that are using it, this project can be used in read-write accessibility mode. In this case you must be careful and remember that your changes to the used project will be reflected in other projects. Using Teamwork Cloud might be advisable in this case. And, of course, there can be mixed usage situations - when a project is used as read-only in some projects and as read-write in others.

Instructions on how to partition your project can be found in the following pages:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This also applies to large projects. For large models with several weakly dependent parts, it is advisable to split them into several separate project files (or project resources in a collaborative environment), also called used projects.</p><p>Project partitioning opens up additional possibilities, such as:</p><ul><li><span>Reusing the same model part (e.g. library) in several projects.</span></li><li>Separate administration of different project parts. You can define different access rights in the collaborative environment for each used project. Separate developers or teams of developers can be assigned for each resource, clearly establishing responsibility boundaries and preventing inadvertent modifications when these projects are used in the read-only mode.</li><li>Separate versioning. Each used project can have its own version history with its own tagging and branching, life cycle of development, feature-freezes, and stable releases.</li><li>Increased performance on very large projects. Whenever used projects are marked as loaded manually, a user can selectively load only the necessary parts of the project. He/she can also open the isolated used project separately.</li></ul><p>A model can be decomposed into separate projects. A used project is a part that can be separated from the main project and can be used for a specific purpose.</p><p>The modeling tool supports the following used project accessibility modes:</p><ul><li>Read-only</li><li>Read-write</li></ul><p>The decision to use a project as read-only or read-write depends on the maturity of a project and the organization ownership or responsibility rules for projects using other projects. If the library in the used project is complete (changes to it are not expected / likely / possible) it should be used in read-only mode. If a project is developed by a separate team and this team is responsible for this project and the project is reused in another project, it is recommended to use this project as read-only. This prevents inadvertent changes in the project by another team.</p><p>If a project is actively developed and evolves together with the projects that are using it, this project can be used in read-write accessibility mode. In this case you must be careful and remember that your changes to the used project will be reflected in other projects. Using Teamwork Cloud might be advisable in this case. And, of course, there can be mixed usage situations - when a project is used as read-only in some projects and as read-write in others.</p><p>Instructions on how to partition your project can be found in the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="83a571da-de4a-4433-a85f-360eea3a5425" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

## PAGE 00889: Project Properties dialog

- page_id: `169673410`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673410/Project+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T07:14:21.276+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Creating projects
- labels: ['forward-compatibility', 'compatibility-range', 'count-elements', 'project-statistics', 'detailed-statistics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1428371638

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1428371640

1228009252

**On this page**

**53**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1428371639

#### EXCERPT: Note

BLOCK

The **Project** **Properties** dialog provides general information about a project. You can add a title, author, version, and project description using this dialog. 
 
 To open the **Project Properties**dialog

1. Open the project.
2. Select the Project Properties command from the File men u.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project properties dialog.

#### TIP: Title

Title

The project title is assigned as a value of the project [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Documentation tab' space='MD2024xR1'].

##### Compatibility range

To specify compatibility range

1. Enter the Version value.
2. Enter the Compatible with. The value must be less or equal to the Version value.
3. Click OK after you are done.

#### NOTE: Compatible With

Compatible With

**Compatible With** defines the compatibility range between two Standard/System profile versions. Once specified, it helps to avoid read-only constraints in TWCloud projects when you use earlier profile versions locally rather than those used in the server project.

When the **Version** of the Standard/System profile on your computer is earlier (e.g. 19.0 SP1) than the **Version** of this profile on TWCloud (e.g. 19.0 SP3), but higher or equal to the value of **Compatible with** (e.g. 19.0), you will still be able to edit TWCloud projects using the profile.

##### Advanced options

To use advanced options

1. Click More to see more options:

- 
  - See the list of shared packages in the Shared Packages tab. In this tab you can:
    - Define a preferred path to the particular package. Click the ... button [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'] in the Preferred Path value box.
    - Set the project as a standard/system profile by selecting the **Standard/System Profile** check box. NoteThe **Shared Packages** tab is available for local projects only.
  - The Used Projects tab shows the projects and standard/system profiles used in the open project. Click the Open Project Usages button to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR1']**Used Projects** dialog , where you can modify project usages.
  - The Advanced tab shows project meta information. In this tab you can:
    - Reset all project element IDs. All project elements have their own ID (a unique identifier). While working with several related projects, you may receive a message about duplicated element IDs. After clicking the **Reset IDs** button, new IDs will be created for all project elements. NoteMake sure the project you are resetting IDs for is not used in other projects.
    - Reset the project ID. After clicking the **Reset Project ID** button, new IDs are created for the project. You cannot reset the project ID for server projects.

1428371636

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating projects' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with multiple projects' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68ebf696-3c61-4c7e-8905-90da56ae8061"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatible With</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="72e1bf56-832d-4ba2-86d7-0a16b65b9f6f"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Compatible With</strong> defines the compatibility range between two Standard/System profile versions. Once specified, it helps to avoid read-only constraints in TWCloud projects when you use earlier profile versions locally rather than those used in the server project.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>When the <strong>Version</strong> of the Standard/System profile on your computer is earlier (e.g. 19.0 SP1) than the <strong>Version</strong> of this profile on TWCloud (e.g. 19.0 SP3), but higher or equal to the value of <strong>Compatible with</strong> (e.g. 19.0), you will still be able to edit TWCloud projects using the profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Advanced options</h3><p>To use <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c742fd9f-2d6a-42d7-841e-614c7f4ceaad">advanced</ac:inline-comment-marker> options</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>More</strong> to see more options:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>See the list of shared packages in the <strong>Shared Packages</strong> tab. In this tab you can:<ul><li>Define a preferred path to the particular package. Click the <strong>...</strong> button <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[to select the package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>Preferred Path </strong>value box.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the project as a standard/system profile by selecting the <strong>Standard/System Profile</strong> check box.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d006b130-6fed-4c33-aa2d-fc74ac9b0ee2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Shared Packages</strong> tab is available for local projects only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>The <strong>Used Projects</strong> tab shows the projects and standard/system profiles used in the open project. Click the <strong>Open Project Usages</strong> button to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, where you can modify project usages.</li><li>The <strong>Advanced</strong> tab shows project meta information. In this tab you can:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Reset all project element IDs. All project elements have their own ID (a unique identifier). While working with several related projects, you may receive a message about duplicated element IDs. After clicking the <strong>Reset IDs</strong> button, new IDs will be created for all project elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b4ce30d-b3e2-467c-a370-04c80e217d89"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the project you are resetting IDs for is not used in other projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Reset the project ID. After clicking the <strong>Reset Project ID</strong> button, new IDs are created for the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b0e2d4c-8df9-4308-bf92-d042fedf055c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot reset the project ID for server projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33b51e8b-7d63-4832-bc54-a253ca8e7211"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428371636</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with multiple projects" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676225 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00890: Project Statistics

- page_id: `169676225`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676225/Project+Statistics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-07-26T11:25:50.035+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Detailed project statistics

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help us solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_statistics_tools.png']
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_statistics_dialog.png']
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_detailed_statistics_options.png']
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export .
6. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Diagrams**

- Diagram dimensions
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind
- Symbol count by diagram

**Elements**

- Element count by UML type
- Element count by DSL type
- Derived properties count
- Property count by size
- Elements used for CDH mapping count
- Elements used for transformation mapping count
- Longest flat list count (longest flat list of elements under one element's root, such as under a package or a classifier)
- Smart package count
- Number of stereotype applications by stereotype name
- Number of stereotype tagged values by defining feature name and tagged value size
- Number of strings in the model by size and metaProperty
- Number of multi-references by size and metaProperty
- Number of profiling elements (stereotype instance, slots, and value specifications)

**Project/Environment options**

- Active/passive validation options
- Number of active validation rules by implementation language
- Number of code engineering options by language
- Symbol styles count
- Symbol styles properties count

**Hardware & system**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disks size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size in a binary blob
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Locked elements count
- Used projects count
- Root object count by type

**Performance**

- Time to build diagrams

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

$project is a project reference enabling a template to return the project information.

##### **$project.getName()**

Returns a project name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project name. |

##### **$project.getTitle()**

Gets a project title.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project title. |

##### **$project.getFileName()**

Gets a project filename.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project filename. |

##### **$project.getExtension()**

Gets a project filename extension.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This function is only for local projects.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project filename extension. |

##### **$project.getDirectory()**

Get a project directory name.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This function is only for local projects.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project directory name. |

##### **$project.getVersionList()**

Returns a list of version information from an open Server project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.List | A list of com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion. |

##### **$project.getDiagrams()**

Returns all existing diagrams stored in a particular project, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram instances. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

$project.getDiagrams() returns all the diagrams in the selected scope, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.

##### **$project.getDiagrams(type)**

Returns existing diagrams of a given type stored in a particular project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A diagram type. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram instances. |

```text

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A diagram type. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram views. |

```text

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.lang.Boolean | Return true if a project is a remote project, otherwise false. |

| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Project Version

The full class name is com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion. 
ProjectVersion is a class that represents some version of the project or module. It contains date, user, number, etc.

ProjectVersion contains the following properties:

| Property | Return type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| comment | java.lang.String | Returns a version committed comment. |
| date | java.util.Date | Returns a committed date. |
| dateAsString | java.lang.String | Returns a committed date as text. |
| number | long | Returns a committed version. |
| numberAsString | java.lang.String | Returns a committed version as text. |
| user | java.lang.String | Returns a committer’s name. |
| version | java.lang.Object | Returns a version object com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.IEsiVersionDescriptor for a Teamwork Cloud project. |
| tags | java.util.List<java.lang.String> | Returns all tags. |

```text

```

#### Project Module

The full class name is com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule. 
ProjectModule is a wrapper Class of an attached project (com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject) that represents a special kind of the project that is used as a component in the current project.

The following are some properties and methods of ProjectModule:

| Property / Method | Return type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| representationString | java.lang.String | Returns user-friendly representation string about the module. |
| description | java.lang.String | Returns project description of the module. |
| version | long | Returns a version number of the module. |
| remoteID | java.lang.String | Returns a remote ID for the module. |
| URI | java.net.URI | Converts EMF URI to java.net.URI. |
| versionList | java.util.List<> | Returns a list of version descriptors of the module. |
| isRemote() | boolean | Checks if the module is remote - from Teamwork Cloud. |

```text

```

ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4a149c2-d6a4-4c07-a720-aae93e212cf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This function is only for local project directory name.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getVersionList()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of version information from an open Server project.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 71.9531%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 6.5708%;" /><col style="width: 6.05449%;" /><col style="width: 20.0807%;" /><col style="width: 67.3377%;" colspan="1">-</td><td colspan="1">-</td></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Return</span></strong></td><td>-</td><td><span>java.util.List</span></td><td><span>A list of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectVersion"><ac:link-body><span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion</span></ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getDiagrams()</strong></h3><p>Returns all existing diagrams stored in a particular project, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 47.7327%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 11.1086%;" /><col style="width: 9.89092%;" /><col style="width: 22.5216%;" /><col style="width: 56.5474%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Return</span></strong></td><td>-</td><td><span>java.util.Collection</span></td><td><span>A collection of diagram instances.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2246b778-96e7-4258-9602-1cc2795efcfe"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>$project.getDiagrams() returns all the diagrams in the selected scope, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>$project.getDiagrams(type)</strong></h3><p>Returns existing diagrams of a given type stored in a particular project.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.4382%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.8278%;" /><col style="width: 7.68322%;" /><col style="width: 17.4921%;" /><col style="width: 61.0481%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Parameter(s)</span></strong></td><td>type</td><td><span>java.lang.String</span></td><td><span>A diagram instances.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="055776fd-e46b-451a-9706-64bb1eaade78"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($classDiagram = $project.getDiagrams("Class Diagram"))
#foreach($cl in $classDiagram)
$cl.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>“Class Diagram” is a diagram type.</li></ul><h3><strong>$project.getPresentationDiagrams()</strong></h3><p>Returns all existing presentation diagrams stored in a particular project.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 48.1678%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 11.1589%;" /><col style="width: 9.80141%;" /><col style="width: 22.3177%;" /><col style="width: 56.7898%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><span>java.util.Collection</span></td><td><span>A collection of diagram views.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$project.getPresentationDiagrams(type)</strong></h3><p>Returns all existing presentation diagrams of a given type stored in a particular project.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 48.4578%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 17.5387%;" /><col style="width: 9.74263%;" /><col style="width: 22.1839%;" /><col style="width: 50.6022%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Parameter(s)</span></strong></td><td><span>type</span></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span></td><td><span>A diagram type.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Return</span></strong></td><td>-</td><td><span>java.util.Collection</span></td><td><span>A collection of diagram views.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3321d04-e5df-4495-aa6a-af6ad1dedd2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($classDiagram = $project.getPresentationDiagrams("Class Diagram"))
#foreach($cl in $classDiagram)
$cl.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>“Class Diagram” is a diagram type.</li></ul><h3><strong>$project.isRemote()</strong></h3><p>Returns the remote or non-remote state of a project.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 66.7319%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 7.0856%;" /><col style="width: 6.52884%;" /><col style="width: 13.9282%;" /><col otherwise false.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.isDirty()</strong></h3><p>Returns true if that particular project was modified after it had been saved or loaded.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 79.1322%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 5.97401%;" /><col style="width: 5.50459%;" /><col style="width: 11.7431%;" /><col style="width: 76.8181%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><span>java.lang.Boolean</span></td><td><span>Return true if a project was modified after it had been saved/loaded, otherwise false.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getElementByID(id)</strong></h3><p>Returns an element with a given ID.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 96.8987%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.01498%;" /><col style="width: 4.41948%;" /><col style="width: 22.0212%;" /><col style="width: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>Description</span></span></th></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Parameter(s)</span></strong></td><td><span>ID</span></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span></td><td><span>An element ID.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong><span>Return</span></strong></td><td><span>-</span></td><td><span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement</span></td><td><span>An element with a given ID or null if the element with such ID is not registered in the project.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" $project.getElementByID("_9_0_62a020a_1105704887361_983947_8206"))
$ele.name
$ele.humanType]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>“_9_0_62a020a_1105704887361_983947_8206” is the element’s ID number.</li></ul><h3><strong>$project.getAllElementId()</strong></h3><p>Returns a collection of all element IDs in a project.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 48.7661%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>projectModule</td><td><ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getServerModule()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used server modules.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 73.766%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 6.40912%;" /><col style="width: 5.90551%;" /><col style="width: 25.5676%;" /><col style="width: 62.1604%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getServerModule(includeAuxiliary)</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used server modules as well as auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.9219%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.57687%;" /><col style="width: 11.5075%;" /><col style="width: 16.483%;" /><col style="width: 63.468%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>includeAuxiliary</td><td>boolean</td><td>Returns server modules including auxiliary resources if this value is true (false by default).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getLocalModule()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used local modules.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 80.0024%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 5.90895%;" /><col style="width: 5.44465%;" /><col style="width: 18.5935%;" /><col style="width: 70.0923%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getLocalModule(includeAuxiliary)</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used local modules as well as auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 91.46%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.33862%;" /><col style="width: 9.36508%;" /><col style="width: 13.9643%;" /><col style="width: 68.3664%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>includeAuxiliary</td><td>boolean</td><td>Returns local modules including auxiliary resources if this value is true (false by default).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getAuxiliaryResource()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 79.8574%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 5.9197%;" /><col style="width: 5.45455%;" /><col style="width: 18.8985%;" /><col style="width: 69.7667%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h2>Project Version</h2><p>The full class name is <span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion</span>.<br />ProjectVersion is a class that represents some version of the project or module. It contains date, user, number, etc.</p><p><br />ProjectVersion contains the following properties:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Return type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>comment</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a version committed comment.</td></tr><tr><td>date</td><td>java.util.Date</td><td>Returns a committed date.</td></tr><tr><td>dateAsString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committed date as text.</td></tr><tr><td>number</td><td>long</td><td>Returns a committed version.</td></tr><tr><td>numberAsString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committed version as text.</td></tr><tr><td>user</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committer’s name.</td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>Returns a version object com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.IEsiVersionDescriptor for a Teamwork Cloud project.</td></tr><tr><td>tags</td><td>java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt;</td><td>Returns all tags.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="472aa08a-04cb-4ed8-92f4-c062256dd26d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Current version : $project.version
All version:
#foreach ($version in $project.getVersionList())
	Date : 				$version.date
	Number : 			$version.number
	Number As String : 	$version.numberAsString
	User : 				$version.user
	Comment : 			$version.comment
	Tags:
#foreach($tag in $version.tags) 
		- tag
#end
---
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Project Module</h2><p>The full class name is <span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule</span>.<br />ProjectModule is a wrapper Class of an attached project (com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject) that represents a special kind of the project that is used as a component in the current project.</p><p><br />The following are some properties and methods of ProjectModule:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 99.9444%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 10.6754%;" /><col style="width: 20.6899%;" /><col style="width: 68.6662%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property / Method</th><th>Return type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>representationString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns user-friendly representation string about the module.</td></tr><tr><td>description</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns project description of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>long</td><td>Returns a version number of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>remoteID</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a remote ID for the module.</td></tr><tr><td>URI</td><td>java.net.URI</td><td>Converts EMF URI to java.net.URI.</td></tr><tr><td>versionList</td><td>java.util.List&lt;<ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectVersion"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project tool" /></ac:link>&gt;</td><td>Returns a list of version descriptors of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>isRemote()</td><td>boolean</td><td>Checks if the module is remote - from Teamwork Cloud.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c8dad29-d010-40c1-b029-6a38467dc0fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($module in $project.getModuleList())
	Name : $module.representationString
	Description : $module.description
	Version : $module.version
	RemoteID : $module.remoteID
	URI : $module.URI 
#if($module.isRemote())
#set($version = $module.projectVersion)
	Current module version :
		date : $version.date
		number : $version.number 
		numberAsString : $version.numberAsString
		user : $version.user
		comment : $version.comment
		tags: 
#foreach($tag in $version.tags)
- $tag
#end
	All module versions :
#foreach($version in $module.versionList)
		date : $version.date
		number : $version.number
		numberAsString : $version.numberAsString
		user : $version.usercomment : $version.comment 
		tags:
#foreach($tag in $version.tags)
- $tag
#end
----------
#end
#end 
	Shared module:
#foreach ($child in $project.getSharedModule($module))
		- $child.representationString
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673559 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00892: Projects with file attachments

- page_id: `169673559`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673559/Projects+with+file+attachments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:27:09.516+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305056268

305056271

305056270

You can attach any type of file from the local file system to your project.

Attached files are bundled with the project and remain attached after transferring the project, keeping project references to these files not broken.

The contents of the attached file can be modified and the changes are instantly saved to the project. Different versions of a server project can refer to different versions of the same attached file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

305056267

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Different versions of a server project can refer to different versions of the same attached file.  </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Concept of Project with File Attachments " ac:alt="Concept of Project with File Attachments "><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_with_file_attachments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Projects with file attachments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="090364b5-81e8-4ee5-adb0-a9dea2351601"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305056267</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6c953183-7162-4bdf-9a06-c148a29501c6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00893: Properties dialog

- page_id: `169674804`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674804/Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-03-15T08:29:29.161+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Saving as image
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Image export options

768901281

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Image export options

768901283

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Image export options

768901282

In the **Properties** dialog, you can specify image export properties specific to the image format selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog. For example, if the selected image format is .jpg, or .jpeg, the **Properties** dialog allows you to specify JPEG compression quality, image resolution (DPI), and exported image size.

To specify image export options

1. From the File menu, select Save as Image .
2. In the Save As Image dialog, specify the desired image format, and click the Options button.
3. In the Properties dialog, specify given properties.
4. Click **OK**. Property related informationIf you need more information about the properties provided in the **Properties** dialog, make sure that the **Show Description** button is pressed (as shown in the figure below), then select the desired property, and read its description in the description area on the bottom of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This Properties dialog displays the image export properties specific to the .jpg, .jpeg image format. If the highlighted button is pressed, the description of the selected property is provided in the description area on the bottom of the dialog.

#### NOTE: Image export options

Image export options

Default image export properties can be changed manually both via the **Environment Options** and the **Project Options** dialogs. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024xR1'] page.

1341338422

**Related pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679114 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00894: Properties Displayed in Compartments

- page_id: `169679114`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679114/Properties+Displayed+in+Compartments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-09-02T08:39:59.715+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using Properties Displayed in Compartments, you can select the specific properties to be displayed on the element shape.

To customize the properties to be displayed in compartments

1. Create a customization for your DSL element or select an existing one.
2. In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , under Symbol, locate Properties Displayed in Compartments , and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='button.png'] button.
3. In the Select Properties dialog, select the specific properties you want to see displayed in the compartment of your shape(s).
4. Reopen or refresh the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using Properties Displayed in Compartments, you can select the specific properties to be displayed on the element shape.</p><p>To customize the properties to be displayed in compartments</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a customization for your DSL element or select an existing one.</li><li>In the element <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, under Symbol, locate <strong>Properties Displayed in Compartments</strong>, and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties Displayed in Compartments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Properties</strong> dialog, select the specific properties you want to see displayed in the compartment of your shape(s). </li><li>Reopen or refresh the project. </li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00895: Properties filename

- page_id: `169678511`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678511/Properties+filename
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:27.599+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

1092254395

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

1092254398

1092279517

**On this page**

- [XML Properties File](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Properties+filename#Propertiesfilename-XMLPropertiesFile)

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

1092254397

When you use -properties, other command actions are no longer necessary; the properties file contains the configuration information. The information in the properties file is the same as the information used in the command line.

Four parameters are needed to specify data for a report: **project**, **output**, **template**, and **package**. There are five more parameters called options, which are additional configuration information such as **report**, **autoImage**, **imageFormat**, **recursive**, and **outputOnBlankField**. All nine parameters are described in*[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='MD2024xR1']*.

A properties file is a simple text file. You can create and maintain a properties file with any text editors (see the following example).

```text

```

In the figure above, the command lines begin with a pound sign (#). The other lines contain key-value pairs. The key is on the left side of the equal sign, and the value is on the right. For instance, template is the key that corresponds to the value of the **Class Specification Report**.

The string representing the key and value in the properties file has a special character. The special character is an escape character. It must be used to prevent interpretation errors. Each escape character starts with a backslash.

| Escape Sequence | Character |
| --- | --- |
| \n | new line |
| \t | tab |
| \b | backspace |
| \f | form feed |
| \r | return |
| \” | “ (double quote) |
| \’ | ‘ (single quote) |
| \\ | \ (backslash) |
| \uDDDD | character from the Unicode character set (DDDD is four hex digits) |

##### XML Properties File

J2SE version 1.5 and Report Wizard version 15.5 allow you to use XML files to load and save key-value pairs.

Properties DTD:

```text
]]>
```

```text
C:\MagicDraw\samples\diagrams\class diagram.mdzip
	C:\\output\\output.rtf<\entry>
	Class Specification Report
	java;Library system;magiclibrary;objects
]]>
```

1092511756

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Uploading generated reports to servers
- Syntax rules
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48abd90f-dc7c-40e8-b876-6c98336bb83c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092254395</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a4bef34-998f-4aed-b6ba-d341361aeb50"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092254398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d12f0f5-93b4-4ff7-ab9b-444720186861"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092279517</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><ul><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Properties+filename#Propertiesfilename-XMLPropertiesFile">XML Properties File</a></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3ad6c0a-a1a1-4356-ad34-16570a4c6760"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092254397</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you use -properties, other command actions are no longer necessary; the properties file contains the configuration information. The information in the properties file is the same as the information used in the command line.</p><p>Four parameters are needed to specify data for a report: <strong>project</strong>, <strong>output</strong>, <strong>template</strong>, and <strong>package</strong>. There are five more parameters called options, which are additional configuration information such as <strong>report</strong>, <strong>autoImage</strong>, <strong>imageFormat</strong>, <strong>recursive</strong>, and <strong>outputOnBlankField</strong>. All nine parameters are described in<em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using the Generate command to generate reports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generate - the Command to Generate Reports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>.</p><p>A properties file is a simple text file. You can create and maintain a properties file with any text editors (see the following example).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3eb2e64-4bec-4780-85a7-f45cda2be16d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Properties file sample</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#----main argument ----#
project = C:\\MagicDraw\\samples\\diagrams\\class diagram.mdzip
output = C:\\output\\output.rtf
template = Class Specification Report
package = java;Library System;magiclibrary;objects


#---- optional ----#
report = Built-in
autoImage = 1
imageFormat = png
recursive = false
outputOnBlankField = #NA]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the figure above, the command lines begin with a pound sign (#). The other lines contain key-value pairs. The key is on the left side of the equal sign, and the value is on the right. For instance, template is the key that corresponds to the value of the <strong>Class Specification Report</strong>.</p><p>The string representing the key and value in the properties file has a special character. The special character is an escape character. It must be used to prevent interpretation errors. Each escape character starts with a backslash.</p><table class="wrapped" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Escape Sequence </th><th style="margin-left: 30.0px;"> Character</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">\n </td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">new line </td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">\t </td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">tab </td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\b</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">backspace</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\f </td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">form feed</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\r</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">return</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\”</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1"> “ (double quote)</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\’</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">‘ (single quote)</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\\</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\ (backslash)</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\uDDDD</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">character from the Unicode character <br />set (DDDD is four hex digits)</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>XML Properties File</h3><p>J2SE version 1.5 and Report Wizard version 15.5 allow you to use XML files to load and save key-value pairs.</p><p>Properties DTD:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb3a1a03-5f03-41f9-97c6-604ccbd0fa48"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!-- DTD for properties -->
<!ELEMENT properties ( comment?, entry* ) >
<!ATTLIST properties version CDATA #FIXED "1.0">
<!ELEMENT comment (#PCDATA) >
<!ELEMENT entry (#PCDATA) >
<!ATTLIST entry key CDATA #REQUIRED>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0d9833b-40cd-4b36-881a-3d24d78cdb91"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPEproperties SYSTEM
"http://java.sun.com/dtd/properties.dtd">
<properties>
	<entry key = "project">C:\MagicDraw\samples\diagrams\class diagram.mdzip</entry>
	<entry key = "output">C:\\output\\output.rtf<\entry>
	<entry key = "template">Class Specification Report</entry>
	<entry key = "package">java;Library system;magiclibrary;objects</entry>
</properties>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6222d8e5-373a-4a47-ac0f-dd711cd2cc95"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092511756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Syntax+rules">Syntax rules</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using the Generate command to generate reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675779 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00896: Properties Inheritance

- page_id: `169675779`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675779/Properties+Inheritance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-03-31T17:31:27.638+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All element properties have the “inherited” check box. This check box indicates whether the property is derived from the base element properties or is it specific.

The **Inherited** column check box value in the element properties pane specifies if the current property is synchronized with its parent property. When the **Inherited** value is “true”, the element property is changed after changing the parent property.

If the property has no correspondent property in the upper (parent) level, the **Inherited** column check box will be cleared and not available.

If the property is modified for the specific element and the value differs from the upper level current property value, the **Inherited** column check box is cleared automatically.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All element properties have the “inherited” check box. This check box indicates whether the property is derived from the base element properties or is it specific.</p><p>The <strong>Inherited</strong> column check box value in the element properties pane specifies if the current property is synchronized with its parent property. When the <strong>Inherited</strong> value is “true”, the element property is changed after changing the parent property.</p><p>If the property has no correspondent property in the upper (parent) level, the <strong>Inherited</strong> column check box will be cleared and not available.</p><p>If the property is modified for the specific element and the value differs from the upper level current property value, the <strong>Inherited</strong> column check box is cleared automatically.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfc67ded-5f15-4cbf-b135-39abac861fd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="84b3992d-f7e3-423e-9378-f287af93aee8"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673063 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00897: Properties Spelling

- page_id: `169673063`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673063/Properties+Spelling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-18T18:16:48.066+02:00`
- ancestors: Spelling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

SpellError

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the property value has a spelling error.

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Element

**Solvers**

- Correct - opens the Spell Checker dialog allowing you to correct the spelling error.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**The element *Car*has a property *Airbags*with the Default Value *Frontl*, containing a spelling error that triggers the validation error. 
The error is resolved by correcting the Default Value's text.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>SpellError</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the property value has a spelling error.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Element</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Correct<span> </span></strong>- opens the Spell Checker dialog allowing you to correct the spelling error.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Properties_Spelling.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties Spelling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>The element <em>Car </em>has a property <em>Airbags </em>with the Default Value <em>Frontl</em>, containing a spelling error that triggers the validation error.<br />The error is resolved by correcting the Default Value's text. </h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673340 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00898: Properties tab

- page_id: `169673340`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673340/Properties+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:48.926+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Quick properties panel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Multiple selection

1425917920

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Multiple selection

1425917923

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Multiple selection

1425917921

#### EXCERPT: Multiple selection

INLINE

The **Properties** tab at the bottom of the Model Browser allows you to quickly access basic information about a selected element or diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Properties tab in the Quick properties panel

#### TIP: Multiple selection

Multiple selection

- If multiple elements or symbols of different kinds are selected, only their common properties are displayed in the Properties tab.
- If more than 10 elements in the Containment tree or symbols on the diagram pane are selected, properties are displayed only by request. You need to click the link Show common properties for the selected items .

To open the **Properties** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Properties tab.
- On the Window menu, click Properties .

The **Properties** tab includes the following tabs:

| Tab name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Element | Contains the main properties of the element's or diagram's specification. |
| Symbol | Contains the element symbol's properties. noteThis tab is available for symbols only. |
| Tags | Contains the tagged values of the selected element.When you click the Show Only Applied Stereotypes Tags button, only the tabs with values are listed. |
| Language properties | Contains the same data as the Language Properties property group in the element's Specification window.This tab is available only for the elements used for code generation, that is, class, attribute, and operation. |
| Traceability | Contains the same data as the Traceability property group in the element's Specification window. |

1425917919

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

## PAGE 00899: Property group toolbar

- page_id: `169675641`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1655141189

1655141191

1655141190

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Icon | Name | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Categorized view | Groups properties by the categories. |
|  | Alphabetical view | Lists properties alphabetically. |
|  | Show Description | Shows or hides the description area of the selected property. |
|  | Expand All | Expand all collapsed categories. |
|  | Collapse All | Collapse all expanded categories. |
|  | Strip Multiline Text | If the text in the property cell (e.g., ToDo) covers more than five rows, it is finished with ... showing only those five rows. The complete text is available in the tooltip or in the cell edit mode. |
|  | Properties | Change the properties display mode to Standard, Expert, or All. Select Customize, to customize displayed properties. |
|  | Show Private Inherited | Show the inherited properties. |
|  | Hide Inherited Relations | Hide the relationships that are inherited. |

The toolbar configuration can vary according to the selected property group.
