<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673882 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00900: Protocol State Machine diagram

- page_id: `169673882`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673882/Protocol+State+Machine+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-06-19T08:29:27.948+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308031736

308031738

308031737

**Overview**

A Protocol State Machine diagram is, in general, a more narrowly defined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']. It is always defined in the context of a classifier. It specifies which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024xR1'] of the classifier can be called, in which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR1'], and under which condition, thus specifying the allowable call sequences on the classifier's operations.

The Protocol State Machine presents the possible and permitted [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR1'] on the instances of its context classifier, together with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024xR1'] that carry the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR1'].

In this manner, an instance lifecycle can be created for a classifier, by specifying the order in which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024xR1'] can be activated and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR1'] through which the instance progresses during its existence.

**Purpose**

The Protocol State Machine diagram is created for use with the Protocol State Machine and the Protocol Transitions. It limits the scope of the State Machine diagram to show only the transitions that trigger the state changes.

**Usage**

A Protocol State Machine diagram can be used to:

- show different stable class states of objects that could exist for some time
- explain how objects could change their states over time

**Summary**

Protocol State Machine diagrams are valuable because they:

- place the focus on what it takes to legally change an object
- reveal conditions that ensure the integrity of the object while it is being manipulated by other objects in the system
- keep the designer from focusing on the implementation details before resolving the integrity issues.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Protocol State Machine diagram

308031735

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a064cb1-166e-4cf2-abee-3c5bbdc2ce0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308031736</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="761c8708-f58c-4333-ae54-cd9c8cb7cfc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308031738</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f02570f3-c55a-4e65-bf9e-f58ff269a165"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308031737</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Overview</strong></p><p>A Protocol State Machine diagram is, in general, a more narrowly defined <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link>. It is always defined in the context of a classifier. It specifies which <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the classifier can be called, in which <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link>, and under which condition, thus specifying the allowable call sequences on the classifier's operations.</p><p>The Protocol State Machine presents the possible and permitted <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Transition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Transitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the instances of its context classifier, together with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that carry the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Transition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Transitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>In this manner, an instance lifecycle can be created for a classifier, by specifying the order in which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be activated and the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="State" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[States]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> through which the instance progresses during its existence.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>The Protocol State Machine diagram is created for use with the Protocol State Machine and the Protocol Transitions. It limits the scope of the State Machine diagram to show only the transitions that trigger the state changes. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A Protocol State Machine diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>show different stable class states of objects that could exist for some time</li><li>explain how objects could change their states over time</li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Protocol State Machine diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>place the focus on what it takes to legally change an object</li><li>reveal conditions that ensure the integrity of the object while it is being manipulated by other objects in the system</li><li>keep the designer from focusing on the implementation details before resolving the integrity issues. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="protocol_state_machine_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Protocol State Machine diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c9f33e9-04d4-4727-a858-96de41adc542"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308031735</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675189 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00901: Provided and Required Interfaces

- page_id: `169675189`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675189/Provided+and+Required+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-03-27T12:35:52.846+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The set of interfaces realized by a classifier is its provided interfaces, which represent the obligations that instances of that classifier have to their clients. They describe the services that the instances of that classifier offer to their clients.

The interfaces may also be used to specify required interfaces, which are specified by a usage dependency between the classifier and the corresponding interfaces. Required interfaces specify services that a classifier needs in order to perform its function and fulfill its own obligations to its clients.

To draw a Provided Interface

1. In the Class diagram, create a Class.
2. Select the Class shape and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Interface Realization and then click on the diagram pane. The provided interface is created.
3. Select the interface shape, click the Compartments smart manipulator button, and then select Suppress All . Alternatively, click the Suppress All button in the diagram toolbar.

To draw a Required Interface

1. In the Class diagram, create a Class.
2. Select the Class shape and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Usage button and then click on the diagram. The required interface is created.
3. Select the interface shape, click the Compartments smart manipulator button, and then select Suppress All . Alternatively, click the Suppress All button in the diagram toolbar.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The set of interfaces realized by a classifier is its provided interfaces, which represent the obligations that instances of that classifier have to their clients. They describe the services that the instances of that classifier offer to their clients.</p><p>The interfaces may also be used to specify required interfaces, which are specified by a usage dependency between the classifier and the corresponding interfaces. Required interfaces specify services that a classifier needs in order to perform its function and fulfill its own obligations to its clients.</p><p> </p><p>To draw a Provided Interface</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Class diagram, create a Class.</li><li>Select the Class shape and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Interface Realization and then click on the diagram pane. The provided interface is created.</li><li>Select the interface shape, click the Compartments smart manipulator button, and then select <strong>Suppress All</strong>. Alternatively, click the <strong>Suppress All</strong> button in the diagram toolbar. <br /><br /></li></ol><p>To draw a Required Interface</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Class diagram, create a Class.</li><li>Select the Class shape and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Usage button and then click on the diagram. The required interface is created.</li><li>Select the interface shape, click the Compartments smart manipulator button, and then select <strong>Suppress All</strong>. Alternatively, click the <strong>Suppress All</strong> button in the diagram toolbar. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675191 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00902: Provided and Required Interfaces in the Composite Structure diagram

- page_id: `169675191`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675191/Provided+and+Required+Interfaces+in+the+Composite+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:19:07.501+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Information about provided and required interfaces is crucial in the assembly stage of complex internal structures. It helps to decide where connectors should be attached. 
Provided and required interfaces are valuable parts of the UML Composite Structure Diagram and SysML Internal Block Diagram.

A provided interface is shown using the "lollipop" notation attached to the port, and a required interface is shown using the "fork" notation attached to the port (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the Composite Structure diagram, you cannot draw provided and required interfaces themselves, but, with the new functionality of the modeling tool, you can display a preexisting port with the required and provided interfaces as images.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Lollipop and fork symbols in the Composite Structure diagram are implemented as small attachments to a Port symbol similar to name a label. It is not the same as the independent stand alone notation of the interface, it is only part of the port symbol. It is important for Composite Structure diagrams in which real Interfaces (as Classifiers) cannot be used. It is an optional notation as a port does not display provided or required interfaces by default.

To display provided/required interfaces in the Composite Structure diagram

1. Select the port shape that has the provided/required interfaces you want to display.
2. Do one of the following: - From the port shortcut menu, select Display > Display Provided/Required Interfaces . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Provided/Required Interfaces.

Ports can provide or require many interfaces, therefore, you can choose which ones to display or hide. Use the **Edit Compartment** dialog to manage the visibility of these interfaces.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Information about provided and required interfaces is crucial in the assembly stage of complex internal structures. It helps to decide where connectors should be attached.<br />Provided and required interfaces are valuable parts of the UML Composite Structure Diagram and SysML Internal Block Diagram.</p><p>A provided interface is shown using the &quot;lollipop&quot; notation attached to the port, and a required interface is shown using the &quot;fork&quot; notation attached to the port (see the following figure).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Provided and Required interface" ac:alt="Provided and Required interface"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interface_incomposite_strd1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Provided and Required Interfaces in the Composite Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In the Composite Structure diagram, you cannot draw provided and required interfaces themselves, but, with the new functionality of the modeling tool, you can display a preexisting port with the required and provided interfaces as images.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Provided and required interface in the Composite Structure diagram" ac:alt="Provided and required interface in the Composite Structure diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interface_incomposite_strd2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Provided and Required Interfaces in the Composite Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Lollipop and fork symbols in the Composite Structure diagram are implemented as small attachments to a Port symbol similar to name a label. It is not the same as the independent stand alone notation of the interface, it is only part of the port symbol. It is important for Composite Structure diagrams in which real Interfaces (as Classifiers) cannot be used. It is an optional notation as a port does not display provided or required interfaces by default.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display provided/required interfaces in the Composite Structure diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the port shape that has the provided/required interfaces you want to display.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br />- From the port shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Provided/Required Interfaces</strong>.<br />- On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Provided and Required Interfaces in the Composite Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and select <strong>Display Provided/Required Interfaces.</strong><br /><br /></li></ol><p>Ports can provide or require many interfaces, therefore, you can choose which ones to display or hide. Use the <strong>Edit Compartment</strong> dialog to manage the visibility of these interfaces.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675195 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00903: Provided/required interfaces in the Component diagram

- page_id: `169675195`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675195/Provided+required+interfaces+in+the+Component+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-02-01T09:42:50.534+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A component specifies a formal contract of the services that it provides to its clients and those that it requires from other components or services in the system in terms of its provided and required interfaces.

The required and provided interfaces may optionally be organized through ports.

To add and manage the provided and required interfaces quickly, in the **Component** Specification window, select the **Provided/Required Interfaces** property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A component specifies a formal contract of the services that it provides to its clients and those that it requires from other components or services in the system in terms of its provided and required interfaces. </p><p>The required and provided interfaces may optionally be organized through ports. </p><p>To add and manage the provided and required interfaces quickly, in the <strong>Component</strong> Specification window, select the <strong>Provided/Required Interfaces</strong> property group.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ordering_interfaces.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Provided/required interfaces in the Component diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00904: Pseudo States

- page_id: `169675415`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675415/Pseudo+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-04T11:35:15.107+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Pseudo state is typically used to connect multiple transitions into more complex state transition's paths. For example, by combining a transition entering a fork pseudo state with a set of transitions exiting the fork pseudo state, we get a compound transition that leads to a set of orthogonal target states.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1'] in the pseudo state [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674788 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00905: Pusher and magnet

- page_id: `169674788`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674788/Pusher+and+magnet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-23T06:17:43.129+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903750137

903750139

903750138

This feature allows for rearranging element symbols on a diagram easily. The Pusher moves a group of symbols towards the pushing direction, thus creating an empty space for adding new symbols to a diagram.

Magnet works in the opposite way. It moves the selected group of symbols to an empty space on a diagram.

Now you do not need to select separate symbols on a diagram to move them to another location. Just click the **Pusher** or **Magnet** button on the diagram palette and move the whole group of symbols toward the direction you need.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To push the diagram fragment

1. On the diagram palette, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='pusher.png'] Pusher button.
2. On the diagram pane, click the diagram fragment you need to move, and holding the right mouse button, drag it to the left or down.

To pull the diagram fragment

1. On the diagram palette, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='magnet.png'] Magnet button.
2. On the diagram pane, click the diagram fragment you need to move, and holding the right mouse button, drag it to the right or up.

903750136

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675965 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00906: Qualified name display style

- page_id: `169675965`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675965/Qualified+name+display+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-02T16:51:13.665+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Owner of model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is an option to show the owner hierarchy starting from the model library as the root. *Model Library*****is a package with the stereotype *«modelLibrary»* applied.

This option is called the **Qualified name display style**.

To change the **Qualified name display style**value

1. From the Options menu, select the Project command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1']**Project Option** dialo g opens.
2. In the option group list, select General > General , and then select the Qualified name display style option.
3. For the option, set Model Library Relative (default value for a new project) to display the full qualified name hierarchy of the element starting from the model library as a root element. The model library itself is not displayed. The Qualified name display style property allows for having the relative path for library items used in the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is an option to show the owner hierarchy starting from the model library as the root. <em>Model Library<strong> </strong></em>is a package with the stereotype <em>«modelLibrary»</em> applied.</p><p>This option is called the <strong>Qualified name display style</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the <strong>Qualified name display style </strong>value</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select the <strong>Project</strong> command. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Option</strong> dialo</ac:link-body></ac:link>g opens.</li><li>In the option group list, select <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>General</strong>, and then select the <strong>Qualified name display style </strong> option.</li><li>For the option, set <strong>Model Library Relative </strong>(default value for a new project) to display the full qualified name hierarchy of the element starting from the model library as a root element. The model library itself is not displayed. The <strong>Qualified name display style</strong> property allows for having the relative path for library items used in the project. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678690 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00907: Query methods

- page_id: `169678690`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678690/Query+methods
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:16:21.370+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Query tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1353381367

1353381369

1353381366

**On this page**

1353381368

This section provides query methods that you can use to retrieve an array of elements by query pattern.

#### Retrieving an Array of Element by Query Pattern

```text

```

- Parameters : query : String (the query string as described in section Recognizable Query Patterns .
- Returns : java.util.List<Element> (a list or a collection of elements or an empty List if there is no matching element).
- The sample code to print all Use Case elements under a packagable element named foo is as follows:

```text

```

#### Getting a Single Result from Query Functions

Since a model element does not have a unique identified attribute so all methods will be returned in a Collection. Thus we will need convenient methods to get a single result from the Collection. Additional methods for getting a single result from query methods are as follows:

- first() – returns the first element from a collection.
- last() – returns the last element from a collection.
- get(int) – returns the nth element of a collection.
- unique() – returns the first element from a collection (equivalent to first).

##### Getting the First Element from a Collection

```text

```

- Returns : Element (the first element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A Sample code to print the first package is as follows:

```text

```

##### Getting the Last Element from a Collection

```text

```

- Returns : Element (the last element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print the last package is as follows:

```text

```

##### Getting the nth Element from a Collection

```text

```

- Parameters: index : int (a zero-based integer indicating which element to retrieve).
- Returns: Element (an element from a specified index of a query result collection or if the index is out of bounds, it will print a warning message to console/log and returns a null value)
- A sample code to print the third package is as follows:

```text

```

##### Getting the Unique Element from a Collection

```text

```

This method is equivalent to the one in section **Getting the First Element from a Collection**.

- Returns: Element (a unique element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print a single package is as follows:

```text

```

#### Retrieving an Array of Elements by Name

```text

```

Retrieve an element from a selected package scope whose element name exactly equals a given parameter. This method is equivalent to query pattern *[name=var].

- Parameters : name : String (the element name string).
- Returns : java.util.List<Element> (a List or a collection of elements or an empty List if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print the first element whose name exactly equals to foo is as follows:

```text

```

#### Retrieving Elements by ID

```text

```

Retrieve an element from a selected package scope whose element ID exactly equals a given parameter. This method is equivalent to query pattern #id.

- Parameters : id : String (an element’s ID).
- Returns : Element (an element or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print an element that contains ID 17_0_3_8e9027a_1325220298609_477646 is as follows:

```text

```

from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).</li><li><strong>A Sample code</strong> to print the first package is as follows:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa74d383-bc48-404a-bd01-f05b56878467"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("package").first()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting the Last Element from a Collection</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65d803ef-c889-4eed-aace-605c8f4f15d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get(query).last()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><strong>Returns</strong>: Element (the last element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).</li><li><strong>A sample code</strong> to print the last package is as follows:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="113b12f1-d695-40f7-87f9-f5320e8d2be5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("package").last()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting the nth Element from a Collection</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2477da4-e000-4cd0-9a79-45856251d03b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get(query).get(index)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Parameters: index : int (a zero-based integer indicating which element to retrieve).</li><li>Returns: Element (an element from a specified index of a query result collection or if the index is out of bounds, it will print a warning message to console/log and returns a null value)</li><li>A sample code to print the third package is as follows:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b0151d4-9f34-458a-af9a-6f4add7d2062"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("package").get(2)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting the Unique Element from a Collection</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2163dccc-e8d6-478d-b002-c85a893f6447"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get(query).unique()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This method is equivalent to the one in section <strong>Getting the First Element from a Collection</strong>.</p><ul><li>Returns: Element (a unique element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).</li><li>A sample code to print a single package is as follows:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc1a02dd-bc2a-46af-92d2-72fdcd219d5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("package").unique()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Retrieving an Array of Elements by Name</h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19a9a36c-a395-4597-95fb-566ffa12262c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.getElementsByName(name)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Retrieve an element from a selected package scope whose element name exactly equals a given parameter. This method is equivalent to query pattern *[name=var].</p><ul><li><strong>Parameters</strong>: <em>name</em> : String (the element name string).</li><li><strong>Returns</strong>: <em>java.util.List&lt;Element&gt;</em> (a List or a collection of elements or an empty List if there is no matching element).</li><li><strong>A sample code</strong> to print the first element whose name exactly equals to <em>foo</em> is as follows:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7be0a5cd-d323-4290-ae16-2a950a317599"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.getElementsByName("foo").unique()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Retrieving Elements by ID</h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7837151e-3906-4a7c-8498-8bec199c17ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.getElementById(id)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Retrieve an element from a selected package scope whose element ID exactly equals a given parameter. This method is equivalent to query pattern #id.</p><ul><li><strong>Parameters</strong>: id : String (an element’s ID).</li><li><strong>Returns</strong>: Element (an element or a null value if there is no matching element).</li><li><strong>A sample code</strong> to print an element that contains ID 17_0_3_8e9027a_1325220298609_477646 is as follows:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439aa58a-b745-4fd2-be04-d5d20ebc5fd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.getElementById("17_0_3_8e9027a_1325220298609_477646")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00908: Query tool

1353003242

1353003243

1353003243

The Query tool provides extra functions to retrieve elements by using a query language. Like other Custom tools, the Query tool '*querytool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tool' space='MD2024xR1']*.

Type the following code in a template to import the Query tool.

```text

```

## PAGE 00909: Query tool API

- page_id: `169678687`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678687/Query+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T05:40:37.673+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Query tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Query Tool Class diagram.

**Class QueryTool**

**Constructor**

- 
  - QueryTool() Creates the Query tool from your selected element scope. This method will use a default scope from the variable $elements.
  - QueryTool( scope : Collection<Element>) Creates the Query tool from a specific element scope.

**Methods**

- 
  - +get( query :String) : QueryList
  - +getElementsByName( name : String) : QueryList
  - +getElementById( name : String) : Element

**Class QueryList**

**Methods**

- 
  - +get(index : int) : Element
  - +first() : Element
  - +last() : Element
  - +unique () : Element

This method will use a default scope from the variable $elements.</li><li>QueryTool( scope : Collection&lt;Element&gt;) <br />Creates the Query tool from a specific element scope.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Methods</strong></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>+get( query :String) : QueryList</li><li>+getElementsByName( name : String) : QueryList </li><li>+getElementById( name : String) : Element</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Class QueryList</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Methods</strong></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>+get(index : int) : Element</li><li>+first() : Element </li><li>+last() : Element </li><li>+unique () : Element</li></ul></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675652 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00910: Quick filter

- page_id: `169675652`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675652/Quick+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:15:48.420+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Specification window
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1655259839

1655259841

1655259840

To find a property, just start to type the desired property name in the open dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Quick filter box appears after starting to type the name you are searching for.

The quick filter box is displayed only when ten or more properties are displayed in the property list.

The quick filter allows you to quickly find the required property in the property list. This is especially handy with a long properties list. Properties can be filtered by the keyword entered in this field.

You can also use the quick filter in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR1'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] dialogs in the Specification window as well.

This is especially handy with a long properties list. Properties can be filtered by the keyword entered in this field.</p><p>You can also use the quick filter in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialogs in the Specification window as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673599 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00911: Quick Find dialog

- page_id: `169673599`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673599/Quick+Find+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:50.251+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can use a quick search to find an element by its name. A very simple and easy to use dialog is designed to perform a quick search. In this dialog, you just need to type a name of the element you are searching for and select, if it's a type, diagram, or element. 
 
 To open the **Quick Find** dialog, do one of the following

- On the toolbar of the Containment , Diagrams , Inheritance , or Model Extensions tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_dialog_.png']
- On the main menu, click Edit > Quick Find .
- Press Ctrl + Alt + F.
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] button and then select Quick Find . [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_dialog_updated.png']

Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree on the Model Browser as well as on the active diagram, if the element has symbols on the active diagram.

The following table provides descriptions of the **Quick Find** dialog elements.

| Dialog element name/image | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search for | Options group | Click Type to search only in types, that is, classes, interfaces, use cases, and others.Click Diagram to search only in diagrams.Click Any Element to search in all elements of your project. |
| Search By Name | Text box with a drop-down list | Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.You may use wildcard characters (*,?) in your search phrase:"*" substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is "*dd", all the elements containing "dd" in their names can be found. For example, "Address" and "ThreadDeath"."?" substitutes exactly one character. So if your search phrase is "?dd", only the elements with "dd" in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, "Address". "ThreadDeath" cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase "dd".Be sure the Match Text Anywhere command is unchecked when using wildcard characters! |
|  | Menu button | Click the button and customize search options. Match Text AnywhereTo search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel CaseTo search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, "and" or "AND" to find "Analysis and Design".Type skipping spacers, for example, "cv3" to find "CV-3".Perform partial search, for example, "ibd" to find SysML Internal Block diagram. Be sure the Match Text Anywhere command is checked! |
| More | Menu button | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| PreviousNext | Menu button | Available in the Tree view only.Click to navigate to a previous or next result.Alternatively, you can press the Shift + F3 keyboard shortcut to select the previous match or press F3 to select the next match. |
| Switch to Tree | Menu button | Click to switch from the List to Tree view.Alternatively, press the Alt+B keyboard shortcut. |
| Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space) | Check box | Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check-box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Menu button | Click the button and customize search scope filter options. Be sure the Apply Filter check box is selected!Hide Uncommon ElementsReduces the search results list by hiding uncommon elements, keeping elements such as Interface, Association Class, Class, and Component in the list. Exclude Auxiliary Resources Reduces the search results list by excluding elements marked as auxiliary. By default, elements in the standard/system profiles are marked as auxiliary. This option does not hide the following:UML datatypes (placed under MagicDraw Profile) and UML2 Metamodel (placed under UML Standard profile) only for UML project.UML2 Metamodel, SysML value types, and stereotypes for SysML project.Elements with <<ignoreAuxFilter>> stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. How to apply stereotype >> |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce16c479-567a-4271-9351-cbb5979bd484"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use a quick search to find an element by its name. A very simple and easy to use dialog is designed to perform a quick search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree on the Model Browser as well as on the active diagram, if the element has symbols on the active diagram.</p><p>The following table provides descriptions of the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog elements.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 10.5028%;" /><col style="width: 8.65922%;" /><col style="width: 80.838%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Dialog element name/image</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Dialog element type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Search for</strong></p></td><td><p>Options group</p></td><td><ul><li>Click <strong>Type</strong> to search only in types, that is, classes, interfaces, use cases, and others.</li><li>Click <strong>Diagram</strong> to search only in diagrams.</li><li>Click <strong>Any Element</strong> to search in all elements of your project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Search By Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Text box with a drop-down list</p></td><td><p>Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.</p><p>You may use wildcard characters (*,?) in your search phrase:</p><ul><li>&quot;*&quot; substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is &quot;*dd&quot;, all the elements containing &quot;dd&quot; in their names can be found. For example, &quot;Address&quot; and &quot;ThreadDeath&quot;.</li><li>&quot;?&quot; substitutes exactly one character. For example, &quot;Address&quot;. &quot;ThreadDeath&quot; cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase &quot;dd&quot;.</li></ul><p>Be sure the <strong>Match Text</strong><strong> Anywhere</strong> command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Menu button</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button and customize search options. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Match Text</strong> <strong>Anywhere</strong></p><ul><li>To search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.</li><li>To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Use Camel Case</strong></p><ul><li><p>To search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.</p></li><li><p>To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.</p></li></ul><p>You can use camel case as follows:</p><ul><li>Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, &quot;and&quot; or &quot;AND&quot; to find &quot;Analysis and Design&quot;.</li><li>Type skipping spacers, for example, &quot;cv3&quot; to find &quot;CV-3&quot;.</li><li>Perform partial search, for example, &quot;ibd&quot; to find SysML Internal Block diagram. Be sure the <strong>Match Text </strong><strong>Anywhere</strong> command is checked!</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>More</strong></td><td colspan="1">Menu button</td><td colspan="1">Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Previous<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="previous_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p><strong>Next<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Menu button</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaf58b3c-09ab-4f0e-ad1b-2002317adc8d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available in the <strong>Tree</strong> view only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click to navigate to a previous or next result.</p><p>Alternatively, you can press the Shift + F3 keyboard shortcut to select the previous match or press F3 to select the next match.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Switch to Tree</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="switch_to_tree_icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Menu button</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to switch from the <strong>List</strong> to<strong> Tree </strong>view.</p><p>Alternatively, press the Alt+B keyboard shortcut.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space)</strong></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check-box once, your selection is remembered for the next time.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Filter Options</strong> <ac:image ac:title="Filter Options" ac:alt="Filter Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Menu button</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button and customize search scope filter options. Be sure the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box is selected!</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_filter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p><strong>Hide Uncommon Elements</strong><br /><span>Reduces the search results list by hiding uncommon elements, keeping elements such as Interface, Association Class, Class, and Component in the list.</span></p><p><br /> <strong>Exclude Auxiliary Resources</strong><br /> R<span>educes the search results list by excluding elements marked as auxiliary. By default, elements in the standard/system profiles are marked as auxiliary.</span><span> This option does not hide the following:</span></p><ul><li>UML datatypes (placed under MagicDraw Profile) and UML2 Metamodel (placed under UML Standard profile) only for UML project.</li><li>UML2 Metamodel,  SysML value types, and stereotypes for SysML project.</li><li>Elements with &lt;&lt;<strong>ignoreAuxFilter</strong>&gt;&gt; stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype">How to apply stereotype &gt;&gt;</a></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

1412562694

1412562696

1412562695

The **Quick properties panel** has three tabs, as shown in the figure below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Quick properties panel

- The**Zoom** tab zooms in on the current diagram.
- The**Documentation** tab shows documentation associated with the selected item.
- The**Properties** tab shows the specification and symbol properties of the selected element or diagram.

## PAGE 00913: Quickly navigating through conflicting changes

- page_id: `169676071`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676071/Quickly+navigating+through+conflicting+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-01-28T13:47:37.999+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['conflicting-changes', 'merged-result']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1981192965

INLINE

1981192968

INLINE

1981192967

INLINE

You can quickly navigate through the conflicting changes in the **Merged Result** tree/list. Use the filter on the right of the **Merged Result** tree/list to filter conflicting changes by type. You can select to filter conflicting changes resolved by the user or resolved automatically. Moreover, click conflicting changes notification and it will filter conflicting changes resolved automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1981192964

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675366 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00914: Realization

- page_id: `169675366`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675366/Realization
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:09.614+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Realization Types

852209378

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Realization Types

852209380

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Realization Types

852209379

The Realization is a specialized [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space='MD2024xR1'] relationship between two sets of Classes, one represents a specification (the supplier), and the other represents an implementation of the latter (the client). The Realization can be used to model stepwise refinement, optimizations, transformations, templates, model synthesis, framework composition, and so forth.

The Realization relationship is drawn as a dashed line with a solid triangular arrowhead (a “dashed generalization symbol”). The client (the one at the tail of the arrow) supports at least all of the operations defined in the supplier (the one at the arrowhead), but not necessarily the data structure of the supplier ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MD2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='CBMP2024xR1']).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about working with symbols, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1'].

The Realization paths can be grouped in a tree. This feature makes the appearance of the diagram more structural and understandable.

#### NOTE: Realization Types

Realization Types

In our modeling tool, you will find three kinds of Realization relationships:

- **Interface Realization.** A dashed line with a solid triangular arrowhead. An Interface Realization is a specialized Realization relationship between a Classifier and an Interface. This relationship signifies that the realizing classifier conforms to the contract specified by the Interface.
- **Realization.** A solid line that represents a relationship between a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers' space='MD2024xR1'] and an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024xR1'].
- **Substitution.** A dashed line with an arrowhead and *«substitute»* stereotype. A Substitution is a relationship between two classifiers. It signifies that the Substituting classifier complies with the contract specified by the contract classifier. This implies that instances of the Substituting classifier are runtime substitutable where instances of the contract classifier are expected.

To specify the selected realization path in the Specification window

- Double-click the path.
- Select Specification from the path's shortcut menu.
- Select the path and press Enter .

Creating the realizing classifiers

- The realizing classifiers are a set of Realizations owned by the Component. The Realizations reference the Classifiers of which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024xR1'] is an abstraction (i.e., that realize its behavior).

To create a Realization relationship between a component and a classifier:

1. Drag the classifier shape to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024xR1'] shape.
2. Select a classifier or Component and select Related Elements from its shortcut menu, then select the Display Paths command.
3. The Realization relationship will be displayed on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

852209376

**Related pages**

- Activity diagram
- Class diagram
- Action
- Package
- Associations
- Model Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Realization can be used to model stepwise refinement, optimizations, transformations, templates, model synthesis, framework composition, and so forth.</p><p>The Realization relationship is drawn as a dashed line with a solid triangular arrowhead (a “dashed generalization symbol”). The client (the one at the tail of the arrow) supports at least all of the operations defined in the supplier (the one at the arrowhead), but not necessarily the data structure of the supplier (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CBMP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Associations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="96"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realization_relationship.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02202a08-01b8-4664-b9d1-3104c6e4ecc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with symbols, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Realization paths can be grouped in a tree. An Interface Realization is a specialized Realization relationship between a Classifier and an Interface. This relationship signifies that the realizing classifier conforms to the contract specified by the Interface. </p></li><li><p><strong>Realization.</strong> A solid line that represents a relationship between a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classifier]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p></li><li><p><strong>Substitution.</strong> A dashed line with an arrowhead and <em>«substitute»</em> stereotype. A Substitution is a relationship between two classifiers. It signifies that the Substituting classifier complies with the contract specified by the contract classifier. This implies that instances of the Substituting classifier are runtime substitutable where instances of the contract classifier are expected. </p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><p>To specify the selected realization path in the Specification window</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the path.</li><li>Select <strong>Specification</strong> from the path's shortcut menu.</li><li>Select the path and press <strong>Enter</strong>. </li></ul><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>Creating the realizing classifiers</span></p><hr /><ul><li>The realizing classifiers are a set of Realizations owned by the Component. The Realizations reference the Classifiers of which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Component" /></ac:link> is an abstraction (i.e., that realize its behavior).</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a Realization relationship between a component and a classifier: </p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the classifier shape to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Component" /></ac:link> shape.</li><li>Select a classifier or Component and select <strong>Related Elements</strong> from its shortcut menu, then select the <strong>Display Paths</strong> command.</li><li>The Realization relationship will be displayed on the diagram pane. </li></ol><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="140"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realization_component_classifier.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed516806-136c-4456-bee4-294e5784bb83"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">852209376</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Activity+diagram">Activity diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Class+diagram">Class diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Action">Action</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Package">Package</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Association">Associations</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677096 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00915: Realization Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `169677096`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677096/Realization+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-08T10:19:23.617+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Realization Diagram Wizard** shows a table of the largest element groups that realize an interface.

To create a diagram using the **Realization Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Realization Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Realization Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Realization Diagram Wizard and click **Start** .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

What you should know when selecting Implementations:

- The **Add all implementations into one diagram** check box creates only one diagram for all selected realizations. This option is available only if the selected realizations can be added into one diagram (the same diagram type).
- The **Show outside specializations** check box shows realizations when derived interfaces are in the scope, but specializations are outside the scope.
- The **Show outside implementations** check box counts outside derived elements from displayed realizations. Otherwise, the realization will not be fully displayed and the diagram will not be valid.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec751953-0d12-400d-906d-0bb0608c6ef0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The <strong>Realization Diagram Wizard</strong> shows a table of the largest element groups that realize an interface.</p><p> </p><p>To create a diagram using the <strong>Realization Diagram Wizard </strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Realization Diagram Wizard</strong><strong> </strong>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Realization</strong><strong> Diagram Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the <strong>Realization</strong><strong> Diagram Wizard</strong> and click <strong><strong>Start</strong></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.</li></ol><p> </p><p>What you should know when selecting Implementations:</p><ul><li><p>The <strong>Add all implementations into one diagram</strong> check box creates only one diagram for all selected realizations. This option is available only if the selected realizations can be added into one diagram (the same diagram type).</p></li><li><p>The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Show outside specializations</strong> check box shows realizations when derived interfaces are in the scope, but specializations are outside the scope.</p></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">The <strong>Show outside implementations</strong> check box counts outside derived elements from displayed realizations. </span>Otherwise, the realization will not be fully displayed and the diagram will not be valid.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676979 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00916: Realized Information Flow has no Conveyed Information

- page_id: `169676979`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676979/Realized+Information+Flow+has+no+Conveyed+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-18T17:00:34.010+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

RIFHNCI[1]

**Description**

This validation rule checks if there are any realized Information Flows without Conveyed Information.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Connector, Association, ActivityEdge, Message

**Solvers**

- **Select Conveyed Information** - opens the Select Conveyed Information dialog where you can choose the Conveyed Information for the validated Information Flow.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Information Flow has no Conveyed Information. The error is resolved by specifying Conveyed Information via the **Select Conveyed Information**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">RIFHNCI</span><span class="error" style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">[1]</span></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">there are any r</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ealized Information Flows without Conveyed Information.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">warning</span></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Connector, Association, ActivityEdge, Message</span></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Select Conveyed Information</strong> - opens the Select Conveyed Information dialog where you can choose the Conveyed Information for the validated Information Flow.</span></li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Realized_Information_Flow_has_no_Conveyed_Information.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Realized Information Flow has no Conveyed Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An Information Flow has no Conveyed Information. The error is resolved by specifying Conveyed Information via the <strong>Select Conveyed Information </strong>solver.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674007 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00917: Rearranging rows and columns

- page_id: `169674007`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674007/Rearranging+rows+and+columns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-21T09:22:11.547+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can sort both rows and columns either in ascending or descending order (see the following figure) or create your own order by shifting selected items (either grouped or non-grouped) from one place to another. The sort order persists after changing the row or column owner's display mode, which can be defined by the **Row Owner Display Mode** or **Column Owner Display Mode** property in the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To shift rows up or down

1. Select rows you need to move. To select*nonadjacent rows*, select a single row, and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other rows that you want to select.To select *adjacent rows*, select a single row, and then holddown the Shift key while you click other rows that you want toselect
2. Do either:

- Right-click the selected rows and on the shortcut menu, click Up or Down . [ATTACHMENT filename='Dep_matrix_shift_down.png']
- Press Ctrl+Up Arrow or Ctrl+Down Arrow. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dep_matrix_ctrl_arrow_keys_2.png']

After the manual rearrangement of rows, the sort order of the rows automatically changes to **Custom**.

To shift columns left or right

1. Select columns you need to move. To select*nonadjacent columns*, select a single column, and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other columns that you want to select.To select *adjacent columns*, select a single column, and then hold down the Shift key while you click other columns that you want to select
2. Do either:

- Right-click the selected columns and on the shortcut menu, click Left or Right . [ATTACHMENT filename='Dep_matrix_shift_right.png']
- Press Ctrl+Left Arrow or Ctrl+Right Arrow. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the manual rearrangement of columns, the sort order of the columns automatically changes to **Custom**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675369 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00918: Reception

- page_id: `169675369`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675369/Reception
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:09.841+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Signal Reception

847553109

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Signal Reception

847553112

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Signal Reception

847553110

Signal Receptions can be specified for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can specify Reception properties in the Reception [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. You can find the description of each property in the same window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

To create a new reception

Do one of the following:

- In the Class's Specification window, click the Receptions property group and then click the Create button. Select the signal and click OK . The Reception's Specification window opens.
- In the Containment tree, right-click a Class and from its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024xR1'] , select Create Element and then Reception . Select the signal and click OK . Type the name for the new signal reception.
- On the diagram pane, click the Class, and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element.png'] Create Element smart manipulator. At the bottom of the menu, click the arrow to expand the menu and then click the Reception command. Select the signal and click OK.
- On the diagram pane, click the Class, then press the Ctrl+Alt+R shortcut keys. Then, select the signal and click OK .
- On the diagram pane, click the Class shape, and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='create_signal_reception.png'] Create Reception smart manipulator.

#### NOTE: Signal Reception

Signal Reception

Note that the Reception compartment is suppressed by default.

- Drag the signal from the Containment tree or the diagram to the Class shape on the diagram pane.

To set the signal for a signal reception

Do one of the following:

- Create a Reception. The Select Signal dialog opens. Select an existing or create a new signal for the signal reception.
- In the Reception's Specification window, select a signal in the Signal drop-down menu. You can also click the “..." button. In the Select Element dialog, select a signal or click the Create button to create a new one.

To display signal reception on shape and to customize a displayed list

1. On the diagram pane, click on a symbol, then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='compartments.png'] Compartments button.
2. On the menu, click to select the Receptions check box. The Receptions compartment is displayed on the shape.
3. On the diagram pane, click a shape, then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='compartments.png'] Compartments button. On the menu, click the Edit Compartments command.
4. In the Compartment Edit dialog, click the Receptions tab.
5. Move the receptions from the Hidden to the Selected list or vice versa.

To change the order of the signal receptions

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the shape. From its shortcut menu, select the Symbol Properties command.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, under the Receptions group, click to change the Receptions Sort Mode property.

847553108
