<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678689 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00919: Recognizable query patterns

- page_id: `169678689`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678689/Recognizable+query+patterns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:23:09.254+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Query tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Query patterns are described as a CSS3 selector pattern (*[http://www.w3.org/TR/css3-selectors](http://www.w3.org/TR/css3-selectors)*). The following table shows all available query patterns of the query tool.

| Pattern | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| E | An element of type E. |
| E[foo] | An E element with a “foo” attribute. |
| E[foo=bar] | An E element whose “foo” attribute value exactly equals “bar”. |
| E[foo^=bar] | An E element whose “foo” attribute value begins exactly with the string “bar”. |
| E[foo$=bar] | An E element whose “foo” attribute value ends exactly with the string “bar”. |
| E[foo*=bar] | An E element whose “foo” attribute value contains the substring string “bar”. |
| E:empty | An E element that has no children. |
| #myid | An element with an ID equals “myid”. |
| E F | An F element descendant of an E element. |
| E > F | An F element child of an E element. |

#### Query by Type

A pattern allows a template to query elements from a given type name, for example:

```text

```

A Type name is generated from the $element.elementType property.

#### Query by Attribute

A pattern allows a template to query elements from given attribute name and value. When a pattern is used as an expression to match an element, the attribute pattern must be considered to match the element and that element must have an attribute that matches the attribute represented by the attribute pattern.

[attr] 
 Matches an element with an attr attribute regardless of the value of the attribute. 
[attr=value] 
 Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value is exactly val.

The Query tool matches against case sensitivity of attribute names and values in a pattern. A *VTL* property syntax (Camel case without space) will be used for the attribute names. You can use spaces in writing values.

When the value of an attribute is NamedElement, the element’s name will be used as a default value. An asterisk (*) can be used to match any element type when combined with the other patterns, for example:

**[name=foo]* 
Retrieve any element whose name attribute is exactly foo. Use an asterisk to match any element.

*actor[name=foo]* 
Retrieve all actor elements whose name attribute is exactly foo.

*actor[name=]* 
Retrieve all actor elements whose name attribute is empty.

*class[owner=foo]* 
Retrieve class elements whose owner attribute name is exactly foo.

*class[owner=foo][name=bar]* 
Retrieve class elements whose owner attribute name is exactly foo and name attribute is exactly bar.

#### Query by Substring Matching Attribute

This topic provides some additional attribute patterns for matching substrings in the value of an attribute: 
 
**[attr^=val]** 
Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value begins with the prefix val. If val is an empty string, the query returns nothing.

**[attr$=val]** 
Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value ends with the suffix val. If val is an empty string, the query returns nothing.

**[attr*=val]** 
Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value contains at least one instance of the substring val. If val is an empty string, the query returns nothing.

The Query tool matches against case sensitivity of attribute names and values in a pattern. A *VTL* property syntax (Camel case without space) will be used for attribute name. You can use spaces in writing values.

When value of attribute is NamedElement, the element name will be used as default value, for example:

```text

```

#### Query Element without Children

You can use :empty to query an element that has no children at all. If an element does not any children, its “Owned Element” attribute value has a zero size of items, for example:

```text

```

#### Query by Element ID

A pattern allows a template to query elements from a given element’s ID. An element’s ID is unique in a project, for example:

```text

```

An element’s ID is generated from the $element.elementID property.

#### Query by Descendent

A pattern allows a template to query an element that is the descendant of another element, for example, an Actor that is contained within a Package. A descendant query is one or more white-spaces that separate(s) two sequences of patterns.

You can use an asterisk (*) to match any element type when combined with the other patterns, for example:

```text

```

#### Query by Child Element

A pattern allows a template to query elements that are the children of another element (describes a parent-child relationship between two elements). An optional white-space around a greater sign can be ignored.

You can use an asterisk (*) to match any element type when combined with the other patterns, for example:

```text
class
Retrieve all classes that are children of a package.
package foo 
 - class bar1
 - class bar2
 - class bar3
The classes bar1 and bar2 are the results.]]>
```

#### Additional Conditions for Query Patterns

When you are writing queries, be sure you observe the following conditions.

- A Query pattern cannot contain only spaces, for example:

```text

```

- An element type cannot contain [, ], :, *, >, #, and white space.
- Characters #, >, and white-space are the end statements except in an attribute name or value, for example:

```text
property") equivalent to
$query.get("class > property")
Retrieve a property element that is the child of a class element.
$query.get("class property")
Retrieve a property element that is a grandchild or a later descendant of a class element.]]>
```

An end statement is required to separate an element and its child, or grandchild, for example:

```text

```

- Characters = , ^=, $=, and *= are keywords and all strings except ] that are written next to these keywords mean the attribute values.
- Attribute names are mandatory.
- An attribute name cannot contain white-space, =, ^=, $=, *=, and ], for example:

```text

```

If an attribute value is not in any double quotes or single quotes, all strings, except ], after a keyword mean an attribute value, for example:

```text

```

- Character # must contain at least one string.
- An ID cannot contain [, : , >, #, and white-space, for example:

```text
*")
Retrieve all elements that are the children of an element ID #17_0_3_8e9027a.
$query.get("#") or 
$query.get("#[name=a]#17_0_3_8e9027a")
An invalid query pattern.]]>
```

- A : must be followed by empty and “:empty” must be followed by [, #, >, or white-space, except in an attribute, for example:

```text
class") or 
$query.get("*:empty[name*=class]")
A valid query pattern but the result of some patterns will be an empty list.
$query.get("*[name=:emptyx]")
Retrieve all elements whose name attribute is :emptyx
$query.get("*:emptyx") or 
$query.get("*:em")
An invalid query pattern.]]>
```

- If an attribute value of an element is null, it is equivalent to an empty attribute value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Query patterns are described as a CSS3 selector pattern (<em><a href="http://www.w3.org/TR/css3-selectors">http://www.w3.org/TR/css3-selectors</a></em>). The following table shows all available query patterns of the query tool.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> Pattern</th><th>Meaning</th></tr><tr><td>E </td><td>An element of type E.</td></tr><tr><td> E[foo]</td><td>An E element with a “foo” attribute.</td></tr><tr><td> E[foo=bar]</td><td>An E element whose “foo” attribute <br />value exactly equals “bar”.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E[foo^=bar] </td><td colspan="1">An E element whose “foo” attribute <br />value begins exactly with the string “bar”.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E[foo$=bar]</td><td colspan="1">An E element whose “foo” attribute <br />value ends exactly with the string “bar”.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E[foo*=bar] </td><td colspan="1">An E element whose “foo” attribute <br />value contains the substring string “bar”.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E:empty</td><td colspan="1">An E element that has no children.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">#myid</td><td colspan="1">An element with an ID equals “myid”.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E F</td><td colspan="1">An F element descendant of an E <br />element.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E &gt; F </td><td colspan="1">An F element child of an E element.</td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Query by Type</h2><p>A pattern allows a template to query elements from a given type name, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e91602c-b40f-4b51-a364-ffaab569fc6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class
Retrieve all classes from a selected package scope.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A Type name is generated from the $element.elementType property.</p><h2>Query by Attribute</h2><p>A pattern allows a template to query elements from given attribute name and value. When a pattern is used as an expression to match an element, the attribute pattern must be considered to match the element and that element must have an attribute that matches the attribute represented by the attribute pattern.</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">[attr]<br />            Matches an element with an attr attribute regardless of the value of the attribute.<br />[attr=value]<br />            Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value is exactly val.</p><p>The Query tool matches against case sensitivity of attribute names and values in a pattern. A <em>VTL</em> property syntax (Camel case without space) will be used for the attribute names. You can use spaces in writing values.</p><p>When the value of an attribute is NamedElement, the element’s name will be used as a default value. An asterisk (*) can be used to match any element type when combined with the other patterns, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="451d806c-47b0-4e3d-a9ee-cb68f9cd3ab3"><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>*[name=foo]</em><br />Retrieve any element whose name attribute is exactly foo. Use an asterisk to match any element.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>actor[name=foo]</em><br />Retrieve all actor elements whose name attribute is exactly foo.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>actor[name=]</em><br />Retrieve all actor elements whose name attribute is empty.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>class[owner=foo]</em><br />Retrieve class elements whose owner attribute name is exactly foo.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>class[owner=foo][name=bar]</em><br />Retrieve class elements whose owner attribute name is exactly foo and name attribute is exactly bar.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Query by Substring Matching Attribute</h2><p>This topic provides some additional attribute patterns for matching substrings in the value of an attribute:<br /><br /><strong>[attr^=val]</strong><br />Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value begins with the prefix val. If val is an empty string, the query returns nothing.</p><p><strong>[attr$=val]</strong><br />Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value ends with the suffix val. If val is an empty string, the query returns nothing.</p><p><strong>[attr*=val]</strong><br />Matches an element with an attr attribute whose value contains at least one instance of the substring val. If val is an empty string, the query returns nothing.</p><p>The Query tool matches against case sensitivity of attribute names and values in a pattern. A <em>VTL</em> property syntax (Camel case without space) will be used for attribute name. You can use spaces in writing values.</p><p>When value of attribute is NamedElement, the element name will be used as default value, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7744c04-d79e-4d7d-856a-29a7619ce810"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[*[name^=foo]
Retrieve any element whose name attributes begins with foo. Use an asterisk to match any element.
actor[name$=foo]
Retrieve all actor elements whose name attribute is ends with foo.
class[qualifiedName*=nomagic]
Retrieve class elements whose qualifiedName attribute contains string nomagic.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Query Element without Children</h2><p>You can use :empty to query an element that has no children at all. If an element does not any children, its “Owned Element” attribute value has a zero size of items, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2a90f32-cb44-4636-9029-d002061a3ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package:empty
Retrieve any package whose ownedElement attribute is empty.
package[name=foo]:empty
Retrieve any package whose name attribute is exactly foo and ownedElement attribute is empty.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Query by Element ID</h2><p>A pattern allows a template to query elements from a given element’s ID. An element’s ID is unique in a project, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a99c57f-81b5-4ba6-85f5-0d2bc4e31ead"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#17_0_3_8e9027a_1325220298609_477646
Retrieve an element whose ID is exactly 17_0_3_8e9027a_1325220298609_477646.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>An element’s ID is generated from the $element.elementID property.</p><h2>Query by Descendent</h2><p>A pattern allows a template to query an element that is the descendant of another element, for example, an Actor that is contained within a Package. A descendant query is one or more white-spaces that separate(s) two sequences of patterns.</p><p>You can use an asterisk (*) to match any element type when combined with the other patterns, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ce03fd4-5e43-452b-823a-a1de7288ac09"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package class
Retrieve all classes that are the descendants of a package element.
package foo 
 - class bar1
 - class bar2
 - class bar3
The classes bar1, bar2, and bar3 are the results.
package class *[name=foo]
Retrieve any element that (i) has a name value exactly foo and (ii) is inside a class that is inside a package.
package * property
Retrieve a property element that is the grandchild or a later descendant of a package element.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Query by Child Element</h2><p>A pattern allows a template to query elements that are the children of another element (describes a parent-child relationship between two elements). An optional white-space around a greater sign can be ignored.</p><p>You can use an asterisk (*) to match any element type when combined with the other patterns, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0bbb4a6-8465-4000-9318-d7302ad04820"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package > class
Retrieve all classes that are children of a package.
package foo 
 - class bar1
 - class bar2
 - class bar3
The classes bar1 and bar2 are the results.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Additional Conditions for Query Patterns</h2><p>When you are writing queries, be sure you observe the following conditions.</p><ul><li>A Query pattern cannot contain only spaces, for example:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c46a31e-b07d-45bc-a23e-cc5c14a58105"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get('') or $query.get(' ')
An invalid query pattern.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p>An element type cannot contain [, ], :, *, &gt;, #, and white space.</p></li><li><p>Characters #, &gt;, and white-space are the end statements except in an attribute name or value, for example:</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c42b48c-cd68-4748-bbfe-0de0924fd04c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("class#17_0_3_8e9027a") equivalent to 
$query.get("class #17_0_3_8e9027a")
Retrieve an element's ID 17_0_3_8e9027a that is a grandchild or a later descendant of a class element.
$query.get("class>property") equivalent to
$query.get("class > property")
Retrieve a property element that is the child of a class element.
$query.get("class property")
Retrieve a property element that is a grandchild or a later descendant of a class element.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>An end statement is required to separate an element and its child, or grandchild, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c029787f-5507-4b57-a94d-1684900590ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("class[attr] property")
Retrieve a property element that is a grandchild or a later descendant of a class element that contains an attr attribute.
$query.get("class[attr]property")
An invalid query pattern.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Characters = , ^=, $=, and *= are keywords and all strings except ] that are written next to these keywords mean the attribute values.</li><li>Attribute names are mandatory.</li><li>An attribute name cannot contain white-space, =, ^=, $=, *=, and ], for example:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e3b3c55-03dd-41ad-ac79-f4a8f116d3f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("*[ name =bean]") is equivalent to 
$query.get("*[name=bean]")
Retrieve all elements whose name attribute is bean.
$query.get("*[ element type = bean ] ")
An invalid query pattern.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If an attribute value is not in any double quotes or single quotes, all strings, except ], after a keyword mean an attribute value, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5468b916-bc5b-4166-8ff3-742169796aae"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("*[name=bean]")
 Retrieve all elements whose name attribute is bean]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Character # must contain at least one string.</li><li>An ID cannot contain [, : , &gt;, #, and white-space, for example:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="004b77e3-6132-47ee-9cac-b0862a712b5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("#17_0_3_8e9027a#17_0_3_8e9027b") is equivalent to
$query.get("#17_0_3_8e9027a #17_0_3_8e9027b")
Retrieve an element ID #17_0_3_8e9027b that is a grandchild or a later descendant of an element ID 17_0_3_8e9027a.
$query.get("#17_0_3_8e9027a > *")
Retrieve all elements that are the children of an element ID #17_0_3_8e9027a.
$query.get("#") or 
$query.get("#[name=a]#17_0_3_8e9027a")
An invalid query pattern.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>A : must be followed by empty and “:empty” must be followed by [, #, &gt;, or white-space, except in an attribute, for example:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb25b2d3-f2e9-48da-a13b-c8f7db79c49b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$query.get("*:empty") or 
$query.get("*:empty#17_0_3_8e9027a") or 
$query.get("*:empty > class") or 
$query.get("*:empty[name*=class]")
A valid query pattern but the result of some patterns will be an empty list.
$query.get("*[name=:emptyx]")
Retrieve all elements whose name attribute is :emptyx
$query.get("*:emptyx") or 
$query.get("*:em")
An invalid query pattern.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>If an attribute value of an element is null, it is equivalent to an empty attribute value.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00920: Rectangular shape

- page_id: `169675560`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675560/Rectangular+shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-27T17:13:50.897+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

491101236

491101238

491101237

You can use a rectangular shape to delineate different parts of a diagram, as seen in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a rectangular shape

You can format the rectangular shape representation properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

A text next to the separator can be displayed as plain text or HTML text. You can edit the HTML text using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024xR1'].

To change rectangular shape properties

1. From the rectangular shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties or select the rectangular shape and press Alt+Enter .
2. The Symbol Properties dialog opens. In that dialog, set particular property values, such as Fill Color , Line Style , Line Width and other, as you need.
3. Click Close , when you are done.

491101235

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

You can edit the HTML text using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change rectangular shape properties</p><hr /><ol><li>From the rectangular shape's shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties </strong>or select the rectangular shape and press <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="280592b1-88d3-4a1a-86b7-15464adba4da">Alt+Enter</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>The <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog opens. ## PAGE 00921: Recursive properties

- page_id: `169679225`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679225/Recursive+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:30.160+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1852081948

1852081950

1852081949

A recursive property is defined as a binary class “*com.nomagic.magicdraw.derivedproperty.RecursiveExpression()*” with parameters*<property-name>* and 
*<recursive-property-name>*.

Specify the expression in the following form *- com.nomagic.magicdraw.derivedproperty.RecursiveExpression(*<property-name>*, *<recursive-property-name>** (e.g.*com.nomagic.magicdraw.derivedproperty.RecursiveExpression(Classifier.specificClassifier, Classifier.allSpecificClassifiers)*

Recursive properties enable you to collect and represent:

- All elements directly or indirectly related to a specific element through the generalization relationships. Refer to the derived property customization expression, available at "UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::Traceability customization::Properties descriptors::Other::All Specific Classifiers::allSpecificClassifiers".

- All elements directly or indirectly related to a specific element through the abstraction and other types of relations. Refer to the derived property customization expression, available at "UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::Traceability customization::Properties descriptors::Realization::Element All Realizing Elements::allRealizingElements".

1852081947

**Related Pages**

- Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- Using Customization Data
- Creating Customization Data

## PAGE 00922: Redefined symbols

- page_id: `169673104`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673104/Redefined+symbols

- ancestors: UML correctness rules (in progress)

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

PortRdf

**Description**

This validation rule checks if

Inherited Port is redefined!

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Port

**Solvers**

- To fix this,

**Example**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>PortRdf</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if </p><p>Inherited Port is redefined!</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Port</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, </li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675686 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00923: Redefinition

- page_id: `169675686`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675686/Redefinition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-12T07:08:05.908+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Any inherited part can now be quickly redefined directly in the composite structure diagrams. A new part redefining the original one is created and graphically replaced in the diagram with all ports and connectors remaining in their places.

To redefine an inherited part

1. Right-click a part and choose Refactor > Redefine To . The Select Type dialog opens.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Type a name, if you want to create a new type.
  - Select one of the existing types.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Any inherited part can now be quickly redefined directly in the composite structure diagrams. A new part redefining the original one is created and graphically replaced in the diagram with all ports and connectors remaining in their places.</p><p>To redefine an inherited part</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a part and choose <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Redefine To</strong>. The <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Type a name, if you want to create a new type.</li><li>Select one of the existing types.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673763 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00924: Refactored OCL2.0 validation rules

- page_id: `169673763`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673763/Refactored+OCL2.0+validation+rules
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-21T07:59:20.851+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Multiple validation rules across several profiles have been refactored from OCL2.0 scripting language to Java for the 2022x Refresh2 version of the modeling tool. Because of this, the refactored rules do not work with server projects committed with the 2022x Refresh2 version and opened with either the 2022x or 2022x Refresh1 version of the tool (i.e., even if there is an error in the project violating an affected validation rule, you will receive a successful validation notification).

To resolve this, you can:

- Download the [ATTACHMENT filename='OCL2.0 Validation Rules.mdzip'] file and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2022x'] it in or copy it to your project to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='MD2024xR1'] it. The attached project contains the "OCL2.0 Validation Rules [2022x-2022x Refresh1]" package, which includes all of the affected validation rules in OCL2.0 language.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom validation' space='MD2024xR1'] needed OCL2.0-based validation rules using the scripts provided in the table below.

See the table below of the refactored validation rules:

| Name | Severity | Profile | Constrained Element | OCL2.0 Script |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Mandatory Tagged Value | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Mandatory Tagged Value | Element | self.appliedStereotype->forAll(c\| c.member->forAll(f\|f.oclIsKindOf(Property) implies (let sf:Property = f.oclAsType(Property) in (not sf.lowerValue.oclIsUndefined()) and (sf.lower >= 1) and (sf.opposite.oclIsUndefined() or not sf.opposite.oclIsKindOf(ExtensionEnd)) implies self.taggedValue->exists(tv\|tv.tagDefinition=sf and tv.value()->size()>=sf.lower) ) )) |
| Multiple Tagged Values | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Multiple Tagged Values | TaggedValue | self.tagDefinition.owner.oclIsTypeOf(Stereotype) and (self.tagDefinition.upper = 1) implies (self.value()->size()<=1) |
| Tags multiplicity 2 | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Tags multiplicity 2 | TaggedValue | self.tagDefinition.owner.oclIsTypeOf(Stereotype) implies (self.tagDefinition.lower <= self.value()->size()) |
| Slot multiplicity 2 | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Slot multiplicity 2 | Slot | let A:Association=self.definingFeature.oclAsType(Property).association in (A.oclIsUndefined() or not self.owningInstance.classifier->includes(A)) andnot self.definingFeature.owner.oclIsTypeOf(Stereotype) implies(self.definingFeature.upper=-1) or (self.definingFeature.lower <= self.value->size()) |
| Multiple Slot Values | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Multiple Slot Values | Slot | (not self.definingFeature.owner.oclIsTypeOf(Stereotype)) and (self.definingFeature.upper = 1) implies (self.value->size()<=1) |
| Link slot multiplicity | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Link slot multiplicity | Slot | let A:Association=self.definingFeature.oclAsType(Property).association in (not A.oclIsUndefined() and self.owningInstance.classifier->includes(A)) impliesself.value->size()=1 |
| No Activity Incoming Edge | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Activity Incoming Edge | ActivityNode | ((not appliedStereotype->exists(name='startEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='messageStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='ruleStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='multipleStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='timerStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='linkStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='intermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='multipleIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='cancelIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='compensationIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='compensationIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='messageIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='ruleIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='errorIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='timerIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='gateway'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='eventBasedXOR'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='aND'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='oR'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='complex'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='dataBasedXOR'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='endEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='cancelEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='linkEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='messageEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='compensationEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='terminateEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='errorEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='task'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='sendTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='scriptTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='userTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='referenceTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='receiveTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='serviceTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='manualTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='usddsderTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='multiInstanceLoop'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='standardLoop'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='subProcess'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='collapsedSubProcess'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='adHoc'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='compensation'))and not (self.oclIsKindOf(InitialNode) or self.oclIsKindOf(ActivityParameterNode) or self.oclIsKindOf(AcceptEventAction) or self.oclIsKindOf(OutputPin) or self.oclIsKindOf(SendSignalAction))) implies (incoming->notEmpty() or self.oclIsKindOf(Action) and self.oclAsType(Action).input->notEmpty() or self.oclIsKindOf(ObjectNode) and self.oclAsType(ObjectNode)._exceptionHandlerOfExceptionInput->notEmpty()) |
| No Activity Outgoing Edge | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Activity Outgoing Edge | ActivityNode | ((not appliedStereotype->exists(name='startEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='messageStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='ruleStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='multipleStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='timerStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='linkStartEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='intermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='multipleIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='cancelIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='timerIntermediateEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='gateway'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='eventBasedXOR'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='aND'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='oR'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='complex'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='dataBasedXOR'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='endEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='cancelEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='linkEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='messageEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='compensationEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='terminateEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='errorEndEvent'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='task'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='sendTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='scriptTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='userTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='referenceTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='receiveTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='serviceTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='manualTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='usddsderTask'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='multiInstanceLoop'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='standardLoop'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='subProcess'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='collapsedSubProcess'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='adHoc'))and (not appliedStereotype->exists(name='compensation'))and not (self.oclIsKindOf(FinalNode) or self.oclIsKindOf(ActivityParameterNode) or self.oclIsKindOf(InputPin) or self.oclIsKindOf(SendSignalAction) or self.oclIsKindOf(AcceptEventAction))) implies (outgoing->notEmpty() or self.oclIsKindOf(ExecutableNode) and self.oclAsType(ExecutableNode).handler->notEmpty() or self.oclIsKindOf(Action) and self.oclAsType(Action).output->notEmpty()) |
| Lower Multiplicity Value to High | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Lower Multiplicity Value to High | MultiplicityElement | ((not lowerValue.oclIsUndefined()) and (not upperValue.oclIsUndefined())) implies ((lower <= upper) or (upper = -1)) |
| Tag type restrictions | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Tag type restrictions | Property | (self.oclIsTypeOf(Property) and not type.oclIsUndefined() and type.oclIsKindOf(Stereotype)) implies (self.aggregation <> AggregationKind::composite) |
| Multiple Compositions | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Multiple Compositions | Classifier | let atr: Set(Property) = attribute->select (a \| not a.opposite.oclIsUndefined() and a.opposite.isComposite)->asSet() inatr -> exists( n \| not n.lowerValue.oclIsUndefined() and n.lower = 1) implies atr -> size() = 1 |
| Abstract Leaf | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Abstract Leaf | Classifier | isLeaf implies not isAbstract |
| No Classifier for Instance | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Classifier for Instance | InstanceSpecification | (not self.oclIsKindOf(EnumerationLiteral)) implies classifier->notEmpty() |
| Nameless Class | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Nameless Class | Class | name <> '' |
| Not Query Datatype Operation | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Not Query Datatype Operation | DataType | ownedOperation->forAll(o\|o.isQuery = true) |
| No Operation for Call Message | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Operation for Call Message | Message | self.messageSort=MessageSort::synchCall implies (not self.signature.oclIsUndefined() and self.signature.oclIsKindOf(Operation)) |
| Call from Foreign Classifier | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Call from Foreign Classifier | Message | let sourceLifeline:Lifeline = self.sendEvent.oclAsType(MessageOccurrenceSpecification).covered->any(true) inlet targetLifeline:Lifeline = self.receiveEvent.oclAsType(MessageOccurrenceSpecification).covered->any(true) in(sourceLifeline <> targetLifeline) implies(let sourceType:Type = sourceLifeline.represents.type inlet targetOperation:Operation = self.signature.oclAsType(Operation) in(not sourceType.oclIsUndefined() and not targetOperation.oclIsUndefined()) implies ((targetOperation.visibility <> VisibilityKind::private) or (targetOperation.UMLClass = sourceType))) |
| No Incoming Transitions | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Incoming Transitions | Vertex | let k : PseudostateKind = self.oclAsType(Pseudostate).kind in(((self.oclIsKindOf(State) and self.oclAsType(State).submachine.oclIsUndefined() and self.oclAsType(State).region->isEmpty()or self.oclIsKindOf(Pseudostate) or self.oclIsKindOf(ConnectionPointReference)) and (not self.oclIsKindOf(ConnectionPointReference)) and (k.oclIsUndefined() or not (k=PseudostateKind::initial or k=PseudostateKind::entryPoint or k=PseudostateKind::exitPoint))))implies incoming->notEmpty() |
| No Outgoing Transitions | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Outgoing Transitions | Vertex | ((self.oclIsKindOf(State) and self.oclAsType(State).submachine.oclIsUndefined() and self.oclAsType(State).region->isEmpty()or self.oclIsKindOf(Pseudostate) or self.oclIsKindOf(ConnectionPointReference))and (not (self.oclIsKindOf(ConnectionPointReference) or self.oclIsKindOf(FinalState) or (self.oclIsKindOf(Pseudostate) and (self.oclAsType(Pseudostate).kind=PseudostateKind::exitPoint or self.oclAsType(Pseudostate).kind=PseudostateKind::entryPoint or self.oclAsType(Pseudostate).kind=PseudostateKind::terminate or self.oclAsType(Pseudostate).kind=PseudostateKind::deepHistory or self.oclAsType(Pseudostate).kind=PseudostateKind::shallowHistory)))))implies outgoing->notEmpty() |
| Multiple Constrained Element | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Multiple Constrained Element | OpaqueExpression | (language->exists(lng:String\|lng='OCL2.0') and not owningConstraint.oclIsUndefined()) implies owningConstraint.constrainedElement->size()=1 |
| Guard on decision | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Guard on decision | ActivityEdge | source.oclIsKindOf(DecisionNode) implies not guard.oclIsUndefined() |
| Covered Lifelines | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Covered Lifelines | InteractionUse | let coveredSize : Integer = covered->size() inif refersTo.oclIsUndefined() then coveredSize = 0elserefersTo.lifeline->size() = coveredSizeendif |

| Wrong Maximum Composite Multiplicity | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Wrong Maximum Composite Multiplicity | Property | let oppose:Property = association.memberEnd->any(p\|p <> self) in(not oppose.oclIsUndefined()and oppose.isCompositeand not upperValue.oclIsUndefined())implies (upper = 1) |
| Flows on decision | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Flows on decision | DecisionNode | outgoing->size()>1 |
| No Stereotype Metaclass | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::No Stereotype Metaclass | Stereotype | self.encoded5FextensionEndOfType->notEmpty() or(self.generalization->notEmpty()) |
| Method for Abstract Operation is Defined | error | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML correctness constraints::Method for Abstract Operation is Defined | Operation | isAbstract implies method->isEmpty() |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Multiple validation rules across several profiles have been refactored from OCL2.0 scripting language to Java for the 2022x Refresh2 version of the modeling tool. Because of this, the refactored rules do not work with server projects committed with the 2022x Refresh2 version and opened with either the 2022x or 2022x Refresh1 version of the tool (i.e., even if there is an error in the project violating an affected validation rule, you will receive a successful validation notification). </p><p>To resolve this, you can:</p><ul><li>Download the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="OCL2.0 Validation Rules.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Refactored OCL2.0 validation rules" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> file and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it in or copy it to your project to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Starting the validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validate]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it. The attached project contains the &quot;OCL2.0 Validation Rules [2022x-2022x Refresh1]&quot; package, which includes all of the affected validation rules in OCL2.0 language.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Custom validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Recreate]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> needed OCL2.0-based validation rules using the scripts provided in the table below.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>See the table below of the refactored validation rules:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 98.2627%;"><colgroup> <col style="width: 9.76469%;" /> <col style="width: 6.50124%;" /> <col style="width: 7.35315%;" /> <col style="width: 10.9669%;" /> <col style="width: 65.4273%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Severity</th><th>Profile</th><th>Constrained Element</th><th colspan="1">OCL2.0 Script</th></tr><tr><td><span>Mandatory Tagged Value</span></td><td><span>warning</span></td><td><p><span>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Mandatory Tagged Value</span></p></td><td><span>Element</span></td><td colspan="1">self.appliedStereotype-&gt;forAll(c|<br />    c.member-&gt;forAll(f|f.oclIsKindOf(Property) implies<br />        (let sf:Property = f.oclAsType(Property) in<br />            (not sf.lowerValue.oclIsUndefined()) and (sf.lower &gt;= 1) and (sf.opposite.oclIsUndefined() or <br />             not sf.opposite.oclIsKindOf(ExtensionEnd)) implies self.taggedValue-&gt;exists(tv|tv.tagDefinition=sf and tv.value()-&gt;size()&gt;=sf.lower)<br />        )<br />    )<br />)</td></tr><tr><td><span>Multiple Tagged Values</span></td><td><span>warning</span></td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::UML completeness constraints::Multiple Tagged Values</td><td><span>TaggedValue</span></td><td rgb(62,63,64);">warning</span></td><td colspan="1">MD Customization for SysML::SysML constraints::SysML ValSuite::SysML ValSuite - Activities::Optional::Invalid No Optional lower multiplicity</td><td colspan="1">Optional<br />Parameter</td><td colspan="1">(not appliedStereotype-&gt;exists(name='Optional')) implies ((not lower.oclIsUndefined()) and lower &gt; 0)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Invalid SharedProperty type</span></td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td colspan="1">MD Customization for SysML::SysML constraints::SysML ValSuite::SysML ValSuite - Blocks::ShareProperty::Invalid SharedProperty type</td><td colspan="1"><span>SharedProperty</span></td><td colspan="1">self.oclIsTypeOf(MD_Customization_for_SysML::additional_stereotypes::SharedProperty) and (not self.type.oclIsUndefined()) implies self.type.oclIsKindOf(SysML::Blocks::Block)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Invalid source or target of DeriveReqt</span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">warning</span></td><td colspan="1">MD Customization for SysML::SysML constraints::SysML ValSuite::SysML ValSuite - Requirements::DeriveReqt::Invalid source or target of DeriveReqt</td><td colspan="1"><span>DeriveReqt</span></td><td colspan="1">client-&gt;forAll(oclIsKindOf(SysML::Requirements::Requirement)) and supplier-&gt;forAll(oclIsKindOf(SysML::Requirements::Requirement))</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Invalid parameter type [Behavior]</span></td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td colspan="1">MD colspan="1">self.oclIsTypeOf(SysML::Activities::ControlOperator) or<br />self.ownedParameter-&gt;forAll(type.name&lt;&gt;'ControlValueKind' or type.oclIsUndefined())</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Invalid isStream tag</span></td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td colspan="1">MD Customization for SysML::SysML constraints::SysML ValSuite::SysML ValSuite - Activities::Rate::Invalid isStream tag</td><td colspan="1"><span>Rate</span></td><td colspan="1">self.oclIsKindOf(UML_Standard_Profile::UML2_Metamodel::Parameter) implies <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675684 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00925: Refactoring

- page_id: `169675684`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675684/Refactoring
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-06-28T07:18:10.633+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Refactoring allows you to modify model elements including all related data. Within refactoring, you can convert element types, replace elements, decompose diagrams, and perform other actions.

All the refactoring commands have been moved under the Refactor command in the shortcut menu of the particular element. Now the refactoring covers the following commands:

- Convert To.
- Replace With.
- Reverse Direction.
- Reverse Flow Direction.
- Extract.
- Split Control Flow for the selected Control Flow edge.
- Split Object Flow for the selected Object Flow edge.
- Join Connector for the selected split Control Flow or Object Flow edge.
- Swap.
- Redefinition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To perform refactoring

1. Right-click an element in the Model Browser or on the diagram pane.
2. From the shortcut menu, point to Refactor and select one of available commands for the selected element.

Some of the Refactor sub-commands are available only in element shortcut menu that is opened from the diagram pane, not available in the Model Browser.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Refactoring allows you to modify model elements including all related data. Within refactoring, you can convert element types, replace elements, decompose diagrams, and perform other actions.</p><p>All the refactoring commands have been moved under the Refactor command in the shortcut menu of the particular element. Now the refactoring covers the following commands:</p><ul><li>Convert To. </li><li>Replace With.</li><li>Reverse Direction.</li><li>Reverse Flow Direction.</li><li>Extract.</li><li>Split Control Flow for the selected Control Flow edge.</li><li>Split Object Flow for the selected Object Flow edge.</li><li>Join Connector for the selected split Control Flow or Object Flow edge.</li><li>Swap.</li><li>Redefinition.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refactoring1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Refactoring" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To perform refactoring</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click an element in the Model Browser or on the diagram pane.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to Refactor and select one of available commands for the selected element.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc397fa6-126e-415f-9c4a-a922303df073"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Some of the Refactor sub-commands are available only in element shortcut menu that is opened from the diagram pane, not available in the Model Browser.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd1cb8d2-8c60-420d-8f7b-74dc292e334e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="84028b45-af34-4e71-84d4-20b86f5330d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00926: References to elements

- page_id: `169678605`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678605/References+to+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-24T12:21:59.346+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > JavaScript tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

References to elements are implicitly inserted into the JavaScript context when calling **eval()**, **execute()**, or **call()**. Examples of implicit variables include, for instance, **$Class**, **$UseCase**, **$sorter**, etc.

For example:

A "functions.js"

```text

```

A template code

```text

```

Example:

A "functions.js"

```text

```

A template code

```text

```

**Reference Documents**

- Mozilla JavaScript Tool [http://developer.mozilla.org/en/Rhino_documentation](http://developer.mozilla.org/en/Rhino_documentation)
- Sun JavaScript programming guide [http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/guides/scripting/programmer_guide/index.html](http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/guides/scripting/programmer_guide/index.html)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>References to elements are implicitly inserted into the JavaScript context when calling <strong>eval()</strong>, <strong>execute()</strong>, or <strong>call()</strong>. Examples of implicit variables include, for instance, <strong>$Class</strong>, <strong>$UseCase</strong>, <strong>$sorter</strong>, etc.</p><p>For example:</p><p>A &quot;functions.js&quot;</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7700b09-2bac-48b8-a3b2-ca6f2126bd99"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[importPackage(java.util)
// variable $Dependency and $sorter can be accessed directly inside
function getSupplier()
{
   var supplierList = new ArrayList();
	var sortedDependencyList = $sorter.sort($Dependency);
   for (var i=0; i<sortedDependencyList.size(); i++)
   {
      var dependency = sortedDependencyList.get(i);
      supplierList.addAll(dependency.getSupplier());
   }
   return supplierList;
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A template code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d62b7ad5-7b3c-47f3-b2bb-ce578f094040"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('js', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.script.JavaScriptTool')
$js.execute("functions.js")
#set ($supplierList = $js.eval("getSupplier()"))
#foreach ($supplier in $supplierList)
$supplier.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Example:</p><p>A &quot;functions.js&quot;</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74380125-b837-4916-a663-05787048685e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[var qualifiedName = "";
function packageQualifiedName(element)
{
   qualifiedName = "";
	packageName(element);
	return qualifiedName==""?"Default":qualifiedName;
}
function packageName(element)
{
	var parent = element.owner;
   if (parent.classType.simpleName == 'Package') {
	   if (qualifiedName == "")
		   qualifiedName = parent.name;
		else
	      qualifiedName = parent.name + '.' + qualifiedName;
		packageName(parent);
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A template code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9142e7ad-f442-4e7c-bd4e-9770915a13fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('js', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.script.JavaScriptTool')
$js.execute("functions.js")
#foreach ($c in $Class)
   Package: $js.eval('packageQualifiedName($c)')
	Name: $c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><strong>Reference Documents</strong></p><ul><li>Mozilla JavaScript Tool<br /><em><a href="http://developer.mozilla.org/en/Rhino_documentation" style="line-height: 1.4285715;">http://developer.mozilla.org/en/Rhino_documentation</a></em></li><li>Sun JavaScript programming guide<br /><em><a style="line-height: 1.4285715;" href="http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/guides/scripting/programmer_guide/index.html">http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/guides/scripting/programmer_guide/index.html</a></em></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678608 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00927: References to elements - Groovy

- page_id: `169678608`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678608/References+to+elements+-+Groovy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T04:37:51.802+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Groovy script tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

References to elements are similar to ones in JavaScript Tool. The elements are implicitly inserted into the Groovy context when calling “eval()”, or “execute()”. Examples of implicit variables include $Class, $UseCase, $sorter, etc.

*File 'AllAbstractClass.groovy'*

```text

```

The report template code is:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>References to elements are similar to ones in JavaScript Tool. The elements are implicitly inserted into the Groovy context when calling “eval()”, or “execute()”. Examples of implicit variables include $Class, $UseCase, $sorter, etc.</p><p><em>File 'AllAbstractClass.groovy'</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f9eeb87-cb93-4e74-8530-2708ab4cc7c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// variable $Class can be accessed directly inside Groovy script
def list = []
for (c in $Class) {
    if (c.isAbstract()) {
        list.add(c)
    }
}
return list]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The report template code is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2875f15-52e6-4f29-a28c-6a402fdd28ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('groovy', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.script.GroovyTool')
#set ($abstractClassList = $groovy.execute('AllAbstractClass.groovy'))
#foreach ($cls in $abstractClassList)
$cls.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00928: Relation Map

- page_id: `169674030`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674030/Relation+Map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:51.827+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams
- labels: ['relation-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393725676

393725679

393725677

The main purpose of all relation maps is to review and analyze relations among the elements and create new elements directly in the relation map. All modeling tools allow you to create the Relation Map Diagram, which provides you with a rapid review, analysis, and creation of relationships among the elements of the entire model. See the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of Relation Map Diagram. It is created by using the *CoffeeMachine sample model* that comes only with SysML Plugin.

You can create other predefined relation maps (Activity Decomposition, Structure Decomposition, Instance, Requirement Containment, Requirement Derivation Maps) only if you have the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR1'] installed. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined Relation Maps' space='SYSMLP2024xR1']>]]>

INLINE

The following procedures explain how to work with relation maps.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aba4ec33-dd2a-4dad-917f-75f6fd5018a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393725676</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="041523a8-3982-4331-bacc-505de5aa0c69"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393725679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1ad3c7a-a129-4ded-aaf2-6271b2ce5133"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393725677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The main purpose of all relation maps is to review and analyze relations among the elements and create new elements directly in the relation map. All modeling tools allow you to create the Relation Map Diagram, which provides you with a rapid review, analysis, and creation of relationships among the elements of the entire model. See the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Relation Map" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of Relation Map Diagram. It is created by using the <em>CoffeeMachine sample model </em> that comes only with SysML Plugin.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5079db6a-df2d-4b77-a4dd-de769b02cc52"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create other predefined relation maps (Activity Decomposition, Structure Decomposition, Instance, Requirement Containment, Requirement Derivation Maps) only if you have the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> installed. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Predefined Relation Maps" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about predefined relation maps in SysML Plugin >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d860df6c-f473-4ecf-9f6a-02f8ae1f2e0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The following procedures explain how to work with relation maps.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1e98e6fd-8026-4f8b-aad2-02b974ba6e4c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00930: Relationship is not in the same owner as its end elements

- page_id: `169673068`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673068/Relationship+is+not+in+the+same+owner+as+its+end+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-18T18:52:20.444+02:00`
- ancestors: Relations Ownership
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

RelOwn

**Description**

This validation rule checks if a relationship is not in the same owner as its end elements.

**Severity**

info

**Constrained Element**

Association, Connector, DirectedRelationship, ActivityEdge, ExceptionHandler, Transition

**Solvers**

- Move <relationship_name> to <appropriate_owner> - moves the relationship to the appropriate owner.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Association between the Blocks *Cooling* and *Compressor* is owned by the package *VCCU Parts*, which does not correspond to either of the Association's end elements, causing an error. 
The error is resolved via the **Move Association to Package Cooling** solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>RelOwn</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if a relationship is not in the same owner as its end elements.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>info</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Association, Connector, DirectedRelationship, ActivityEdge, ExceptionHandler, Transition</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Move &lt;relationship_name&gt; to &lt;appropriate_owner&gt; </strong>- moves the relationship to the appropriate owner.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Relationship is not in the same owner as its end elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Relationship is not in the same owner as its end elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Association between the Blocks <em>Cooling</em> and <em>Compressor</em> is owned by the package <em>VCCU Parts</em>, which does not correspond to either of the Association's end elements, causing an error.<br />The error is resolved via the <strong>Move Association to Package Cooling</strong> solver.  </h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673682 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00931: Reloading used projects in a project

- page_id: `169673682`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673682/Reloading+used+projects+in+a+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-20T14:30:19.169+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Managing used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The best way to access the latest changes in a used project is to reload it. All modifications made in the used project become visible after it is reloaded in the open project. Alternatively, used projects are reloaded automatically when the open project is reloaded, or the project is updated on the server.

To reload the used project

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the package of the used project.
2. From the shortcut menu, select **Project Usages** > **Reload**. If you open a used project for editing, be sure to save any changes you make. The modifications made appear after reloading the used project in the main project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The best way to access the latest changes in a used project is to reload it. All modifications made in the used project become visible after it is reloaded in the open project. Alternatively, used projects are reloaded automatically when the open project is reloaded, or the project is updated on the server.</p><p>To reload the used project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the package of the used project.</li><li><p>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Reload</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88863e70-f636-4ffc-9b27-ad2ba1b806cf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you open a used project for editing, be sure to save any changes you make. The modifications made appear after reloading the used project in the main project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674741 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00932: Removing a relationship

- page_id: `169674741`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674741/Removing+a+relationship
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-15T10:14:57.422+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['delete-relationship', 'delete-path']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a relationship from the diagram pane

1. Select a relationship and click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_from_diagram.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.

To remove a relationship from the model

1. Select a relationship and click [ATTACHMENT filename='delete.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. In the Question dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To remove a relationship from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a relationship and click <ac:image ac:title="Remove From Diagram (Delete)" ac:alt="Remove From Diagram (Delete)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_from_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To remove a relationship from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a relationship and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>In the <strong>Question</strong> dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm the deletion.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674839 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00933: Removing applied layout template from shape

- page_id: `169674839`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674839/Removing+applied+layout+template+from+shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-10-03T12:53:07.483+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can remove the applied layout template from the Part, Class, or classifier shape, if you want to see their predefined style notations.

To remove an applied layout template from a shape

- Revert the Apply Template command by using the Undo button from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'] , or press Ctrl+Z.
- Delete the shape and display it again by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR1']**Display Parts/Ports** dialog. Ensure that the Use Layout Template check box is clear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can remove the applied layout template from the Part, Class, or classifier shape, if you want to see their predefined style notations.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove an applied layout template from a shape</p><hr /><ul><li>Revert the Apply Template command by using the <strong>Undo</strong> button from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[main toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or press Ctrl+Z.</li><li>Delete the shape and display it again by using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</ac:link-body></ac:link> Ensure that the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box is clear.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679625 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00934: Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro

- page_id: `169679625`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679625/Removing+keyboard+shortcuts+from+Macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:40:57.378+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Macro keyboard shortcuts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a keyboard shortcut

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Select a keyboard shortcut from the Current keys box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The selected keyboard shortcut will be removed from the Current keys box.

You can also remove a keyboard shortcut through the **Environment Options** dialog by clicking **Options** > **Environment** > **Keyboard** on the main menu.

To remove all keyboard shortcuts

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . All keyboard shortcuts will be deleted from the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box.

- The [IMAGE alt='' src=''] , [IMAGE alt='' src=''] , and [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons in the Macro Information or Environment Options dialog will be disabled if there is no keyboard shortcut either in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box.

- The [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button will be enabled if the new keyboard shortcut entered has not been assigned to any other macro. You can assign more than one keyboard shortcut to a macro.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The selected keyboard shortcut will be removed from the <strong>Current keys</strong> box.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3724a596-2ff5-4707-99d3-8b7e2fd983df"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also remove a keyboard shortcut through the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog by clicking <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>Keyboard</strong> on the main menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove all keyboard shortcuts</p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the (i) <strong>Environment Options</strong> or (ii) <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-all-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>. All keyboard shortcuts will be deleted from the <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current-keys-area.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> box.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6fc5501-a8a8-4461-9d93-be15740a563e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>, <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>, and <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-all-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> buttons in the<strong> Macro Information</strong> or <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog will be disabled if there is no keyboard shortcut either in the<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> or <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current-keys-area.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> box.</li></ul><ul><li>The <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> button will be enabled if the new keyboard shortcut entered has not been assigned to any other macro. You can assign more than one keyboard shortcut to a macro.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00935: Removing projects

- page_id: `169677193`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677193/Removing+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:25.492+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a project

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select the project you want to remove.
3. Click **Remove**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

4. In the dialog that opens, select **Yes**. The project will be removed from Teamwork Cloud.

Data Manager

In the dialog that opens, select </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Yes</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The project will be removed from Teamwork Cloud.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a32fe1f2-e685-4ab7-96ba-8155e9178b41"><ac:rich-text-body>Removed projects are not deleted from the Teamwork Cloud database. ## PAGE 00936: Removing recovered element symbol

- page_id: `169673710`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673710/Removing+recovered+element+symbol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-06T15:02:56.252+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305696338

305696340

305696339

This is the case when a recovered element is depicted on diagrams of your project.

To remove a recovered element’s symbol, do one of the following

- On a diagram pane, select the symbol. On its smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='error_icon.png'] and then select Symbol of Recovered Element > Remove Symbol , as shown in the following figure.
- On the diagram pane, select the symbol and press Delete.
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the symbol and select Remove Symbol from its shortcut menu.

The symbol of the recovered element is removed from the diagram.

The same diagram can have many symbols of the recovered element. To remove all of them, repeat the same procedure for each symbol.

Remove Symbol

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using smart manipulator toolbar to remove recovered element’s symbol

305696337

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673709 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00937: Removing unresolved reference

- page_id: `169673709`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673709/Removing+unresolved+reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-09T07:54:51.937+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305661011

305661013

305661012

At times, a reference to some recovered element is no longer needed and can be removed.

To remove a reference to the recovered element, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Clean Usage of Recovered Element .
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is URE. From its shortcut menu select Clean Usage of Recovered Element .

The reference to the particular recovered element is removed.

You can use the command for more than one element at a time.

If some of the referring elements are non-editable, belong to the read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked, the command will skip these elements.

You can remove all unresolved references (including all used modules) of a recovered element in one operation.

To remove all references to a recovered element, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Clean Usage of Recovered Element .
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Clean Usage of Recovered Element .

All unresolved references to the particular recovered element are removed. As the unresolved references are cleared, the recovered element is removed as well.

Unresolved references will not be removed from the referring elements, if they are stored in a read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked.

305661010

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replacing recovered element with another element' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing recovered element symbol' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space='MD2024xR1']
