<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677190 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00938: Renaming projects

- page_id: `169677190`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677190/Renaming+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-08-05T13:56:49.658+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To rename a project

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select the project you want to rename.
3. Click Rename .
4. In the dialog that opens, type a new name for the project in the **Name:** field: [ATTACHMENT filename='renaming_project.png']

5. In this dialog, you can also change the project category by clicking the three dots menu [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the desired category.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To rename a project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select<span> </span> <strong>Collaborate</strong> <span> </span>&gt;<span> </span> <strong>Projects</strong>. The<span> </span> <strong>Manage Projects</strong> <span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the project you want to rename.</li><li>Click <strong>Rename</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the dialog that opens, type a new name for the project in the <strong>Name:</strong> field:</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="renaming_project.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Renaming projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>       5. In this dialog, you can also change the project category by clicking the three dots menu <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_dots.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Renaming projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and selecting the desired category.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675984 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00939: Reordering elements

- page_id: `169675984`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675984/Reordering+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-02T17:23:44.488+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the order of elements/properties presented in a list, tree, and Specification window, so if you need to move them, you can easily do so using one of the following methods:

To reorder elements in dialogs

1. Select one or more element(s) to move to the desired place.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Up (to move up) or Down (to move down) buttons on the right side of the dialog
  - Press Ctrl + Up (to move up) or Ctrl + Down (to move down) on your keyboard [ATTACHMENT filename='customize diagram wizard dialog.png']
  - Drag-and-drop the selected element(s) [IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you need to move multiple elements, press Ctrl on your keyboard and click to select the elements to reorder.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the order of elements/properties presented in a list, tree, and Specification window, so if you need to move them, you can easily do so using one of the following methods:</p><p>To reorder elements in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more element(s) to move to the desired place.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the <strong>Up </strong>(to move up) or<strong> Down</strong> (to move down) buttons on the right side of the dialog</li><li>Press Ctrl + Up (to move up) or Ctrl + Down (to move down) on your keyboard<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize diagram wizard dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reordering elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag-and-drop the selected element(s) </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_and_dropping_element_in_a_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reordering elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29652724-5bc7-4acd-add7-62624c32125c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to move multiple elements, press Ctrl on your keyboard and click to select the elements to reorder.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00940: Replacing elements

- page_id: `169675691`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675691/Replacing+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-21T14:37:13.973+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

###### Original element

The original element is the element that is going to be replaced. In other words, the replacement source.

###### Replacement target

The replacement target is the element with which the original element is going to be replaced.

###### Replaced element

The replaced element is the element to which the original element has been changed during the replacement.

###### Element properties

The element properties are so called the element specification properties (specified in the element Specification window) and the element symbol properties (defined in the element Symbol Properties dialog).

###### Inner elements

Inner elements are the elements stored inside the element (that is, owned by that element), for example, the class as inner package element.

You can replace one model element with another of the same type (metaclass) element. The model element replacement is useful when during the modeling process you notice that one model element needs to be replaced with another. All relations and references of the original element are transferred to the newly replaced element.

After the replacement, an original element will be replaced with a replacement target:

- The replacement target will be displayed in all diagrams instead of the original element.
- All references to the original element will be replaced by references to the replacement target.
- The replacement target after the replacement will have all the relationships of the replacement target and the original element.
- The original element will be deleted from the project.
- The original element properties and inner elements will be deleted from the project.

See the replacement example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The preceding figure illustrates the replacement of the *User* class instead of the *Customer* class. After the replacement the *User* class has been removed and the *Customer* class has been created instead of it.

INLINE

To replace one element with another

1. Select a symbol on the diagram pane or an element in the Model Browser and open its shortcut menu.
2. Click Refactor > Replace With . The warning message appears:
  - Click Yes to continue the element replacement. The properties and inner elements of the original element will be lost.
  - Click No to cancel the element replacement. The selected element will not be replaced.
3. If you decided to continue the replacement, the element selection dialog opens.
4. In the element selection dialog, select the element with which you want to replace the original element.
5. Click **OK**. The original element is replaced with the target element.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024xR1']

## PAGE 00941: Replacing recovered element with another element

- page_id: `169673708`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673708/Replacing+recovered+element+with+another+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-13T10:44:05.837+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305622476

305622479

305622477

At times, you may decide to reference a different element rather than the recovered one in the model.

To replace a reference from a recovered element to another one

1. Do either:
  - In the Model Browser, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Use Another Element .
  - In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is URE. From its shortcut menu select Use Another Element .
2. In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.
3. Click OK . The reference is now pointing to the selected element. Also, it is no longer unresolved.

You can use the command for more than one element at a time.

If some of the unresolved references are read-only, belong to the read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked, the command will skip these elements.

Alternatively, you can replace all references from a particular recovered element to another one using one operation.

To replace a recovered element with another one in all references

1. Do either:
  - In the Model Browser, right-click the recovered element and from its shortcut menu select Validation > Recovered Element > Change Usages To .
  - In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the recovered element and from its shortcut menu select Change Usages To .
2. In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.
3. Click OK . All the references are now pointing to another element. Also, they are no longer decorated as unresolved.

305622474

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing unresolved reference' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing recovered element symbol' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space='MD2024xR1']

## PAGE 00942: Report Data Management Pane

- page_id: `169677987`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677987/Report+Data+Management+Pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:20:40.042+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1277078685

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1277078691

1277078679

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1277078690

Report Wizard provides predefined report data templates that you can use to generate a report. The built-in report data template consists of several report data variables such as title, author, scope, and purpose that you can modify and adjust to meet your needs. You can also create a new report data template as needed, and manage the variables using the report data management pane.

This section defines report data, and how to use it in your report to represent variables that you want to include. Click the following links to navigate to the content of this page.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='MD2024xR1']

Reportdata

#### Report Data and Variables

A report in Report Wizard has a collection of variables which are called report data. Report Wizard provides a built-in report data for every predefined template. The built-in report data comes with predefined variables and values which you can edit or delete. You can also add more variables to the built-in report data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The built-in report data's predefined variables and values in Report Wizard.

You can create your own report data, and add variables and child variables under it if you do not want to use the built-in one. Report Wizard also allows you to create a report data as an element in the Containment tree inside a project by using the Report Profile which allows you to commit the Report Data to Teamwork Cloud, and share it with other users.

Reportdatapane

#### Report Data Management Pane

Report Wizard stores the built-in report data in the **Report Data**management pane which helps you to organize information into groups, and keep revision history created in a project.

This pane also allows you to:

- Create, edit, delete, or clone a Report Data.
- Create or organize the variables in a Report Data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the built-in report data in the Report Data management pane.

The following table lists the buttons and their function in the **Report Data**management pane.

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Creates report data. |
|  | Edits report data. |
|  | Deletes report data. |
|  | Clones report data. |
|  | Creates a report variable. |

WorkingwithReportdata

#### Working with Report Data

You can create report data either in:

- Report Wizard in which all data will be saved into an XML file.
- A modeling tool project, in which case the report data will be saved with the project itself. Saving report data in a project will enable you to commit the report to Teamwork Cloud.

The following instructions show you how to create and manage your report data in Report Wizard and in a modeling tool project.

To create report data in Report Wizard

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **New****Report Data** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a Report Data in the New Report Data dialog.
4. Enter a new report name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Before creating report data in a project, you need to use a report profile,*Report Profile.mdzip*, which is located in the*<install.root>\profiles*folder.

To use a report profile (Report Profile.mdzip)

1. On the main menu, click **File******> **Use Project** > **Use Local Project**. The **Use Project** dialog will open, and the project module path *<install.root>\profiles* will be selected by default. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Use Local Project menu opens the Use Project dialog that contains a report profile you need before creating a report data in a project. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Selecting a project module path for thereport profile and Report Profile.mdzip in the Use Project dialog.
2. Select *Report Profile*and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Specify the usage options. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Usage Options.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The “Report Profile” profile will open in the Containment tree as a read-only profile. You can now use it in your project.

create new report data in MD project

To create a Report Data in a project

1. Use *Report Profile.mdzip* in your project.
2. Right-click a data model in the Containment tree and select **Create Element**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating Report Data in the project
3. Select **Report Data** from the menu.
4. Type the name of the Report Data element in the Containment tree.

You can right-click a data model, a package, or a profile in the Containment tree to create a Report Data.

| Tag Values | Function |
| --- | --- |
| autoImageSize | Change the image size. |
| imageFormat | Select an image format, either JPEG or PNG. |
| emptyText | Store a string value that will be replaced with an empty variable. |
| data | Contain elements that will be published by Report Wizard. |
| template | Determine which template to use in a particular Report Data. |
| generateRecursively | Determine whether or not a report will be generated recursively. |

To edit report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The**Edit Report Data** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a report data in the Edit Report Data dialog
4. Edit the report’s name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

To delete report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Confirm delete** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Confirm delete dialog.
4. Click either [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] to delete the selected data or [ATTACHMENT filename='No_button.png'] to cancel the operation.

To clone report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Clone Report** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloning a report data in the Clone Report dialog.
4. Enter the name and description of the report. The name should begin with **Copy of** (cloned report name).
5. Click[IMAGE alt='' src='']. The cloned Report Data will then be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Copy of My Report created in the Report Data Management pane.

When a cloned Report Data is created, a copy of the Report Data (variable) will also be created.

WorkingwithReportvariables

#### Working with Report Data Variables

Variables are created because some information is not “naturally” contained in a model, e.g. the company's name, author's name, revisions. Although you can put all of the information in a model's specification (Document/Hyperlinks in the **Specification** dialog), it is difficult and tedious to get data from the model's specification, as this results in more lines of codes and scripts in the report templates. Therefore, instead of writing lines of codes, you can simply create a variable, give it a name, and call it directly from the template. For example, you can create a variable called “Author” in Report Wizard and write **$Author** in the template. When you generate the report, you will see the value inside “Author.” Essentially, a variable is used to represent data that you want to include in the report, not in the model.

Variables contain information that you want to store in a project, such as names and dates. You can create a report variable in a project or the Report Wizard dialog.

To create a report variable in a project

1. Right-click a **Report Data** in the Containment tree and select **Create Element** > **Variable**. The variable will appear inside the **Report Data**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a variable for Report Data in the Containment tree.

You can also create, edit, and delete variables through the **Report Variable** dialog in Report Wizard.

To create a report variable in the Report Wizard dialog

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='New_button.png'] . The New Variable dialog will open. Enter the variable name and description. You can create either a parent variable or a child variable in the Owner box.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.

To create a parent variable, type the variable name in the **Name** box, enter the description, select an empty value in the **Owner** box, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating MyNewVariableName as a parent variable.

To create a child variable, type the variable name in the **Name** box, enter the description, select**Parent** in the **Owner** box, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating a child variable of MyNewVariableName.

3. The new variable will appear in the table in the **Report Variable** dialog.

4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the variables in the Report Data.

To edit a report variable

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Double-click on the column next to the variable name column and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. A dialog will open in which you can edit the variable value. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a variable in the Template Variable dialog
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='OK_button.png'] . The new variable value will appear in the column next to the variable name column.

To delete a report variable

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Click a variable in the table, and then click **Delete**. A dialog will open prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Dialog to confirm the deletion of a report variable
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] and the variable will be deleted.

To get the value of a top-level variable

1. Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.
2. Type: **$Parent**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Referencing to a parent variable in the template.
3. Save the file as “*sampletemplate.rtf*”. Choose Rich Text Format (**.rtf*) as the file type.
4. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new template. The **New** **Template** dialog will open.
5. Type the name and description of the new template. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select “*sampletemplate.rtf*” as the template file.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] >**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. The**Select Report Data** pane will open.
7. Select a report data, in this example, **My Report Data** and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you include the variables you have created in a template, each variable value will be included in the generated report. This section will use the following **My Report Data** as an example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### My Report Data as an example.

To get the value of the child of a variable

1. Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.
2. Type any of the following to print a child variable. (i) **$Parent.get(1)**to get a child value by index. (ii) **$Parent.get(“Child”)** to get a child value by name comparison. (iii) **$Parent.Child**to get a child value by referencing its name, in this case “Child”. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Referencing to the child variable in the template
3. Save the file as “*sampletemplate.rtf*”. Choose **Rich Text Format** (**.rtf*) as the file type.
4. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new template. The **New Template** dialog will open.
5. Type the name and description of the new template. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select “*sampletemplate.rtf*” as the template file.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Select Report Data** pane will open.
7. Select a report data, in this example, **My Report Data** and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The resulting RTF file.

#### Glossary

- Clone To clone is to create an exact copy of report data.

- RTF Rich Text Format (RTF) is a file format developed by Microsoft that can be used with different word processors in different operating systems.

#### More information about working with reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about reports in Report Wizard, you can visit the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the command line' space='MD2024xR1']

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']250

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4db7214-d3dc-499d-90ea-77da7bd57240"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078685</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f93fb5-fb8b-43cd-91b4-80a0bf5b2a74"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078691</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a25830c1-cfcf-4066-856c-083cc4ce0976"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d916590-ea04-46c4-86b6-fee4fa116189"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21d8533e-1f56-4f37-99f9-489fa8427806"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078690</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Report Wizard provides predefined report data templates that you can use to generate a report. The built-in report data template consists of several report data variables such as title, author, scope, and purpose that you can modify and adjust to meet your needs. You can also create a new report data template as needed, and manage the variables using the report data management pane.</p><p>This section defines report data, and how to use it in your report to represent variables that you want to include. Click the following links to navigate to the content of this page.</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reportdata"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Data and Variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reportdatapane"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Data Management pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithReportdata"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Report Data]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithReportvariables"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Report Data Variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54b10626-197a-4056-b21d-7620bb95addb"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Reportdata</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Report Data and Variables</span></h2><p>A report in Report Wizard has a collection of variables which are called report data. Report Wizard provides a built-in report data for every predefined template. The built-in report data comes with predefined variables and values which you can edit or delete. You can also add more variables to the built-in report data.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BuiltinVariables.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The built-in report data's predefined variables and values in Report Wizard.</h6><p>You can create your own report data, and add variables and child variables under it if you do not want to use the built-in one. Report Wizard also allows you to create a report data as an element in the Containment tree inside a project by using the Report Profile which allows you to commit the Report Data to Teamwork Cloud, and share it with other users.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eac4b8e3-d7df-4514-80f6-25bf3295f39a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Reportdatapane</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Report Data Management Pane</h2><p>Report Wizard stores the built-in report data in the <strong>Report Data </strong>management pane which helps you to organize information into groups, and keep revision history created in a project. </p><p>This pane also allows you to:</p><ul><li>Create, edit, delete, or clone a Report Data.</li><li>Create or organize the variables in a Report Data.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Built-in Report Data.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting the built-in report data in the Report Data management pane.</h6><p>The following table lists the buttons and their function in the <strong>Report Data </strong>management pane.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Creates report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Edits report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Deletes report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Clones report data.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Creates a report variable.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18d004f5-45e4-4a94-9453-e0b0858b4c4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">WorkingwithReportdata</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Working with Report Data</h2><p>You can create report data either in:</p><ul><li>Report Wizard in which all data will be saved into an XML file.</li><li>A modeling tool project, in which case the report data will be saved with the project itself. Saving report data in a project will enable you to commit the report to Teamwork Cloud.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>The following instructions show you how to create and manage your report data in Report Wizard and in a modeling tool project.</p><p>To create report data in Report Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>New </strong><strong>Report Data</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="New Report Data Dialog" ac:alt="New Report Data Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-report-data-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Creating a Report Data in the New Report Data dialog.</h6></li><li>Enter a new report name and description, and then click<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</li></ol><p>Before <span>creating report data in a project, you <span>need to use a report profile, </span><em>Report Profile.mdzip</em><span>, which is located in the </span><em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em><span> folder. </span></span></p><p>To use a report profile (Report Profile.mdzip)</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong><strong> </strong>&gt; <strong>Use Project</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Local Project</strong>. The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog will open, and the project module path <em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em> will be selected by default.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UseLocalProject_menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Use Local Project menu opens the Use Project dialog that contains a report profile you need before creating a report data in a project.</h6><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_select.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Selecting a project module path for the<span> report profile and Report Profile.mdzip</span> in the Use Project dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Select <em>Report Profile </em>and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Specify the usage options.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UsageOptions_select.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Usage Options.</h6></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Finish_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The “Report Profile” profile will open in the Containment tree as a read-only profile. You can now use it in your project.</p></li></ol><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="140d6f37-2e15-4467-9f5d-5bed364565bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create new report data in MD project</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To create a Report Data in a project</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Use <em>Report Profile.mdzip</em> in your project.</p></li><li><p>Right-click a data model in the Containment tree and select <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_ReportData.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span>Creating Report Data in the project</span></h6></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Data</strong> from the menu.</p></li><li>Type the name of the <strong>Report Data</strong> element in the Containment tree.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03eb30b1-3dc1-478d-b1fe-919fdfbbc25f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can right-click a data model, a package, or a profile in the Containment tree to create a Report Data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Tag Values</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>autoImageSize</td><td>Change the image size.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>imageFormat</td><td>Select an image format, either JPEG or PNG.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>emptyText</td><td>Store a string value that will be replaced with an empty variable.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>data</td><td><p>Contain elements that will be published by Report Wizard.</p></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">template</td><td colspan="1">Determine which template to use in a particular Report Data.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">generateRecursively</td><td colspan="1">Determine whether or not a report will be generated recursively.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">To edit report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The<strong> Edit Report Data</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Edit Report Data Dialog" ac:alt="Edit Report Data Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit-report-data-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Editing a report data in the Edit Report Data dialog</h6></li><li><p>Edit the report’s name and description, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target">To delete report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Confirm delete</strong> dialog will appear.    </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Confirm Delete Dialog" ac:alt="Confirm Delete Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm-delete-report-data.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Confirm delete dialog.</h6></li><li>Click either <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to delete the selected data or <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="No_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to cancel the operation.</li></ol><p>To clone report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Clone Report</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Clone Report Dialog" ac:alt="Clone Report Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone-report-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Cloning a report data in the Clone Report dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Enter the name and description of the report. The name should begin with <strong>Copy of</strong> (cloned report name).</p></li><li><p><span>Click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span>. The cloned Report Data will then be created.</span></p></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Copy of My Report.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Copy of My Report created in the Report Data Management pane.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ccbe5a1-e65d-4424-8267-cab5e66c46a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a cloned Report Data is created, a copy of the Report Data (variable) will also be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38ad4cab-6003-4d7d-9f6e-d9583c22eb71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">WorkingwithReportvariables</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Working with Report Data Variables</h2><p>Variables are created because some information is not “naturally” contained in a model, e.g. the company's name, author's name, revisions. Although you can put all of the information in a model's specification (Document/Hyperlinks in the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog), it is difficult and tedious to get data from the model's specification, as this results in more lines of codes and scripts in the report templates. Therefore, instead of writing lines of codes, you can simply create a variable, give it a name, and call it directly from the template. For example, you can create a variable called “Author” in Report Wizard and write <strong>$Author</strong> in the template. When you generate the report, you will see the value inside “Author.” Essentially, a variable is used to represent data that you want to include in the report, not in the model.</p><p>Variables contain information that you want to store in a project, such as names and dates. You can create a report variable in a project or the Report Wizard dialog.</p><p>To create a report variable in a project</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Right-click a <strong>Report Data</strong> in the Containment tree and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Variable</strong>. The variable will appear inside the <strong>Report Data</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_Variable.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span>Creating a variable for Report Data in the Containment tree.</span></h6></li></ol><p>You can also create, edit, and delete variables through the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog in Report Wizard.</p><p>To create a report variable in the Report Wizard dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>New</strong> <strong>Variable</strong> dialog will open. Enter the variable name and description. You can create either a parent variable or a child variable in the <strong>Owner</strong> box.</li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="New variable dialog" ac:alt="New variable dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To create a parent variable, type the variable name in the <strong>Name</strong> box, enter the description, select an empty value in the <strong>Owner</strong> box, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Creating a Parent Variable" ac:alt="Creating a Parent Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-c.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Creating MyNewVariableName as a parent variable.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To create a child variable, type the variable name in the <strong>Name</strong> box, enter the description, select<strong> Parent</strong> in the <strong>Owner</strong> box, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Creating a Child Variable" ac:alt="Creating a Child Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-d.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Creating a child variable of MyNewVariableName.</p><p>     3.  The new variable will appear in the table in the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</p><p>     4.  Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to save the variables in the Report Data.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To edit a report variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Double-click on the column next to the variable name column and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="browse_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. A dialog will open in which you can edit the variable value.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Editing Variables" ac:alt="Editing Variables"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-e.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Editing a variable in the Template Variable dialog</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The new variable value will appear in the column next to the variable name column.</li></ol><p>To delete a report variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Click a variable in the table, and then click <strong>Delete</strong>. A dialog will open prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Deleting Variable" ac:alt="Deleting Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete-variable-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Dialog to confirm the deletion of a report variable</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and the variable will be deleted.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To get the value of a top-level variable<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.</li><li><p>Type: <strong>$Parent</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Referencing to Parent Variable in the Template" ac:alt="Referencing to Parent Variable in the Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MSWORD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Referencing to a parent variable in the template.</h6></li><li><p>Save the file as “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>”. Choose Rich Text Format (<em>*.rtf</em>) as the file type.</p></li><li><p>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a new template. The <strong>New</strong> <strong>Template</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Type the name and description of the new template. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to select “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>” as the template file.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt;<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>. The<strong> Select Report Data</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Select a report data, in this example, <strong>My Report Data</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parent_result.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b50bd350-075c-47d5-b0ac-c193e56e5d64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you include the variables you have created in a template, each variable value will be included in the generated report. This section will use the following <strong>My Report Data</strong> as an example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Report Data" ac:alt="Sample of Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sample of report data.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">My Report Data as an example.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To get the value of the child of a variable</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.</li><li><p>Type any of the following to print a child variable.</p><p>(i) <strong>$Parent.get(1) </strong>to get a child value by index.</p><p>(ii) <strong>$Parent.get(“Child”) </strong> to get a child value by name comparison.</p><p>(iii) <strong>$Parent.Child </strong>to get a child value by referencing its name, in this case “Child”.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Referencing to Child Variable in the Template" ac:alt="Referencing to Child Variable in the Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MSWORD2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Referencing to the child variable in the template</h6></li><li><p>Save the file as “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>”. Choose <strong>Rich Text Format</strong> (<em>*.rtf</em>) as the file type.</p></li><li><p>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a new template. The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Type the name and description of the new template. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to select “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>” as the template file.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Report Data</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Select a report data, in this example, <strong>My Report Data</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="child_result.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><br /></span></p></li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span>The resulting RTF file.</span></h6><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Clone</strong><br />To clone is to create an exact copy of report data.</li></ul><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>RTF</strong><br />Rich Text Format (RTF) is a file format developed by Microsoft that can be used with different word processors in different operating systems.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with reports in Report Wizard</h2><p class="confluence-link">To learn more about reports in Report Wizard, you can visit the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Reports from the Containment Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating reports from the command line" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Reports from the Command Line]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Sample model</h2><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="view-file" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f35fc673-0b17-4051-9a7d-cf664c12b0b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">250</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00943: Report Designer

- page_id: `169678068`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678068/Report+Designer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-08-24T03:52:13.150+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1769283899

1769283902

1769283901

Report Designer is a tool for conveniently drafting your template in Microsoft Word Document (DOCX) and Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet (XLSX) formats. Without requiring knowledge in UML and expertise in Velocity template creators, this tool provides you with an easy way to access elements and properties in a few clicks.

The Report Designer diagram is for starting to create and design your report template. This diagram helps you draft the template structure or pseudocode. When you drag template diagram elements to the diagram, Report Designer will allow you to select available properties of the particular elements, view the appearance of your template, generate the drafted template that contains skeleton code, add more detail of code logic, and arrange text style and layout to the drafted template later to complete creating the template.

982760273

**Related pages**

Without requiring knowledge in UML and expertise in Velocity template creators, this tool provides you with an easy way to access elements and properties in a few clicks.</p><p>The Report Designer diagram is for starting to create and design your report template. This diagram helps you draft the template structure or pseudocode. When you drag template diagram elements to the diagram, Report Designer will allow you to select available properties of the particular elements, view the appearance of your template, generate the drafted template that contains skeleton code, add more detail of code logic, and arrange text style and layout to the drafted template later to complete creating the template.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c909faa3-1543-4f51-9ae0-4ac92c125ded"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">982760273</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4e88b85d-c02a-425c-a379-205e7a4d36b5" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675371 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00944: Report Template

- page_id: `169675371`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675371/Report+Template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-13T13:43:42.791+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

846411724

846411726

846411725

This is a custom element used to store a report template file (*.mrzip*) attached to the project.

In contrast to the report templates stored in the local report templates folder (*<modeling tool [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MD2024xR1']>\data\reports*), a report template attached to the project as this specific type of model element is not lost when you open the project on another computer. It is safely transferred within the project (either local or server).

To attach a report template to the project

1. From the main menu, select Tools > Report Wizard . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Report Wizard** dialog opens.
2. Select the report template you want to attach to the project and click the Attach button. The selected report template is duplicated, and the duplicate is attached to the open project. It is visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1'] directly under the root package. As the following figure shows, in the Report Wizard dialog it as marked with the “[Attached to Project]” tag.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Attached to a project report template in the Report Wizard dialog.

You can generate a report from the attached report template in one of the following ways:

- Right-click the attached report template in the Containment tree. From the shortcut menu select Generate Report . The Report Wizard dialog opens with the attached report template already selected. Proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.
- Right-click the namespace (for example, a Package) in the Containment tree. From the shortcut menu select Generate Report . Then select the appropriate report templates category and click the attached report template.

The *“[Attached]”* tag helps to identify attached to project report templates on this menu.

Proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.

- From the main menu, select Tools > Report Wizard . In the Report Wizard dialog, select the report template and proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting attached to project report template for report generation.

If you no longer need the attached report template, you can simply delete it from the model. Just select it in the Containment tree and press **Delete**.

If you need to modify the attached report template, do the following:

- 
  - Extract it to the local report templates folder.
  - Open the extracted template for edit and make changes in it.
  - After you finish editing, reattach the updated report template to your project.

To extract a report template to the local report templates folder

- 
  - From the main menu, select Tools > Report Wizard. The Report Wizard dialog opens.
  - Select the report template you want to extract to the local report templates folder and click the Extract button.
  - If a message asking to confirm the update of already existing report template appears, click Yes.

The report template appears in the local report templates folder and can be opened for edit. It also remains attached to the open project.

846411723

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

It also remains attached to the open project. </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cedf4174-fb8b-4171-8c27-e9a02eef671e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">846411723</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

$report is a utility tool enabling a template to get the modeling tool data.

##### **$report.createValueSpecificationText(specification)**

Create texts representing the ValueSpecification element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | specification | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.ValueSpecification | A given Value Specification. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A String value for ValueSpecification. |

****

##### **$report.filterDiagram(diagramList, diagramTypes)**

Return a collection of diagrams from a list of diagrams filtered by types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | diagramList | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagrams. |
| diagramTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered diagrams. |

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.filterElement(elementList, humanTypes)**

Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by human types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| humanTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of human types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can change the human type depending on the language used in the modeling tool. For example, "Class" is the human type of the classes in the English modeling tool while "Classe" is the human type for the classes in French. The specified humanTypes needs to be changed when you change the language used in the modeling tool.

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.filterElementType(elementList, elementType)**

Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by element types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| elementTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of element types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

##### **$report.filter(elementList, propertyName, propertyValue)**

Return a collection of elements filtered by a specified property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |  |
| propertyValue | java.util.Collection | A collection of property names used as filters. |  |
| Return |  | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.filterElementByStereotypeName(elementList, appliedStereotypeNameList)**

Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by applied stereotype names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| appliedStereotypeNameList | java.util.Collection | A collection of applied stereotype names. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.findElementInCollection(elementList, name)**

Find an element from a collection of elements by name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| name | java.lang.String | An element name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element instance. If it cannot find the element, it will return a null value. |

For example:

```text

```

##### **$report.findRelationship(modelPackage)**

Search and return a collection of relationship elements inside a package.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelPackage | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package | A package element. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of relationships in a package. |

****

##### **$report.findRelationship(modelPackage, recursive)**

Search and return a collection of relationship elements inside a package.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelPackage | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package | A package element. |
| recursive | boolean | If true, performs recursively. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of relationships in a package. |

****

##### **$report.getAppliedStereotypeByName(element, stereotypeName)**

Return a stereotype assigned to an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| stereotypeName | java.lang.String | A stereotype name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype | An assigned stereotype with a specified name. |

****

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | classifier | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier | A classifier. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of associations. |

****

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | classifier | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier | A classifier. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of attributes. |

****

##### **$report.getBaseClassInheritableOperations(classifier)**

Obtain a collection of inheritable operations of a classifier.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | classifier | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier | A classifier. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of operations. |

****

##### **$report.getBaseClassPorts(classifier)**

Find a collection of ports of a classifier.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | classifier | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier | A classifier. |

##### **$report.getElementProperties(element)**

Return a list of property names, including UML properties and properties that are customized through DSL customization.

For example:

```text
$pp.value
#end]]>
```

By default, all properties are exported. They can be filtered by adding a "STANDARD" or "EXPERT" argument, e.g.*$report.getElementProperties($element, "STANDARD").*

##### **$report.getElementName(element)**

Get an element’s name. If the name is empty, it will return "<unnamed>". This method performs the following procedures:

- If the element is generalized from the NamedElement metaclass, it will return $element.name
- If the element is generalized from the Slot metaclass, it will return $element.definingFeature.name
- If the element is a UML element, it will return $element.humanName
- Else returns $element.toString()

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | java.lang.Object | An element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An element name. |

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(element)**

Return an image icon of an element with a default icon filename (“icon_”+element type).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling toolelement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object of the element's icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.

****

##### $report.getImageFor(element, dpi)

Return an image with specified DPI of an element.

| Name | Type | Description | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling toolelement. |
| dpi | int | DPI of the element image. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object with specified DPI. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The DPI value can be 1 to 4800.
- If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI set in *Template* and *Global* will be used respectively. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting image DPI' space='MD2024xR1'] .
- If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800 .

##### **$report.getIconFor(element, prefix, suffix, hashCode)**

Return an image icon of an element. The icon name depends on the ***hashCode*** value if ***hashCode*** equals true, the icon’s name is set as***”icon_”+hash*** code value of the icon. If the ***hashCode***value equals false, the icon name is set as

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling tool element. |
| prefix | java.lang.String | A prefix of an icon name. |  |
| suffix | java.lang.String | A suffix of an icon name. |  |
| hashCode | boolean | If true, use hash code of an icon as the icon name and exclude a prefix or suffix from the name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object of an element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.

****

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(type)**

Return an image icon for an element type with a default icon’s filename ((“icon_”+element type).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A type name. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.

****

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(type, prefix, suffix, hashCode)**

Return an image icon for an element type. The icon’s name depends on the ***hashCode*** value if ***hashCode*** equals true, the icon name is set as ***“icon_”+hash*** code value of the icon. If the ***hashCode*** value equals false, the icon name is set as ***prefix + element type + suffix***.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A type name. |
| prefix | java.lang.String | A prefix for an icon name. |  |
| suffix | java.lang.String | A suffix for an icon name. |  |
| hashCode | boolean | If true, use hash code ofan icon as the icon’s name and exclude a prefix or suffix from the name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for an element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.

****

##### **$report.getQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)**

Get a qualified name. A qualified name is the name that allows a ***NamedElement***to be identified within a hierarchy of nested ***Namespaces***. It is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the name of the ***NamedElement***itself.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | namedElement | NamedElement | A NamedElement. |
| separator | java.lang.String | A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A qualified name. |

****

##### **$report.getPackageQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)**

Get a qualified name by considering only **Packages** and a given element. **Models** and **Profiles** will not be included in the qualified name.

For example, given the element hierarchy:

```text
com : Package -> nomagic : Package -> ui -> Package -> BaseDialog : Class]]>
```

```text

```

If $class is a “BaseDialog” element, the result from the above template code will be as follows.

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | namedElement | NamedElement | A NamedElement. |
| separator | java.lang.String | A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A qualified name. |

****

| Parameter(s) | parent | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier | A parent. |

| Return | - | boolean | True if it is derived from the parent by generalization. |

****

##### **$report.isNamedElement(element)**

Return whether an element is a NamedElement.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element to test. |
| Return | - | boolean | True if the given element is a NamedElement; otherwise, false |

****

##### **$report.isNull(obj)**

Test and return true if an object is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | obj | java.lang.Object | An object being tested. |
| Return | - | boolean | True if the object is null. |

****

##### **$report.isRelationship(element)**

Test and return true if an element is a relationship.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element being tested. |
| Return | - | boolean | True if the object is a relationship. |

****

##### **$report.serialize(hyperlink)**

Convert *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink* to *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.magicreport.helper.Hyperlink*. Report Wizard needs the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.magicreport.helper.Hyperlink* class wrapper to return *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink* data.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | hyperlink | com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink | A modeling toolhyperlink. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.magicreport.helper.Hyperlink | A Report Wizard hyperlink instance. |

****

##### **$report.getUsedBy(element)**

Return a list of element(s) used by this element (except diagrams).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element of which to determine the usage. |
| Return |  | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements used by an input element. |

****

Return true when a property with a given name is specified in this element, otherwise returns false.

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.Boolean | Return true when a property with a given name is specified in this element, otherwise returns false. |

For example:

```text

```

- $element is the element.
- data is the name of the property.

****

Get element property.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.Object | A property value. |

For example:

##### **$report.findElementByName(source, regex)**

Search and return elements that match the name using a regular expression.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | source | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
|  | regex | java.lang.String | A regular expression with which the name is to be matched. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of matching elements. |

For example:

```text

```

- $elements is a collection of elements to be found.
- “[A]+.*” is a regular expression to find which element matches the name. In this example, the following are the names that match, such as Auxiliary, AppServer, and Alternative Fragment.

****

| Return | - | java.lang.String | A formatted element. |

For example:

```text

```

- false if a full path is not used. In this example, a full path is not used.
