<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678723 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00949: Report Wizard Directives

- page_id: `169678723`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678723/Report+Wizard+Directives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T06:57:35.898+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1357591840

1357591841

1357591840

Report Wizard introduces three directives: **#forrow**, **#forcol**, and **#forpage**. These directives allow you to use Velocity to generate outputs other than web documents. With these directives, you can eliminate problems that usually occur when using Velocity to generate an rtf document, for example, **#foreach** generates broken rows in a table.

**#forrow** 
 This directive is used in the Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text, and OpenDocument spreadsheet, Microsoft Word Document (DOCX), and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX) documents. Whenever #forrow iterates through a list, it creates a row in a table.

**#forcol** 
 This directive is used only in Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX), OpenDocument spreadsheet, and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX). Whenever #forcol iterates through a list, it creates a column in a table.

**#forpage** 
 This directive is used in Rich Text Format document, OpenDocument Text, OpenDocument spreadsheet, OpenDocument presentation, Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), and Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX). Whenever #forpage iterates through a list, it creates a new page in Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text or Microsoft Word (DOCX). It also creates a new sheet in OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), or a new slide in OpenDocument presentation or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX).

1357591839

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02cd345f-0dfa-4de2-95ab-9b5b1960bd90"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357591840</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5fd9279-bdc3-40f3-9ccf-c45e4ccdb2e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357591841</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="308f75f0-872d-4ec8-aefa-065ebe097225"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357591840</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Report Wizard introduces three directives: <strong>#forrow</strong>, <strong>#forcol</strong>, and <strong>#forpage</strong>. These directives allow you to use Velocity to generate outputs other than web documents. With these directives, you can eliminate problems that usually occur when using Velocity to generate an rtf document, for example, <strong>#foreach</strong> generates broken rows in a table.</p><p><strong>#forrow</strong><br /> This directive is used in the Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text, and OpenDocument spreadsheet, Microsoft Word Document (DOCX), and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX) documents. Whenever #forrow iterates through a list, it creates a row in a table.</p><p><strong>#forcol</strong><br /> This directive is used only in Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX), OpenDocument spreadsheet, and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX). Whenever #forcol iterates through a list, it creates a column in a table.</p><p><strong>#forpage</strong><br /> This directive is used in Rich Text Format document, OpenDocument Text, OpenDocument spreadsheet, OpenDocument presentation, Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), and Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX). Whenever #forpage iterates through a list, it creates a new page in Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text or Microsoft Word (DOCX). It also creates a new sheet in OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), or a new slide in OpenDocument presentation or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca5360a2-51d0-433c-bbfd-bb15fb10902a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357591839</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8a118dac-10d5-49c2-8c92-e5f75d8cabab" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678519 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00950: Report Wizard Environment options

- page_id: `169678519`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678519/Report+Wizard+Environment+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:54:32.773+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1334042562

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1334042564

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1334042564

The Report Wizard Environment options in the **Environment Options** dialog allow you to configure the report engine. There are three things that you can configure: (i) Report engine properties, (ii) Template mappings, and (iii) Template folder.

**(i) Report engine properties**

Report engine properties can be configured as follows:

- Template process size (template.process.size) This integer value defines the size of allocated memory for processing a template. If the template is larger than the allocated size, the engine will create temporary files on a local disk and process the template from these files.
- Template pool size (template.pool.size) This integer value defines the number of processing threads for evaluating a template. An increase in the number of threads may improve the performance of the engine, which will also depend on your hardware and JVM.
- Velocity properties These properties are a key-value pair. You can add any number for these properties. You will need a basic knowledge of Velocity to enter the value. (See [http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/apidocs/org/apache/velocity/runtime/RuntimeConstants.html](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/apidocs/org/apache/velocity/runtime/RuntimeConstants.html) )
  - velocimacro.max.depth
  - velocimacro.context.localscope
- Debug report template . You can configure this value to enable or disable any invalid properties, references, or exception messages on the message window. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Debug report template' space='MD2024xR1'] for more details.
  - template.warn.invalid.property
  - template.warn.invalid.reference
  - template.warn.invalid.method
  - template.warn.exception
- rememberOutputOptions . This value determines whether Report Wizard will remember output options of the report file when generating report. Yes if this value is true, and No if this value is false. See the last figure on this page.
- synchronizeAttachedTemplate . This value is for synchronizing an attached local report with the latest report in the server project. This property is used every time a report is generated from the attached template. Other properties that are not in the Configure report properties table are as follows:
  - template.rtf.newline Specify the new line character for the RTF documentation. The values are line (by default) and paragraph .
  - template.docx.ignorechildstyle A key for ignoring styles from included templates in the generated report. When False (by default), the styles from included templates are included in the generated report. When True , the styles from included templates are not included in the generated report, and matching styles in the main template are applied instead.
  - template.xlsx.html.link A key for selecting the first or last hyperlink in the HTML string of any cell and applying the selected hyperlink to the cell. The values are first , last , and skip .
  - element.additional.include Use an additional element and owned element to get children of the element.
  - template.odp.use.virtual.name A key for enabling the engine to replace an ODP hyperlink with a page name.

**(ii) Template mappings**

Template mappings associate a template type with the template engine. The Report engine allows you to determine how to handle different types of files, such as JS or DOCBOOK files. All you have to do is specify a filename extension and select an engine name. The Report engine also allows developers to add a user custom engine to this option.

**(iii) Template folder**

You can either select or clear the **Monitor template folder** check box in the Report Wizard Environment options in the **Environments Options** dialog to enable or disable the option to deploy an MRZIP template file. If you select the check box, Report Wizard will automatically deploy the MRZIP template file to the Report Wizard dialog whenever the file is added to your folder.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Report Wizard GUI configuration in the Environment Options dialog. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Report properties dialog in the Report Wizard Environment options.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

After updating the Report Wizard Environment options, restart the application to take effect of the changes.

1334042561

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e087a50-2c37-41c2-bace-111884f83126"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334042562</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3747c80e-e801-4f13-b932-892c3302697d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334042564</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84713bac-f345-4078-91a7-19f3786a2c4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334042564</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Report Wizard Environment options in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog allow you to configure the report engine. There are three things that you can configure: (i) Report engine properties, (ii) Template mappings, and (iii) Template folder.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>(i) Report engine properties</strong></p><p>Report engine properties can be configured as follows:</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Template process size (template.process.size)</strong><br />This integer value defines the size of allocated memory for processing a template. If the template is larger than the allocated size, the engine will create temporary files on a local disk and process the template from these files.<br /><br /></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Template pool size (template.pool.size)</strong><br />This integer value defines the number of processing threads for evaluating a template. An increase in the number of threads may improve the performance of the engine, which will also depend on your hardware and JVM.<br /><br /></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Velocity properties</strong><br />These properties are a key-value pair. You can add any number for these properties. You will need a basic knowledge of Velocity to enter the value. (See <em><a href="http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/apidocs/org/apache/velocity/runtime/RuntimeConstants.html" style="line-height: 1.4285715;">http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/apidocs/org/apache/velocity/runtime/RuntimeConstants.html</a></em>)<br /><ul><li>velocimacro.max.depth</li><li>velocimacro.context.localscope<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Debug report template</strong>. <br />You can configure this value to enable or disable any invalid properties, references, or exception messages on the message window. See <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Debug report template" /></ac:link></em> for more details.<br /><ul><li>template.warn.invalid.property</li><li>template.warn.invalid.reference</li><li>template.warn.invalid.method</li><li>template.warn.exception<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>rememberOutputOptions</strong>. <br />This value determines whether Report Wizard will remember output options of the report file when generating report. <strong>Yes</strong> if this value is true, and <strong>No</strong> if this value is false. See the last figure on this page.<br /><br /></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>synchronizeAttachedTemplate</strong>.<br />This value is for synchronizing an attached local report with the latest report in the server project. This property is used every time a report is generated from the attached template.<br /><br />Other properties that are not in the <strong>Configure report properties</strong> table are as follows:<br /><br /><ul><li>template.rtf.newline<br />Specify the new line character for the RTF documentation. The values are <strong>line</strong> (by default) and <strong>paragraph</strong>.<br /><br /></li><li>template.docx.ignorechildstyle<br />A key for ignoring styles from included templates in the generated report. When <strong>False</strong> (by default), the styles from included templates are included in the generated report. When <strong>True</strong>, the styles from included templates are not included in the generated report, and matching styles in the main template are applied instead.<br /><br /></li><li>template.xlsx.html.link<br />A key for selecting the first or last hyperlink in the HTML string of any cell and applying the selected hyperlink to the cell. The values are <strong>first</strong>, <strong>last</strong>, and <strong>skip</strong>.<br /><br /></li><li>element.additional.include<br />Use an additional element and owned element to get children of the element.<br /><br /></li><li>template.odp.use.virtual.name<br />A key for enabling the engine to replace an ODP hyperlink with a page name.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong>(ii) Template mappings</strong></p><p>Template mappings associate a template type with the template engine. The Report engine allows you to determine how to handle different types of files, such as JS or DOCBOOK files. All you have to do is specify a filename extension and select an engine name. The Report engine also allows developers to add a user custom engine to this option.</p><p><strong>(iii) Template folder</strong></p><p>You can either select or clear the <strong>Monitor template folder</strong> check box in the Report Wizard Environment options in the <strong>Environments Options</strong> dialog to enable or disable the option to deploy an MRZIP template file. If you select the check box, Report Wizard will automatically deploy the MRZIP template file to the Report Wizard dialog whenever the file is added to your folder.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Report Wizard GUI Configuration in MagicDraw Environment Options Dialog" ac:alt="Report Wizard GUI Configuration in MagicDraw Environment Options Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="env-options-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard Environment options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Report Wizard GUI configuration in the Environment Options dialog.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Report Wizard Environment Options" ac:alt="Report Wizard Environment Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="env-options-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Report Wizard Environment options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Report properties dialog in the Report Wizard Environment options.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3a65857-f818-49ee-ac9b-46bfee8ceecf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After updating the Report Wizard Environment options, restart the application to take effect of the changes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="757a94e9-cebd-4f05-894b-23c18f1e1053"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334042561</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="591c186a-5723-46a1-9b1e-6e23c77fede7" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678149 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00951: Report Wizard Quick Print

- page_id: `169678149`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678149/Report+Wizard+Quick+Print
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-12T10:32:54.448+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard provides five keyboard shortcuts to generate a report, with last used options, from a recently-used template. The table below lists the predefined keyboard shortcuts:

| Keyboard Shortcut | Quick Print Template |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Template name A |
| Alt + 2 | Template name B |
| Alt + 3 | Template name C |
| Alt + 4 | Template name D |
| Alt + 5 | Template name E |

To change a keyboard shortcut

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment to open the Environment Options dialog.
2. Select Keyboard from the tree list.
3. Select Tools from the Category drop-down menu.
4. Press the new shortcut keys on your keyboard, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Assign_button.png'] .

To use Report Wizard Quick Print

1. After a report has been generated, a new menu will appear below the **Report Wizard** menu. The name of the new menu is the name of the recently generated template. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Newly generated reports are listed on the **Tools** main menu.
2. Either click the template name, or press **Alt+1**, **Alt+2**, or**Alt+3**to generate this template with the last used options.
3. Select the saved location and filename.
4. A message dialog will open, asking if you want to open the report in the default viewer once the generating process has completed.

#### INFO: Info

Info

- The next time you generate a report, a new menu will create and map the keyboard shortcuts until the keyboard shortcuts list is fully filled. The number of keyboard shortcuts is limited to five.

- If the keyboard shortcuts list is full, and the new template has been generated, the keyboard shortcuts will be reused.

The table below lists some of the keyboard shortcuts provided by Report Wizard Quick Print:

| Shortcut Key | Template Name |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Tutorial 01 – Print Element |
| Alt + 2 | Tutorial 02 – Print Diagram |
| Alt + 3 | Tutorial 03 – Print Project |
| Alt + 4 | Tutorial 04 – Print Report Data |
| Alt + 5 | Tutorial 05 – Macro |

The table above shows that the Tutorial 01 – Print Element keyboard shortcut is the most outdated template, and the following report has been completed using the template. Report Wizard Quick Print will reuse the keyboard shortcut of the most outdated template.

The table below lists commonly re-used keyboard shortcuts:

| Shortcut Key | Template Name |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Business Process Diagram |
| Alt + 2 | Tutorial 02 – Print Diagram |
| Alt + 3 | Tutorial 03 – Print Project |
| Alt + 4 | Tutorial 04 – Print Report Data |
| Alt + 5 | Tutorial 05 – Macro |

If the next template to be generated has already been in the Quick Print list, the list will not change.

#### Glossary

- Template A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.

#### More information about Report Wizard options

To learn more about Report Wizard environment options, you can visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1'].

Report Wizard Quick Print will reuse the keyboard shortcut of the most outdated template.</p><p>The table below lists commonly re-used keyboard shortcuts:</p><table style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Shortcut Key</th><th>Template Name</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 1 </td><td>Business Process Diagram</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 2</td><td>Tutorial 02 – Print Diagram</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 3 </td><td>Tutorial 03 – Print Project</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 4</td><td>Tutorial 04 – Print Report Data</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 5</td><td>Tutorial 05 – Macro</td></tr></tbody></table><p>If the next template to be generated has already been in the Quick Print list, the list will not change.</p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Template</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.<br /></span></li></ul><h2>More information about Report Wizard options</h2><p>To learn more about Report Wizard environment options, you can visit <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.<br /></span></p>

## PAGE 00953: Report Wizard template language

- page_id: `169678156`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678156/Report+Wizard+template+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-17T06:39:01.575+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1266456373

1266456374

1266456374

Report Wizard is built on Velocity Engine. Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language and the Report Wizard Custom Language used within the Report Wizard template is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates.

1266456372

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673381 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00954: Reporting Issues

- page_id: `169673381`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673381/Reporting+Issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-08T14:38:43.463+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428101707

INLINE

1428101711

INLINE

1428137241

INLINE

**On this page**

5

1428101710

INLINE

INLINE

Need help? Send notifications of a problem, suggest improvements for our modeling tools, or ask about new features.

###### offlineReport an issue when the program is unresponsive

If you encounter an issue or the modeling tool becomes unresponsive, a separately executable tool is provided for analyzing the status of the process to aid in bug submission.In these situations, manually start the *submit_issue.exe* file (located in the *<modeling tool installation directory>\bin folder*).After you start *submit_issue.exe*, the**Report an Issue** dialog opens.

In this dialog, you can easily dump threads, memory heap, or memory histogram into files and provide those files when reporting an issue to the support team.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Report an Issue dialog in the offline mode

First of all, select the specific instance of the modeling tool to dump reports from. Then, click one of the following:

- **Dump Threads to Log File** - threads will be dumped into a Log file.
- **Dump Memory Heap to File** - memory heap will be dumped to a new .hprof file.
- **Dump Memory Histogram to File** - a memory histogram will be dumped to a new .txt file. To use the **Dump Memory Histogram to File** functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.

###### View and Submit Internal Errors

Internal errors are displayed as notifications by default. The internal error does not necessarily mean damage to your data but can be a reason for unexpected tool behavior. We highly recommend submitting internal errors to No Magic for further investigation. We strongly recommend attaching the log file. An internal error message appears at the bottom of the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Internal Error message in the modeling tool

To view internal errors

To view internal errors you must open the **Internal Errors** dialog, using one of the two methods outlined below:

- Click the View internal errors button in the Notification Window.
- Click the notification icon on the status line.

To submit an error

1. Open the Internal Errors dialog.
2. Click the Export To File button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Specify the name and location of the error log file and click Export .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC'] and attach the exported error log file.

Internal errors will not be shown for 24 hours (or until you restart your modeling tool) after clicking the **Clear And Close** buttons.

To Display Internal Errors

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment options dialog will open.
2. In the General options group , click Internal Errors.
3. In the Internal Errors options list change the Display Internal Errors option value to true to display internal errors.

1428101705

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']

Send notifications of a problem, suggest improvements for our modeling tools, or ask about new features. </span></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bb4ad24-062c-4a01-8ff1-2f0000496544"><ac:parameter ac:name="">offline</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Report an issue when the program is unresponsive</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If you encounter an issue or the modeling tool becomes unresponsive, a separately executable tool is provided for analyzing the status of the process to aid in bug submission. </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In these situations, manually start the <em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">submit_issue.exe</span></em> file (located in the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin folder</em>). </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">After you start <em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">submit_issue.exe</span></em>, the </span><strong>Report an Issue</strong> dialog <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">opens. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In this dialog, you can easily dump threads, memory heap, or memory histogram into files and provide those files when reporting an issue to the support team.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_an_issue_offline_dialog.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Report an Issue dialog in the offline mode</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">First of all, select the specific instance of the modeling tool to dump reports from. Then, click one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Dump Threads to Log File</strong> - threads will be dumped into a Log file.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Dump Memory Heap to File</strong> - memory heap will be dumped to a new .hprof file.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Dump Memory Histogram to File</strong> - a memory histogram will be dumped to a new .txt file. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd25920a-a29b-47bd-815e-fca56b11154a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the <strong>Dump Memory Histogram to File</strong> functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><h4>View and Submit Internal Errors</h4><p>Internal errors are displayed as notifications by default. The internal error does not necessarily mean damage to your data but can be a reason for unexpected tool behavior. We highly recommend submitting internal errors to No Magic for further investigation. We strongly recommend attaching the log file. An internal error message appears at the bottom of the modeling tool. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_error_message_.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of Internal Error message in the modeling tool</h6><p>To view internal errors</p><hr /><p>To view internal errors you must open the <strong>Internal Errors</strong> dialog, using one of the two methods outlined below:</p><ul><li>Click the <strong>View</strong> <strong>internal errors</strong> button in the Notification Window.</li><li>Click the <strong>notification</strong> icon on the status line.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To submit an error</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Internal Errors </strong>dialog.</li><li>Click the <strong>Export To File</strong> button.<br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_errors.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Specify the name and location of the error log file and click<strong> Export</strong>.</li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report an issue to the customer support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and attach the exported error log file. </li></ol><p>Internal errors will not be shown for 24 hours (or until you restart your modeling tool) after clicking the <strong>Clear And Close</strong> buttons.</p><p><br /></p><p>To Display Internal Errors</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment options </strong>dialog will open.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options group<strong>, </strong>click <strong>Internal Errors.</strong></li><li>In the <strong>Internal Errors</strong> options list change the <strong>Display Internal Errors</strong> option value to <em>true</em> to display internal errors.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03742df4-e780-4c06-9cb7-879ea15dd4af"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428101705</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677505 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00955: Representation of Teamwork Cloud users

- page_id: `169677505`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677505/Representation+of+Teamwork+Cloud+users
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:26.600+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to have the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1']**List All Users** permission assigned to change the user representation.

You can customize how users are represented when, for example, they lock model elements by changing the value of the**Representation of Users**environment option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.

A *User Name* is shown by default in the following cases:

- the corresponding fields (e.g., Full name) are not filled in the Web Application Platform.
- the List All Users permission is not assigned to the user.

To choose how to represent users

1. In the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. In the panel on the left, select Collaboration .
3. Under General , click Representation of Users . [ATTACHMENT filename='representation_of_users_environment_option.png']
4. Select to represent users by displaying one of the following:
  - User Name
  - Full Name
  - Email
5. When you are done, click OK .
6. Reload an open server project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d54b005c-9d28-482b-b1b5-610b7d06084f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the <ac:link ac:anchor="List All Users permission"><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:link-body><strong>List All Users</strong> permission</ac:link-body></ac:link> assigned to change the user representation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="727d6f66-a4ae-4f97-b8bd-c2f0c8e06225">customize</ac:inline-comment-marker> how users are represented when, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e147007c-5efc-458d-a703-8e4a286136ed">for example, they lock model elements by changing the value of the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e147007c-5efc-458d-a703-8e4a286136ed">Representation of Users</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e147007c-5efc-458d-a703-8e4a286136ed"> environment option.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_users.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Representation of Teamwork Cloud users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.</h6><p>A <em>User Name</em> is shown by default in the following cases:</p><ul><li>the corresponding fields (e.g., Full name) are not filled in the Web Application Platform. </li><li>the <strong>List All Users</strong> permission is not assigned to the user.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To choose how to represent users </p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the panel on the left, select <strong>Collaboration</strong>.</li><li>Under <strong>General</strong>, click <strong>Representation of Users</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_users_environment_option.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Representation of Teamwork Cloud users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select to represent users by displaying one of the following:<ul><li>User Name </li><li>Full Name</li><li>Email</li></ul></li><li>When you are done, click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e47549c-9a73-4665-9f16-79a42f4344d8">Reload an open server project.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675533 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00956: Representing Use Case scenarios in Activity diagrams

- page_id: `169675533`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675533/Representing+Use+Case+scenarios+in+Activity+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-04-09T10:25:39.307+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879905382

879905384

879905383

After you have created a Use Case scenario, you can represent this scenario in an Activity diagram. The following procedure describes how to represent the Use Case scenario in the Activity diagram and how to turn the automatic layout on or off in the Activity diagram when representing the Use Case scenario.

To represent a Use Case scenario in an Activity diagram

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. Click the Open Activity Diagram button [ATTACHMENT filename='open_and_update_button.png'] . The Activity diagram opens with the Use Case scenario represented on it.

You need to reopen the Activity diagram each time after you make modifications to the Use Case scenario in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. Modifications to the Activity diagram where the Use Case scenario is represented automatically appear in the textual Use Case scenario.

For Use Cases having read-only accessibility, the use Case Scenario cannot be represented on the Activity diagram.

In the Activity diagram, all symbols are laid out automatically every time the diagram is opened.

To leave symbols in the same place while opening the Activity diagram after modifying the Use Case scenario in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'], change the **Layout use case scenario activity diagram**option value to *false*. This option appears in the **Project Options**dialog, in the **General project options**group.

Your modeling tool might not be able to interpret the Use Case scenario in some cases. These are the most common reasons why:

| There is no merge node or call behavior action after the element. |
| --- |
| There is no initial node in Activity. |
| There is no final node in Activity. |
| There are no outgoing control flows from the element. |
| There is no merge node after the element. |
| There are no outgoing flows from the element. |
| There is no alternative flow step after the element. |
| Cyclic scenario. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Use Case Scenario cannot be interpreted

879905381

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678525 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00957: Reset to defaults option

- page_id: `169678525`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678525/Reset+to+defaults+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:25:17.881+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to reset data to the default settings, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Reset to Defaults button.

- The modeling tool allows you to configure the report engine settings in three levels: (i) Environment option, (ii) global config.xml , and (iii) template config.xml .
- The template config.xml configuration settings will override the environment option configuration settings, which will override the global config.xml configuration settings.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to reset data to the default settings, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Reset to Defaults Button" ac:alt="Reset to Defaults Button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportOption9.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reset to defaults option" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The Reset to Defaults button.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c6b50cc-101c-4966-a939-4cc55e249b80"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The modeling tool allows you to configure the report engine settings in three levels: (i) Environment option, (ii) global <em>config.xml</em>, and (iii) template <em>config.xml</em>.</li><li>The template<em> config.xml</em> configuration settings will override the environment option configuration settings, which will override the global <em>config.xml</em> configuration settings.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678227 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00958: Resizing fixed-measurement images

- page_id: `169678227`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678227/Resizing+fixed-measurement+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:11:43.516+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050277646

2050277651

2050277644

**On this page**

43

2050277650

There are six methods to specify the resulting dimensions in terms of measurements to resize an image, for example, in inch (in), centimeter (cm), millimeter (mm), point (pt), or pixel (px)], and specifying the dot-per-inch (*DPI*) value. Every measurement parameter must be specified in a pair of quote marks.

###### **$image.setSize(image, measureWidth, measureHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in terms of measurements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Parameter(s) | measureWidth | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | measureHeight | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, measureSize)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height in terms of measurement while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, measureSize, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, measureSize)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width in a term of measurement, while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, measureSize, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setDPI(dotsPerInches)**

This method is used to set the resolution (dpi) of images. The default value is 96 dpi. The dpi value will only affect the methods that take inches, centimeters, and millimeters as their parameters.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | dotsPerInches | java.lang.Integer | Image resolution (dpi). |
| Return | - | - | - |

Use **$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘1.5in’, ‘2in’)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 1.5 inches and height to 2 inches. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set size function.

Use either **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’)** or **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, true)**, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set height function.

Use **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, false)**, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set height function.

Use **$image.setDPI(192)** and then **$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘0.9in’, ‘1.1in’)**, for example, to set the resolution (dpi) of the image to 192 dots per inch, and then resize the image width to 0.9 inches and height to 1.1 inches. The resulting image size will become 173x211 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set DPI function.

Instead of using **$image.setDPI(192)**, if you set the resolution (dpi) to 144 using **$image.setDPI(144)**, the resulting image size will become 130x158 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set DPI function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1dc9f2dc-529c-4ba4-ab62-e13e9d007e5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277646</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ae3e954-0fbd-4296-8aa6-133aa7ef0489"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277651</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d66e770a-d557-40f6-a22e-a8fdef742c2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2020b523-34e2-4606-94ef-8f2bf2525626"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050277650</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are six methods to specify the resulting dimensions in terms of measurements to resize an image, for example, in inch (in), centimeter (cm), millimeter (mm), point (pt), or pixel (px)], and specifying the dot-per-inch (<em>DPI</em>) value. Every measurement parameter must be specified in a pair of quote marks.</p><h4><strong>$image.setSize(image, measureWidth, measureHeight)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in terms of measurements.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object <br />for the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>measureWidth </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The width of the image <br />in a term of measurement.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureHeight</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The height of the image <br />in a term of measurement.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image object <br />for the element icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setHeight(image, measureSize)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height in terms of measurement while maintaining the image aspect ratio.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The height of the <br />image in a term <br />of measurement. </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setHeight(image, measureSize, keepRatio)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image  </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The height of the <br />image in a term <br />of measurement.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>keepRatio</td><td>java.lang.Boolean </td><td>A flag to maintain <br />the image aspect ratio. </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setWidth(image, measureSize)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width in a term of measurement, while maintaining the image aspect ratio.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>The width of the <br />image in a term of <br />measurement.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setWidth(image, measureSize, keepRatio)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained (depending on the keepRatio parameter).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object <br />for the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>measureSize </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The width of the <br />image in a term of <br />measurement. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>keepRatio</td><td>java.lang.Boolean  </td><td>A flag to maintain the <br />image aspect ratio.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element <br />icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.setDPI(dotsPerInches)</strong></h4><p>This method is used to set the resolution (dpi) of images. The default value is 96 dpi. The dpi value will only affect the methods that take inches, centimeters, and millimeters as their parameters.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>dotsPerInches </td><td>java.lang.Integer </td><td>Image resolution (dpi). </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>- </td><td>- </td><td>- </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>Use <strong>$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘1.5in’, ‘2in’)</strong>, for example, to resize the image’s width to 1.5 inches and height to 2 inches. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.1.setSize1.5inx2in.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resizing fixed-measurement images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set size function.</h6><p>Use either <strong>$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’)</strong> or <strong>$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, true)</strong>, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.2.setHeight45mm.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resizing fixed-measurement images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set height function.</h6><p>Use <strong>$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, false)</strong>, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.3.setHeight45mmfalse.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resizing fixed-measurement images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set height function.</h6><p>Use <strong>$image.setDPI(192)</strong> and then <strong>$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘0.9in’, ‘1.1in’)</strong>, for example, to set the resolution (dpi) of the image to 192 dots per inch, and then resize the image width to 0.9 inches and height to 1.1 inches. The resulting image size will become 173x211 pixels. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="5.1.dpi192.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resizing fixed-measurement images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set DPI function.</h6><p>Instead of using <strong>$image.setDPI(192)</strong>, if you set the resolution (dpi) to 144 using <strong>$image.setDPI(144)</strong>, the resulting image size will become 130x158 pixels. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="5.2.dpi144.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resizing fixed-measurement images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image set DPI function.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678223 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00959: Resizing fixed-pixel images

- page_id: `169678223`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678223/Resizing+fixed-pixel+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:10:02.796+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050270131

2050270136

2050270129

**On this page**

43

2050270135

There are five methods to specify the resulting dimensions for an image to be resized in pixel size (the size parameters must be positive numbers or -1 number). If the value is -1, it will resize an image to the document paper dimensions. For example:

```text
$image.setWidth($diagram.image, -1)
```

As shown by the example, the image will be resized to a paper width while maintaining the aspect ratio. The value -1 applies only to certain template types such as *RTF*, *ODT*, and *DOCX*. If the value is -2, it will resize an image to document paper bounds if and only if image bounds are larger than paper bounds. For example:

```text
$image.setWidth($diagram.image, -2)
```

Using the value -2 also maintains the image aspect ratio. However, it can only be applied to certain template types such as *RTF*, *ODT*, and *DOCX*.

****

###### **$image.setSize(image, sizeWidth, sizeHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in pixels.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | sizeWidth | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
|  | sizeHeight | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, size)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (in pixels), while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, size, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (in pixels) and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, size)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (in pixels), while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, size, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (in pixels) and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.setSize($diagram.image, 200, 200)**, for example, to resize the image’s width and height to 200 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set size function.

Use either **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150)** or **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150, true)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 150 pixels and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set width function.

Use **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150, false)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 150 pixels and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set width function.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675950 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00960: Resolving not synchronized elements

- page_id: `169675950`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675950/Resolving+not+synchronized+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-05-26T09:30:44.659+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Parameters synchronization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The active validation functionality marks the not synchronized elements in the Model Browser and highlights the shapes on the diagram pane. For more information about the active validation and solving the incorrect model, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1'].

In addition, you can solve the broken parameters synchronization by using the **Synchronization** dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The active validation functionality marks the not synchronized elements in the Model Browser and highlights the shapes on the diagram pane. For more information about the active validation and solving the incorrect model, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link>.</p><p>In addition, you can solve the broken parameters synchronization by using the <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673688 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00961: Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages

- page_id: `169673688`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673688/Resolving+unconfirmed+usages+of+packages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:50.529+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Model Usages

1020795580

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Model Usages

1020795582

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Model Usages

1020795581

For the overview of automated project usages and situations when they can become unconfirmed, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Controlling dependency creation between used projects' space='MD2024xR1'].

Unconfirmed project usages are caught by special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated project usages' space='MD2024xR1'] and displayed as a warning or error validation result. The severity depends on the exact model composition situation, but the solution methods are the same in both cases.

#### INFO: Model Usages

Model Usages

Model

#### WARNING: Model Usages

Model Usages

These recovered elements are in turn flagged as errors. Hence single unconfirmed usage can cause a lot of error validation results: one for the unconfirmed usage itself (rule abbreviation - NCAMU) and one for each recovered element (rule abbreviation - REF). In this situation, the unconfirmed usage result should be examined first, because solving it may automatically solve all other results.

There are two ways to resolve an unconfirmed used project usage. The usage can be either confirmed or rejected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two ways for resolving unconfirmed used project usages.

###### Confirming usages

If the used project usage A → B is good and necessary according to the user policy, it can be confirmed, i.e., the user-defined usage is to be created instead of the current unconfirmed automated used project usage.

You can confirm an automated usage

- By using the specific command
- Manually

To confirm an automated usage by using the specific command

1. Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.
2. Select Confirm and use the used project into <used project_name> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024xR1']**Used project** wizard opens. The required used project is already selected in the first step of the wizard.
3. Click Finish. The necessary user-defined used project usage will be created.

To confirm an automated usage manually

#### INFO: Example

Example

In this case, you can better control the usage creation process. So if you need, for example, to use not only a required used project but some other one that brings the required used project as a part of it, be advised to confirm the usage manually.

For example, let’s say there are three unconfirmed usages from the project *RadioSet* to used projects *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances.* Instead of confirming each usage, you can create just one user-defined usage *RadioSet → ElectricComponents*, since the used project *ElectricComponents* brings in *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances* as sub-used projects.

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

However, this is the case when you must be sure that the user-defined usage will be created in the right place.

1. In the main menu, click Options > Used Projects . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR1']**U****sed Projects** dialog opens.
2. Select a used project that is the usage target resource.
3. Click the Used project button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024xR1']**Used Project** wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the usual procedure (select a required used project and finish or continue to the second wizard step to provide more configuration options for used project usage and then finish).

****

**Rejecting usages**

If the usage A → B is not good according to the user policy, it must be rejected and removed.

You can reject an automated usage

- By using the specific command (for removing underlying model level references)
- Manually (by removing or redirecti ng underlying model level references to different elements)

To reject an automated usage by using the specific command

#### INFO: Model Usages

Model Usages

Use this way to reject a model-level usage that, for example, was created inadvertently.

1. Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.
2. Select Remove underlying model references from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). All underlying model-level references (the ones that cause this automated usage) will be removed.

Clear Recovered Element Usages

To remove an automated usage manually

In more complex cases, you may want to address each usage individually. Use dependency analysis means to examine what dependencies are there and review them on a case-by-case basis (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR1'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024xR1'], and so forth).

**Useful commands for preparing to resolve unconfirmed used project usages**

When you need to explore your project composition in detail before attempting to resolve unconfirmed usages, use the following commands for navigating:

- Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Used project management' space='MD2024xR1']
- Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] for collaboration projects

1020807231

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="462182ba-0b2f-4308-8aab-ecd778a562b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1020795580</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72cf0dc5-66d9-4e9a-a1b2-99240b2de703"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1020795582</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d7119ed-65ae-439b-9c36-5b4d12a8ee22"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1020795581</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the overview of automated project usages and situations when they can become unconfirmed, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Controlling dependency creation between used projects" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Unconfirmed project usages are caught by special <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Automated project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatic validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and displayed as a warning or error validation result. The severity depends on the exact model composition situation, but the solution methods are the same in both cases.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdb65676-3118-4232-ab6f-8a74c6d5464e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Since problematic project usages are not model elements, they cannot be visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. So when the model contains unconfirmed usages, the root package <em>Model </em>is highlighted as incorrect (regardless of which usage is actually problematic).</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2245b057-e895-473e-9238-a082c9bdf592"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When there is an error level unconfirmed usage of some used project, then all the elements of the used project, that are referenced from other model places are shown as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing unresolved references" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Recovered Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">These recovered elements are in turn flagged as errors. Hence single unconfirmed usage can cause a lot of error validation results: one for the unconfirmed usage itself (rule abbreviation - NCAMU) and one for each recovered element (rule abbreviation - REF). In this situation, the unconfirmed usage result should be examined first, because solving it may automatically solve all other results. </span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>There are two ways to resolve an unconfirmed used project usage. The usage can be either confirmed or rejected.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="two_ways_for_resolving_unconfirmed_usages.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Two ways for resolving unconfirmed used project usages.</h6><h4>Confirming usages</h4><p>If the used project usage A → B is good and necessary according to the user policy, it can be confirmed, i.e., the user-defined usage is to be created instead of the current unconfirmed automated used project usage.</p><p>You can confirm an automated usage</p><ul><li>By using the specific command</li><li>Manually</li></ul><p>To confirm an automated usage by using the specific command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.</li><li>Select <strong>Confirm</strong> <strong>and use the used project into &lt;used project_name&gt;</strong> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used project</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. The required used project is already selected in the first step of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish.</strong> The necessary user-defined used project usage will be created. </li></ol><p>To confirm an automated usage manually</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bdde69e-abc7-48e4-b330-b66f9f20e958"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this case, you can better control the usage creation process. So if you need, for example, to use not only a required used project but some other one that brings the required used project as a part of it, be advised to confirm the usage manually.</p><p>For example, let’s say there are three unconfirmed usages from the project <em>RadioSet</em> to used projects <em>Capacitors, Inductances,</em> and <em>Resistances.</em> Instead of confirming each usage, you can create just one user-defined usage <em>RadioSet → ElectricComponents</em>, since the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> brings in <em>Capacitors, Inductances,</em> and <em>Resistances</em> as sub-used projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="972dce95-3005-401a-a44c-16d441e602d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important!</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>However, this is the case when you must be sure that the user-defined usage will be created in the right place.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Used Projects</strong> . The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>U</strong><strong>sed Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Select a used project that is the usage target resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Used project</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Project</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the usual procedure (select a required used project and finish or continue to the second wizard step to provide more configuration options for used project usage and then finish). </li></ol><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Rejecting usages</strong></p><p>If the usage A → B is not good according to the user policy, it must be rejected and removed.</p><p>You can reject an automated usage</p><ul><li>By using the specific command (for removing underlying model level references)</li><li><span>Manually (by removing or redirecti</span>ng underlying model level references to different elements)</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To reject an automated usage by using the specific command</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d8a46b3-8f7e-4354-a8f0-b4e5c7e419f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Use this way to reject a model-level usage that, for example, was created inadvertently.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove underlying model references</strong> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). All underlying model-level references (the ones that cause this automated usage) will be removed.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="045cf87d-638f-4e8b-a934-4e3b5b14322e"><ac:rich-text-body>This command is very similar to the <strong>Clear Recovered Element Usages</strong> command.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove an automated usage manually</p><hr /><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In more complex cases, you may want to address each usage individually. Use dependency analysis means to examine what dependencies are there and review them on a case-by-case basis (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Analyzing package dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependency matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and so forth).</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Useful commands for preparing to resolve unconfirmed used project usages</span></strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you need to explore your project composition in detail before attempting to resolve unconfirmed usages, use the following commands for navigating:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Open <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Used project management" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></li><li>Open <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resouce Usage Map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for collaboration projects </li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="478c7e5d-d3be-4dd5-9703-8a9c9766850c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1020807231</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Analyzing Package Dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Using other Projects in a Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation"
````
