following figure). Different colors help identify new, modified, and deleted values. You can analyze exact changes in the textual value of the selected property in the <strong>Text Diff</strong> box. If you want to see the original texts of both versions, click the Compare property values button <ac:image ac:title="Compare property values" ac:alt="Compare property values"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_values_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the dialog to open the <strong>Compare property</strong> dialog and then click <strong>Original Texts</strong>.</p><p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_diff_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of the Quick Diff dialog of an element (class).</h6><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2828fb39-eb48-4699-8915-a9572fffd161"><ac:parameter ac:name="">FullDiff</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>After you select the <strong>Full Diff</strong> command for an element, the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> dialog opens, showing changes in element property values, as well as detailed information for directly related elements and new relationships (see the figure below). For more information about using this dialog, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing comparison results" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="full_diff_viewer_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of the Difference Viewer dialog of an element.</h6><p>After you select the <strong>Full Diff</strong> command for a diagram, the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> dialog opens, showing all changes in the diagram (see the figure below). For more information about using this dialog, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Viewing and analyzing differences of modified diagrams" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_differences.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">An example of the Difference Viewer dialog of a diagram.</span> </h6><p>Be aware that <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ddc4eb3f-a87a-41a0-80be-ee73928d3ce6">Quick Diff is not available for displaying diagram changes. Diagram changes (as diagram layout changes) can be displayed only in the <strong>Full Diff </strong>mode. </ac:inline-comment-marker>To see these changes you must initiate the comparison of entire project versions. For this, select a couple of project versions in the <strong>History</strong> panel and on the shortcut menu click<strong> Compare Projects</strong>. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1988b8de-75bc-4216-a4f1-1f6fc7c3d2bd">For</ac:inline-comment-marker> more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing comparison results" /></ac:link>.</p><h3>Generating reports</h3><p>To generate an element history report</p><hr /><ol><li>Perform element history as described above.</li><li>On the <strong>History</strong> toolbar, click the<strong> </strong>Generate Report button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element_history.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>The <strong>Save As </strong>dialog opens to specify the location of the report file.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c46d9ddc-f9eb-4c61-a47a-be58c039c7b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358543554</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing model history" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing comparison results" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677381 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00972: Reviewing historical versions of the project

- page_id: `169677381`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677381/Reviewing+historical+versions+of+the+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T14:11:01.104+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Reviewing model history
- labels: ['history-dialog', 'out-of-sync', 'set-as-latest']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To review historical versions of a Teamwork Cloud project

1. From the Collaborate menu, select Projects or Open Server Project . The Manage Projects or Open Server Project dialog opens appropriately.
2. Select the project, whose historical versions you want to review.
3. Do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='project_history.png'] on the toolbar of the open project.
  - Right-click the selected project and from the shortcut menu, select [ATTACHMENT filename='project_history.png'] Project History .
4. In the **History** dialog, review a list of all versions of the selected project. If a version has tags, you can see them next to the version number. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reviewing versions of a project.

#### TIP: Search bar

Search bar

Use the search bar to filter projects according to:

- Project Version (together with the commit tags)
- Author
- Comment

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Project version | The version number of a project. In the example above, the last project version number is denoted as “3/1”. This is the result of setting a previous project version as the newest one. In this particular example, “3” denotes the current project version number and “1” denotes the number of the version, which has been set as the newest one. |
| Author | The login name of the user who created the project version. |
| Date | The date and time the project version was created. |
| Comment | The description of changes in the version. You can make a version comment when committing a version. |
| Button | Description |
| Set As Latest | Set the selected project version as the latest version in the branch.You must have the read-write permission for the branch in which you set the selected project version as the latest version. |
| Set As Latest > Select Branch... | Set a particular version as the latest in the specified branch.To access this feature, click the drop-down arrow next to the Set As Latest button to view the Select Branch... button: |
| Compare | Compares two selected branches. If there are differences between project branches, the result is presented in the Difference Viewer dialog. The button is available only when two project versions are selected (use the SHIFT or CTRL keys). |
| Properties | Opens Version Properties dialog. |
| Open | Open the selected version of the project. You can make changes only to the latest version of the project. Earlier versions are read-only. |
| Cancel | Closes the dialog. |
| Help | Documentation for the project version will be opened. |

Also, you can review comments and tags of any historical version as well as edit or add new tags to it and set that version as the latest one. 
 
To review comments and tags

1. In the History dialog, select a project version, whose properties you need to review and edit.
2. Click the Properties button. The Version Properties dialog opens with version comments displayed by default.
3. If you need to review and edit version tags, click the Tag tab. After the Tab opens, do ether:
  - If you need to edit a tag, select it and click Edit .
  - If you need to add a new tag, click Add .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677379 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00973: Reviewing model history

- page_id: `169677379`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677379/Reviewing+model+history
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:25.927+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every time a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR1'], a new version of that project is created. Each project can have many [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing historical versions of the project' space='MD2024xR1']. Versions can be tagged. 
The number of project version is shown in the title of the client application window - see the highlighted area in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project version number is highlighted.

Every element in the model, including diagrams, has its own history, and you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing element history and inspecting changes' space='MD2024xR1'] it in any open project.

A new version of the element is created once you modify element properties, for example, update the documentation, change the multiplicity or type of the element, create a new attribute or operation. A new version of the diagram is correspondingly created after you modify its properties, for example, rearrange the diagram layout.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing content history' space='MD2024xR1'] at the element level - that is a selected package or a classifier element (a composed element). The latter is a big benefit for system engineers as now you can see the history of, e. g., a selected[Block](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR1/Block),[Requirement](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR1/Requirement), or other composed element.

While modeling, you can analyze model history by

````html
<p>Every time a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project is committed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, a new version of that project is created. Each project can have many <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing historical versions of the project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[historical versions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Versions can be tagged.<br />The number of project version is shown in the title of the client application window - see the highlighted area in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="version_number.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing model history" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The project version number is highlighted.</span></h6><p>Every element in the model, including diagrams, has its own history, and you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[review]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it in any open project.</p><p>A new version of the element is created once you modify element properties, for example, update the documentation, change the multiplicity or type of the element, create a new attribute or operation. A new version of the diagram is correspondingly created after you modify its properties, for example, rearrange the diagram layout.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reviewing content history" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[view changes in the model in a particular scope]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> at the element level - that is a selected package or a classifier element (a composed element). The latter is a big benefit for system engineers as now you can see the history of, e. g., a selected </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR1/Block">Block</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR1/Requirement">Requirement</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, or other composed element.</span></p><p>While modeling, you can analyze model history by</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d40f8338-4e2c-42e3-ae78-b7b3ffcab65b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674067 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00974: Robustness diagram

- page_id: `169674067`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674067/Robustness+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:35:56.583+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

373764986

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

373764988

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

373764987

#### NOTE: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Robustness Analysis involves analyzing the narrative text of use cases, identifying a first-guess set of objects that participate in those use cases and classifying the objects based on the roles they play.

- Boundary or Interface objects are what actors use in communicating with the system.
- Entity objects are mostly objects from the domain model.
- Control objects (known as controllers because they often are not real objects) serve as the "glue" between boundary objects and entity objects.

The Robustness Analysis acts as a preliminary design within the project lifecycle, providing the missing link between an analysis and a detailed design.

Four basic rules apply:

1. Actors can only talk to the boundary objects.
2. The boundary objects can only talk to the controllers and actors.
3. The entity objects can only talk to the controllers.
4. The controllers can talk to the boundary objects and entity objects, and to other controllers, but not to the actors.

Both the boundary objects and entity objects are nouns; the controllers are verbs. Nouns cannot talk to other nouns, but verbs can talk to either nouns or verbs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the Robustness diagram *Logging In*

373764985

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extension diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']

between boundary objects and entity objects.</li></ul><p>The Robustness Analysis acts as a preliminary design within the project lifecycle, providing the missing link between an analysis and a detailed design.</p><p>Four basic rules apply:</p><ol><li>Actors can only talk to the boundary objects.</li><li>The boundary objects can only talk to the controllers and actors.</li><li>The entity objects can only talk to the controllers.</li><li>The controllers can talk to the boundary objects and entity objects, and to other controllers, but not to the actors.</li></ol><p>Both the boundary objects and entity objects are nouns; the controllers are verbs. Nouns cannot talk to other nouns, but verbs can talk to either nouns or verbs.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-13 at 12.07.56 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Robustness diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of the Robustness diagram <em>Logging In</em></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52511bf1-358a-4c85-900c-540aa8e272a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">373764985</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Extension diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extension diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ad1d5667-7503-48cd-bd11-ffd59bf9b032" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00976: Rotating images

- page_id: `169678218`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678218/Rotating+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:08:29.283+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050254487

2050254501

2050254485

**On this page**

43

2050254491

There are two rotating methods that you can use to rotate a given image by 90 degrees: (i) **rotateRight** for clockwise rotation and (ii) **rotateLeft** for counterclockwise rotation.

###### **$image.rotateRight(image)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The rotated image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.rotateLeft(image)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The rotated image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.rotateRight($diagram.image)**, for example, to rotate the image 90 degrees clockwise. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image rotate right function.

Use **$image.rotateLeft($diagram.image)**, for example, to rotate the image 90 degrees counterclockwise. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image rotate left function.

RTF Image rotation, with the scaling quality set to 'highest,' is not supported in any RTF document when opened with [OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="id">2050254487</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a773f51-12ea-4996-8790-2fc2e7fb2560"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050254501</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06cd6f35-7bdb-4838-9c4e-2e9733670d04"><ac:parameter ac:macro-id="f716663b-ac5c-494d-99d9-c6ce1117522e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050254491</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are two rotating methods that you can use to rotate a given image by 90 degrees: (i) <strong>rotateRight</strong> for clockwise rotation and (ii) <strong>rotateLeft</strong> for counterclockwise rotation.</p><h4><strong>$image.rotateRight(image)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td> com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object <br />for the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td> com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The rotated image <br />object for the element <br />icon. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.rotateLeft(image)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td> com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The rotated image <br />object for the element <br />icon. </td></tr></tbody></table><p>Use <strong>$image.rotateRight($diagram.image)</strong>, for example, to rotate the image 90 degrees clockwise. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="2.1.rotateRight.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Rotating images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image rotate right function.</h6><p>Use <strong>$image.rotateLeft($diagram.image)</strong>, for example, to rotate the image 90 degrees counterclockwise. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="2.2.rotateLeft.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Rotating images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image rotate left function.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1073661c-ee02-4cd9-be14-4fd12809a6f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>RTF Image rotation, with the scaling quality set to 'highest,' is not supported in any RTF document when opened with <a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679260 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00977: Round Trip

- page_id: `169679260`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679260/Round+Trip
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:22:44.563+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Global options for Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419414421

419414423

419414422

The Round Trip feature keeps your code and model synchronized. Because Round Trip traces all model and code changes, you can freely change entity specifications without discarding code changes made outside the tool.

For example, Round Trip prevents a job from being damaged by code editions or changes when the following steps are performed:

1. A Class Base is created within the tool.
2. The Operation getInstance is added to the Class.
3. Code is generated.
4. With an external tool, a programmer adds some code to that operation.
5. With a modeling tool, a name of the operation is changed to Instance .
6. Code is generated.

If the tool rewrites the whole code, these changes are made without corrupting the programmer’s job. The name of the operation is changed, but the internals remain the same.

Round Trip catches all changes in your project and controls the following actions:

- If the source code is not changed, you are not allowed to refresh the UML model. The Refresh command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable.
- If the model is changed but the code remains the same (new members were added or their headers were changed), refresh is not allowed, and the Refresh command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable. When generating code according to changes, all changes in the model are written to the signatures of class members, leaving the old implementation in place.
- If the code is changed but the model remains the same, refresh can be executed; code will reverse to the UML models. If the Code Generation Options dialog box appears when you attempt to generate code, you can select a code action that differs from the UML model.
- I f the code and model are changed while refreshing, all changes in the code are treated as new items and added to the model.
- If data in the model file is deleted, it will be restored while refreshing, even when the code has not been changed or the data itself is unimportant.

419414420

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global options for Code Engineering' space='MD2024xR1']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Because Round Trip traces all model and code changes, you can freely change entity specifications without discarding code changes made outside the tool.</p><p>For example, Round Trip prevents a job from being damaged by code editions or changes when the following steps are performed:</p><ol><li>A Class <em>Base</em> is created within the tool.</li><li>The Operation <em>getInstance</em> is added to the Class.</li><li>Code is generated.</li><li>With an external tool, a programmer adds some code to that operation.</li><li>With a modeling tool, a name of the operation is changed to <em>Instance</em>.</li><li>Code is generated.</li></ol><p>If the tool rewrites the whole code, these changes are made without corrupting the programmer’s job. The name of the operation is changed, but the internals remain the same.</p><p>Round Trip catches all changes in your project and controls the following actions:</p><ul><li>If the source code is not changed, you are not allowed to refresh the UML model. The <strong>Refresh</strong> command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable.</li><li>If the model is changed but the code remains the same (new members were added or their headers were changed), refresh is not allowed, and the <strong>Refresh</strong> command from the set shortcut menu is unavailable. When generating code according to changes, all changes in the model are written to the signatures of class members, leaving the old implementation in place.</li><li>If the code is changed but the model remains the same, refresh can be executed; code will reverse to the UML models. If the <strong>Code Generation Options</strong> dialog box appears when you attempt to generate code, you can select a code action that differs from the UML model.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(90,109,148);">I</span>f the code and model are changed while refreshing, all changes in the code are treated as new items and added to the model.</li><li>If data in the model file is deleted, it will be restored while refreshing, even when the code has not been changed or the data itself is unimportant. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99c3f05f-8cc4-4cdc-80cb-3541cfbaa5b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419414420</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ce106958-b2cc-43fc-b69e-a24c7bc6d31d"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Global options for Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2571bc6d-81a9-4890-b937-dd03baf03e2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00978: Routing paths

- page_id: `169674743`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674743/Routing+paths
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-23T15:53:26.593+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['route-paths-rectilinearly', 'route-paths']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To route paths using the **Route Paths Rectilinearly** button, do either

- Select a path on the diagram pane and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='route_paths_rectilinearly.png'] . The selected path is routed rectilinearly.
- Select a shape on the diagram pane and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='route_paths_rectilinearly.png'] . All the paths that are connected to the shape are routed rectilinearly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00979: Row and column element shortcut menu

- page_id: `169674028`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674028/Row+and+column+element+shortcut+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-02-28T13:56:43.945+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open a row/column element shortcut menu

- Right-click the appropriate row/column.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Command (Shortcut key) | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Specification | Opens the element’s . |
| Select in Containment Tree | Selects the element in the of the . |
| Go To | Finds and navigates to a diagram which is owned by the selected element, or to the one, wherein the element shape is drawn, or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship.For more information, see, |
| Related Elements | Opens the dialog for defining options to search for specified usages or dependencies of the selected element.For more information, see . |
| Refactor | the selected element to an element indicated by the user. |
| Tools | Opens a list of that are available for the selected element. |
| Up (Ctrl+Up Arrow) | Moves selected rows (either grouped or non-grouped) up. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more rows. |
| Down (Ctrl+Down Arrow) | Moves selected rows (either grouped or non-grouped) down. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more rows. |
| Left (Ctrl+Left Arrow) | Moves selected columns (either grouped or non-grouped) left. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more columns. |
| Right (Ctrl+Right Arrow) | Moves selected columns (either grouped or non-grouped) right. The command is available on the shortcut menu of one or more columns. |
| Sort Order | Point to the command and select the order for sorting rows or columns. It can be either Ascending or Descending. Selecting Custom does not affect the order of rows/columns. The Custom order is automatically selected after shifting at least one row or column to from one place to another.The sort order persists after changing the row or column owner's display mode, which can be defined by the Row Owner Display Mode or Column Owner Display Mode property in the matrix . |
| Remove from Matrix | Removes the selected element from the matrix. |
| Delete | Deletes the selected element from both the matrix and the model.If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679252 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00980: Rules of association or attribute creation on reverse

- page_id: `169679252`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679252/Rules+of+association+or+attribute+creation+on+reverse
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:09:10.733+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > Reverse Options
- labels: ['managing-association', 'managing-attribute', 'define-rules']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Sample of the association creation on reverse

419319165

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Sample of the association creation on reverse

419319167

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample of the association creation on reverse

419319166

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MD2024xR1'] creation on the reverse is the ability to enter rules to help decide if an association or attribute must be created on reverse.

To create association or attribute creation rules on reverse

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] , select the code engineering set for reverse.
2. Right-click the set and choose the Reverse command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reverse Options' space='MD2024xR1']**Reverse Options** dialog opens.
3. In the Create class fields as area, select According to rules and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button. The Class Field Creation Rules dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='class_field_creation_rules.png']
4. Click Add to add a rule.

Managing association or attribute creation on reverse rules

The **Class Field Creation Rules** dialog box lists the described rules. If a type qualified name matches any rule, a specified type is created. Rules described in this dialog are executed in order from top to bottom. If one rule matches, no others are executed. The dialog allows you to add a new rule, edit or remove an old one, and reorder rules.

- If no rules are matched, an attribute or association is created according the radio button choice at the bottom of the dialog.
- Click Add to add an expression.
- Click Edit to edit a selected rule.

To define rules

You can define rules in the **Rules** dialog. Click the **Add** or **Edit** button in the **Class Field Creation** dialog to open this dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Rules** dialog box description:

- In the Create drop-down list, choose Attribute or Association on reverse if a property matches a defined criterion.
- The Qualified Name text box supports simple search patterns, as well as '*' and '?' symbols.
- The Qualified Name pattern can contain a qualified name of the target element including a separator of a UML separator style (::). The qualified name is counted to the first package with the «ModelLibrary» stereotype.
- The Element Type drop-down list contains a subset of UML Type names: Any, Class, Interface, DataType, Primitive, Enumeration, Stereotype values are displayed.
- If the <UNSPECIFIED> value is selected in the Element Type drop-down list, the element type is matched by the qualified name expression.

#### TIP: Sample of the association creation on reverse

Sample of the association creation on reverse

These two samples show how to create associations among reversed classes, while avoiding creating associations to the used libraries (java).

If you place your classes inside the package “*com::myProduct*”, you must follow these Creation rules:

- Create Association, if the property type qualified name matches “ com::myProduct::* ” with “ all ” element type
- If the rules do not match, create class fields as attributes.

If you place your classes inside two packages “*myClient*” and “*myServer*”, the Creation rules should be:

- Create Association , if the type name “match” path “ myClient::* ” with an <UNDEFINED> meta type.
- Create Association , if the type name “match” path “ myServer::* ” with an <UNDEFINED> meta type.
- If the rules do not match, create class fields as attributes.

419319163

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reverse Options' space='MD2024xR1']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='MD2024xR1']

The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reverse Options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Reverse Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. </li><li>In the <strong>Create class fields as</strong> area, select <strong>According to rules</strong> and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Class Field Creation Rules</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_field_creation_rules.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Rules of association or attribute creation on reverse" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Add</strong> to add a rule.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Managing association or attribute creation on reverse rules</p><hr /><p>The <strong>Class Field Creation Rules</strong> dialog box lists the described rules. If a type qualified name matches any rule, a specified type is created. Rules described in this dialog are executed in order from top to bottom. If one rule matches, no others are executed. The dialog allows you to add a new rule, edit or remove an old one, and reorder rules.</p><ul><li>If no rules are matched, an attribute or association is created according the radio button choice at the bottom of the dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>Add</strong> to add an expression. </li><li>Click <strong>Edit</strong> to edit a selected rule.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To define rules</p><hr /><p>You can define rules in the <strong>Rules</strong> dialog. Click the <strong>Add</strong> or <strong>Edit</strong> button in the <strong>Class Field Creation</strong> dialog to open this dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="236"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rule_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Rules of association or attribute creation on reverse" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <strong>Rules</strong> dialog box description:</p><ul><li>In the <strong>Create</strong> drop-down list, choose<strong> Attribute</strong> or <strong>Association</strong> on reverse if a property matches a defined criterion.</li><li>The <strong>Qualified Name</strong> text box supports simple search patterns, as well as '*' and '?' symbols.</li><li>The <strong>Qualified Name</strong> pattern can contain a qualified name of the target element including a separator of a UML separator style (::). The qualified name is counted to the first package with the «ModelLibrary» stereotype.</li><li>The <strong>Element Type</strong> drop-down list contains a subset of UML Type names: Any, Class, Interface, DataType, Primitive, Enumeration, Stereotype values are displayed.</li><li>If the <strong>&lt;UNSPECIFIED&gt;</strong> value is selected in the <strong>Element Type</strong> drop-down list, the element type is matched by the qualified name expression.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3239f5d5-c6f8-4120-b06e-765857af0546"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of the association creation on reverse</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>These two samples show how to create associations among reversed classes, while avoiding creating associations to the used libraries (java).</p><p>If you place your classes inside the package “<em>com::myProduct</em>”, you must follow these Creation rules:</p><ul><li>Create Association, if the property type qualified name matches “<em>com::myProduct::*</em>” with “<em>all</em>” element type</li><li>If the rules do not match, create class fields as attributes.</li></ul><p>If you place your classes inside two packages “<em>myClient</em>” and “<em>myServer</em>”, the Creation rules should be:</p><ul><li>Create <em>Association</em>, if the type name “match” path “<em>myClient::*</em>” with an &lt;UNDEFINED&gt; meta type.</li><li>Create <em>Association</em>, if the type name “match” path “<em>myServer::*</em>” with an <span>&lt;UNDEFINED&gt;</span> meta type.</li><li>If the rules do not match, create class fields as attributes.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4152041c-801e-4eb8-96f3-a60870ed96d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419319163</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7193ba90-af2c-4422-b9d9-a6c7caa9dde1"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reverse Options" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="13a00db8-aa4e-4894-8b34-42a5dbb14086"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675703 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00981: Rules of extracting in Sequence diagram

- page_id: `169675703`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

257578332

257578343

257578333

Outgoing intersected messages are not selected for the extraction. The exception to this rule is the reply message. You can see an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The exception to this rule is the reply message. You can see an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of the return message extraction" ac:alt="Example of the return message extraction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extracting_sequence6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Rules of extracting in Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6e357f2-f118-49ad-afad-34f5fbc1ff4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">257578331</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extracting">Extracting</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extracting+in+Sequence+diagram">Extracting in Sequence diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Extract+Sequence+Wizard">Extract Sequence Wizard</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Working+with+model+elements">Working with model elements</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
